question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will answer AQW 42710/22-27.
To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of school days that will be lost on training for familiarisation with his TransformED strategy.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether their office has facilitated any meetings with the Loyalist Communities Council.
To ask the Minister of Finance whether his Department is gathering data on the effectiveness of the Defamation Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.
To ask the Minister of Finance whether he has brought a paper to the Executive on no fault divorce.
To ask the Minister of Finance, further to AQW 33540/22-27, whether he has made any additional progress on further fiscal devolution.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail how often their Department has discussed the Public Sector Transformation Board with Executive colleagues, in the last 12 months.

To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of applications for project approval for social housing, including schemes and number of units, currently being considered by the Housing Executive.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) his Department's; and (ii) the NI Environment Agency’s, most recent assessment of the levels of asbestos at the Meridi Street site in South Belfast, including any associated health risks should the site be (a) disturbed; or (b) undisturbed.
answer_text: Removing asbestos is a highly specialised, complex and delicate operation.	Remedial works on the Meridi Street Site, Belfast, began in August 2025 and were completed in November 2025. NIEA then procured an independent consultant to conduct an environmental assessment of the site. 		Report findings indicate that fragments of Asbestos Containing Material (ACM) are still present on site. If the site remains secure the material presents minimal risk however if disturbed due to unauthorised access the risk increases to both those accessing the site and those they subsequently come into contact with.		Therefore, NIEA, alongside other agencies, have highlighted concerns about people entering the site, as doing so could disturb the asbestos posing a risk of harm to themselves and others.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) any formal structures that have been established between her Department and the Department for Communities to coordinate responses to energy supply shocks; and (ii) the number of occasions any structures have been activated, in the last two years.

To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of occasions she has met with the Minister for Communities, regarding the development or implementation of the Fuel Poverty Strategy, in the last two years.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when a new Renewable Energy Support Scheme is expected to be in place.

To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of occasions she has met with the Minister for Communities, regarding the development or implementation of the Energy Strategy, in the last two years.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any steps she is taking to improve mobile phone (i) signals; and (ii) data speed, in the BT10 area.
To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on any steps her Department is taking to address the ongoing issue of criminal damage and arson attacks on 5G masts.
answer_text: I share your concerns regarding these attacks and fully appreciate the impact they have on public safety, our emergency services and the wider community.  Disruptions to mobile connectivity can have far-reaching effects, particularly for vulnerable individuals who rely on these services for essential communication and access to support.The Police Service of Northern Ireland has confirmed that there have been no attacks on a 5G mast since November 2025. The operational detection and investigation of these attacks is an operational matter for the Chief Constable, who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board.  You may therefore wish to also direct your question to the PSNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any steps his Department is taking to enforce the Outer Belfast Lough Marine Conservation Zone.
answer_text: The Outer Belfast Lough Marine Conservation Zone (MCZ) is monitored regularly by the Department as part of its wider Marine Protected Area enforcement programme. The only activity currently prohibited within the boundary is fishing using demersal mobile gear, and this is enforced under existing arrangements. The Department is progressing preparatory work on potential management measures for anchoring activity in the MCZ and any such measures, once introduced under the Marine Act (Northern Ireland) 2013, would be enforced in the same way.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether statutory bereavement leave for miscarriage will include freelancers as well as employees.
To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any communication that is made with freight and cruise shipping about the Outer Belfast Lough Marine Conservation Zone.
answer_text: The Department engaged with maritime stakeholders, including freight and harbour interests, through two meetings held in January and April 2025 on proposed anchoring management measures for the Outer Belfast Lough Marine Conservation Zone. As available evidence of anchoring relates only to cargo vessels, no direct communication has been required with cruise ship operators to date.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail when the next meeting on the Outer Belfast Lough Marine Conservation Zone will take place.
answer_text: The Department has not scheduled a further meeting in relation to the Outer Belfast Lough Marine Conservation Zone. Two stakeholder engagement meetings were held in 2025 to provide updates on proposed anchoring management options. Further engagement will take place once the Department is ready to bring forward proposals for public consultation on any potential byelaw.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any actions his Department is taking to ensure wrasse fishing is conducted in a sustainable way.
answer_text: My department is introducing a licence variation for Northern Ireland vessels which will prohibit fishing for wrasse during an annual closed season from 1 December to 19 June. The licence variation will also provide for a minimum conservation reference size, below which wrasse cannot be landed.My department is also developing additional management measures for wrasse fishing for my consideration which would be subject to consultation. The aim of such measures would be to safeguard stock sustainability while providing clarity for commercial and recreational fishers.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he is considering any support for oil costs for families where a parent is on maternity leave.
answer_text: My Department remains firmly committed to providing practical assistance to those most at risk of fuel poverty. I am working with Executive colleagues to determine how the recently announced £17.2m allocation from the UK Government may be best utilised.There are a range of supports available to those on maternity leave, subject to meeting eligibility criteria, including Maternity Allowance, Statutory Maternity Pay and Universal Credit. Further information is available at https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/information-and-services/benefits-and-financial-support/expecting-or-bringing-childrenAdditional financial support may also be available through the Discretionary Support Scheme which can help those in need of short-term assistance in crisis or emergency situations. My Department’s Make the Call service is available to ensure people are receiving all of the benefits, services and supports they are entitled to.Further information on the Make the Call service and the Discretionary Support Scheme can be found here: https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/contacts/make-call-service and https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/articles/discretionary-supportThe NI Energy Advice Service (NIEAS), funded by my Department and delivered by the Housing Executive, continues to offer free and impartial advice including grant referrals, heating tips and oil buying savings. Information on how they can be contacted can be found here: https://www.nihe.gov.uk/housing-help/ni-energy-advice/ni-energy-advice-get-in-touch Applications can also be made to the Affordable Warmth Scheme through NIEAS, for more information on the scheme visit https://www.nihe.gov.uk/housing-help/affordable-warmth/affordable-warmth-scheme Longer term action is set out in the Warm Healthy Homes Strategy, with a new fund launching in 2027 to support low income and vulnerable households living in the least energy efficient homes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department is exploring any barriers arising from the UK Exit from the European Union that could prevent NI businesses operating on an all-island basis from benefitting from a potential European Capital Markets Union.
answer_text: I recognise the devastating impact of domestic abuserelated suicides and extend my heartfelt thoughts to all those affected. I remain committed to expanding the Domestic Homicide Review (DHR) model to include domestic abuse–related deaths by suicide; however, a number of key conditions must be met before this can be taken forward.My priority continues to be that the existing DHR system is fully embedded and operating effectively, with reviews progressed in a timely manner and learning shared appropriately across agencies. It is essential that the current model is functioning well and delivering meaningful outcomes before any extension is considered. As part of this my officials are taking forward work to enhance current procedures, including reviewing the existing guidance, developing a training plan for participating agencies and supporting materials.Extending the scheme to include deaths by suicide would increase both the volume and complexity of reviews and would therefore require substantial additional investment from the Executive and relevant partner organisations. To expand the current system without having adequate resourcing and specialist expertise in place would risk placing further strain on agencies and could undermine the quality and timeliness of reviews, as well as potentially detracting from other priority frontline business.As I have also previously made clear, there are a number of complex matters of policy that will need to be carefully considered and resolved before we are in a position to expand DHRs to include domestic abuse–related suicides; these include considerations around how to establish causal links in cases where there has been no criminal investigation or conviction. Any expansion would therefore require careful scoping, including sensitive and consistent case identification, and would cut across a range of departments and agencies. Given that my Department’s current capacity is focused on delivering existing priorities to prevent and address domestic and sexual abuse, it has not been possible to progress this work at this stage.The need for further preparation and investment does not diminish the seriousness of these cases or their impact on families and communities. Rather, it reflects the importance of ensuring that any future expansion of the DHR model is robust, properly resourced and capable of delivering meaningful learning.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline any plans his Department has to provide financial support to people experiencing higher home heating costs.

To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department is exploring the potential benefits of a European Capital Markets Union on NI businesses and commercial projects operating on an all-island basis.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department will support the introduction of School Street pilot schemes.
answer_text: At present my Department only has powers to close roads for road works and road races, although local councils also have powers to close roads for other purposes.Within my Department, work in this area is currently focussed on developing a toolkit of measures that can be implemented around schools to make the environment safer and more attractive for pedestrians at all times of day. Some of the measures under consideration include the provision of greater pedestrian priority at crossing points in the vicinity of schools, as well as measures to discourage inconsiderate parking that can cause difficulties for those walking or wheeling. As part of this work, officials are progressing a small number of pilot projects to inform future policy development. At present my Department only has powers to close roads for road works and road races, although local councils also have powers to close roads for other purposes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department will support the introduction of School Street pilot schemes.
answer_text: This matter falls under the remit of the Minister for Infrastructure, who has advised me that her Department is progressing a small number of pilot projects to inform future policy development and I am supportive of this pilotbased approach.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of households, year-on-year, which have transitioned away from home heating oil to another form of energy with support from her Department, since 2022.
answer_text: In line with the Executive’s Energy Strategy and as a result of the regulatory environment established by my Department there have been over 40,000 new domestic connections to the gas network since 2022. This includes both new build households and those previously using unregulated heating oil.Additionally, since 2023, my department has provided an additional £5.6m in support to NISEP. This funding has delivered a range of energy efficiency measures to almost 2,500 low-income households. This investment serves the dual purpose of preparing homes for future adoption of low carbon heating technologies and insulating those who are most impacted by fossil fuel cost volatility.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any steps her Department is taking to promote the use of plug-in solar.
answer_text: Financial support is not currently available for homeowners who wish to install solar panels, including plug-in solar. Feedback from the low carbon heat consultation in 2025 suggested that combining solar panels with a heat pump could reduce overall operating costs. This option is being considered as we take forward the development of the low carbon heat and energy efficiency support scheme in 2026.In the meantime, households may still find solar panels, including plug-in solar, or solar with battery storage, a worthwhile investment offering long-term savings, even in the absence of grant support.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any assessment made of her Department's delivery of the Belfast Cycling Network Delivery Plan 2022-31.
answer_text: Significant progress has been made on the majority of short term schemes in the Belfast Cycling Network Delivery Plan. Street lighting has been provided on the urban section of Comber Greenway and sections of Forth Meadow Greenway and Lagan Gateway have been delivered in partnership with Belfast City Council. Work is also complete on the upgrade to existing facilities on Stranmillis Embankment. Translink are delivering sections of the Belfast Cycling Network in the vicinity of Belfast Grand Central Station and schemes on Lagmore Avenue, West Belfast Greenway Phase 1a and Island Street are currently in construction. Design is substantially complete on the Montgomery Road, Sydenham Greenway Phase 1 and Durham Street schemes, all of which are at various stages of the statutory process required to legislate the proposed cycle routes before construction can commence. A further nine schemes are currently in design, including the next phase of Sydenham Greenway, which is being taken forward as one of six major Active Travel Signature Projects. Initial stakeholder engagement has been completed for the Sailortown & Ulster University schemes, and a planning application has recently been submitted for West Belfast Greenway Phase 1b. Stakeholder engagement is currently underway for the Dublin Road & Botanic Avenue scheme and it is expected that the statutory consultation process for the Ravenhill Road scheme will commence in the coming weeks, with documentation now being finalised for publication. Prioritisation of future schemes on the Belfast Cycling Network will be determined in line with the emerging Eastern Transport Plan and other significant changes that have occurred in Belfast in recent years, not least the relocation of Ulster University and the increased numbers of students now living in the city centre.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any action he is taking to improve school safety for students with allergies.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they have agreed a contribution to the UK state report to the Council of Europe Committee of Experts on the European Charter for Regional or Minority Languages.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail (i) the purpose of the deputy First Minister's meeting with Gunster Strategies on 15 or 16 March 2026; and (ii) whether any civil servants were in attendance.

To ask the Minister of Education to detail any steps he is taking, in conjunction with the Education Authority, to provide information and education to schools about female genital mutilation (FGM), including (i) any signs to be aware of in children and young people who may be at risk of, or victim to, this practice; and (ii) any safeguarding procedures in place for schools who need to take action regarding concerns that a child is vulnerable to or has been a victim of FGM.
answer_text: All persons in school safeguarding teams receive appropriate training and are aware of the need to respond correctly to Female Genital Mutilation (FGM) issues. The Education Authority’s Child Protection Support Service (CPSS), which has dealt with a number of potential cases, can provide support and advice to schools as FGM is included as part of its child protection training. All school safeguarding staff should be aware of warning signs and where there is an immediate risk of FGM occurring this should be reported to the PSNI Sexual Referral Unit without delay. If there is a concern that a child or young person may be at risk of FGM, referral should be made to the relevant HSCT Gateway Team. Schools need to be aware that a sudden, unexpected drop off in attendance and attainment for a female child from an ethnic background could be an indicator that FGM has taken place and if this is suspected it should be reported to PSNI. The EA CPSS is also available to provide advice as to how the school might support the child as part of the broader pastoral care framework.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) any steps he is taking to provide information and education to medical professionals in Health and Social Care Trusts to ensure they are adequately informed about female genital mutilation (FGM) including (ii) how to recognise potential indicators in children and adults who may have been victim to this practice; and (iii) how to treat and support those who have suffered from FGM.
To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the provision of a 24/7 thrombectomy service.
answer_text: My Department’s Reshaping Stroke Care Action Plan sets out a specific commitment to expand the current regional service which is available 8am-5pm Monday to Sunday to a 24/7 service. As you will be aware, officials are working with the Belfast Trust to develop a business case which will provide further detail on the estimated £5 million recurrent funding required to support a 24/7 service. In the absence of the required additional funding, I am committed to maximising access to thrombectomy within the current hours of operation and promoting equity across Trusts. In this context, it should be noted that the current thrombectomy rate in Northern Ireland of 5.2% ranks highly in relation to regional rates elsewhere in the UK.But I am confident on progress on a move to a 24/7 model being made soon.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she will approve the amendments to the current traffic calming measures in Orpen park, Finaghy, before the end of the 2022-27 Assembly mandate.
answer_text: My officials are considering traffic calming options for Orpen Park, Finaghy.  Recommendations will be brought forward for my consideration before the end of the 2022-27 Assembly mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department's Disposal Stakeholders Group will brief local elected representatives on the disposal process for surplus land at Knockbracken Healthcare Park.
answer_text: I understand that locally elected representatives have an interest in the Department’s disposal plan for its surplus land at Knockbracken Healthcare Park.  I can confirm that my officials are currently liaising with officers from Lisburn & Castlereagh City Council, one of the key stakeholders in the disposal stakeholder group, to provide a report to locally elected members setting out the background to the disposal project and confirming the latest position in the disposal process.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any steps his Department is taking to ensure timely pay parity for health workers in future years.
answer_text: The NHS Pay Review Body has recommended a 3.3% consolidated uplift for all Agenda for Change pay points from 1 April 2026, and England and Wales have already confirmed they will implement the NHSPRB recommendation, and, subject to budgetary clarity, it is my intention to do likewise for AfC staff in 2026/27.I am fully committed to ensuring that pay will be the first priority for my Department and I and my officials continue to engage regularly with Trade Unions on delivering a pay award as early as possible in the new financial year, most recently on 16 February. I also remain fully committed to progress towards HSC becoming a Real Living Wage Employer and my officials are working closely with Trade unions to agree a pathway towards this before the end of March 2026.Whilst it is my firm ambition that staff should receive their pay uplift as early as possible in the incoming financial year, I am fully aware that I can only deliver on those commitments when clear about my budgetary position. However, even in the absence of any agreement, I have asked my officials to progress the necessary preparatory work at pace.While my ambition is to deliver pay uplifts as early as possible, this is dependent on a confirmed budget. In the meantime, officials have been asked to progress all preparatory work at pace. The draft budget under consultation would present significant financial challenges for HSC, underscoring the need for a multi-year budget to provide stability and support long-term planning.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department is considering installing a filter light for traffic turning right on the Annadale Embankment, Belfast, to alleviate congestion.
answer_text: I recognise that there can be some congestion at this junction at peak times, which reflects wider traffic conditions in the Belfast area.The traffic signals at the Ormeau Road / Annadale Embankment junction are designed to accommodate current traffic demands. Vehicles turning right are required to give way to oncoming traffic and proceed only when it is safe to do so.Full rightturn signal control would require significant junction alterations, including road widening and land acquisition, which is not feasible due to the constraints of the Ormeau Bridge. While a rightturn filter light could be considered, this would likely increase queuing and could introduce safety concerns for opposing traffic movements.In light of these considerations, there are currently no plans to alter the existing junction layout at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether any assessment has been made of whether the Minister for Education's proposed removal of AS levels will have any impact on her Department, specifically in relation to her remit for further and higher education.
answer_text: I recognise that any significant changes to AS levels could have consequences for further and higher education and have written to the Education Minister to outline my concerns.  In respect of higher education, the potential implications of the proposed reforms on crossborder mobility have been highlighted. The review of A-level grade equivalencies recently implemented in the south means that, currently, northern applicants can now achieve top CAO points with three A-levels and one AS-level, making it easier for students from here to study at institutions in the south.  Changes in respect of AS Levels may impact the progress made on reducing barriers to north/south student mobility. In respect of further education, my Department is currently considering the potential impact of the proposed reforms on future further education provision and will continue to engage with the Department of Education as this work progresses.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether his Department provides any guidance to officials in relation to providing communications support to the Minister when visiting their own constituency.
answer_text: The Department does not issue separate guidance specifically for constituency visits. Instead, officials follow the established NICS Code of Ethics and central government communications principles, which make clear that support provided by civil servants must relate only to the Minister’s official duties and maintain impartiality.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any education systems (i) within; and (ii) outside, the UK that his Department examined to inform his decision to remove AS levels from the curriculum.
answer_text: My Department gave consideration to the other UK jurisdictions that offer GCE A levels (England and Wales) given the similarity of the qualifications.Furthermore, my officials have participated over several years in a specific OECD international working group on upper secondary education examining and comparing qualifications and accreditation offered across a wide range of OECD member countries.Annex B of the consultation report below, lists in detail the evidence considered in reaching decisions on the future of CCEA qualifications.Consultation Report - The Future of CCEA GCSE AS and A Level Qualifications_0.pdf

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether their Department provides any guidance to officials in relation to providing communications support to the Minister when visiting their own constituency.

To ask the Minister of Education, in relation to the school hall at Rosetta Primary School, to detail (i) any proposed programme of works to reopen the hall; (ii) any plan and timetable for permanent remedial works; (iii) any meetings he has had with school leaders to provide clarity and information on the works.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) appointed an external architect-led design team in January 2025 to review the works required to the roof of the assembly hall at Rosetta Primary School. Following an initial structural survey, the scope of the proposed works was extended, and a more extensive condition survey was initiated. The EA received the formal condition report from the design team on 4 March 2026, and this will now be incorporated into a feasibility report, to inform the business case. It is anticipated that the feasibility report will be completed during March 2026. 		A timeline for the work will be determined when the business case is completed and the preferred option identified. The EA will liaise closely with the school on a timeline for completion of the work.		The EA has confirmed that internal steel props have been erected to make safe the roof structure. In parallel, a planning application has been submitted for a bespoke, standalone modular toilet block and storage provision. 		While the roof works are being planned and delivered as per part (i), the EA has established a licence agreement with the neighbouring church to enable the school to use the church hall, with EA covering this cost. The EA has also installed a gate in the boundary wall to enable pupils to access the church grounds directly from the school site.		EA officials are working closely with the school to provide clarity and information on the works. Dale Hanna, Chief Operating Officer for Operations and Estates visited the school on 10 March to discuss the situation with the principal and school governors and further meetings are planned in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, in light of comments made by the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland at the 4 March 2026 meeting of the Northern Ireland Affairs Committee, whether he will allocate any additional resources to support local voluntary sector organisations impacted by the transition to the Local Growth Fund.
answer_text: Years of austerity have left us with a dire budget situation with pressures that far outstrip the funding we have available. It is unrealistic for Westminster to expect the Executive to step in and address the harm caused by their decisions. Suggestions to the contrary by the Secretary of State were unhelpful.		Both I and the Executive have repeatedly made representations to Westminster, highlighting the devastating impacts of their decisions, particularly on the Community and Voluntary Sector, and calling for a fairer Resource allocation. 		Earlier this month, MHCLG Secretary of State, Steve Reed, replied to a letter from me in December and a letter from the Executive in January on this issue. He indicated the Capital-heavy split will not be amended. This is despite English regions receiving a more favourable Resource profile.		The Executive wrote on 19 March to Hilary Benn informing him of our disappointment at comments made at the NI Affairs Committee and, again, urging his Government to reverse, and correct, its fundamentally unfair approach in respect of the Local Growth Fund. Even at this late stage, Westminster could and should rectify this situation.I would urge Members to follow the lead of the Executive and press the British Government to resolve this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of school places that were not filled in the (i) current; and (ii) previous, school year.
answer_text: There were 59,182 available places within nursery, primary and post-primary schools in the 2025/26 academic year. In 2024/25, this figure was 54,452.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will make amendments to dental regulations to include a medical exemption for people with diabetes.
answer_text: My Department recognises the significant relationship between diabetes and oral health, particularly the wellestablished increased risk of periodontitis (severe gum disease) among people living with both type 1 and type 2 diabetes. UK evidence highlights that patients with diabetes are more likely to develop severe gum disease, and that effective dental care is an important component of their overall health management. In addition, I note a bidirectional relationship between diabetes and periodontitis, whereby gum disease can also worsen glycaemic control and contribute to complications.It is important to acknowledge that other long-term medical conditions also impact oral health needs and contribute to demands for both preventive care and treatment of oral disease.  This context is important when considering exemption arrangements, which are currently based on specific eligibility criteria rather than diagnosis of individual medical conditions.  My Department has no current plans to amend the existing exemption framework to include medical condition specific entitlements. Moving to such a model would require substantial regulatory, financial and operational consideration across the wider dental health system.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline any consideration his Department has given to introducing a basic income fund for artists in Northern Ireland, similar to the system in the Republic of Ireland.
To ask the Minister of Justice, in light of recent Investigatory Powers Tribunal cases, to outline any steps her Department will take to protect the freedom of the press.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount that was (i) bid for; (ii) secured for; and (iii) spent on, the Belfast Rapid Transport 2 project, in the 2025-26 financial year.
answer_text: As part of Final Budget 2025-26 BRT2 was successful in bidding for and securing £5.0m of Belfast Region City Deal funding. This funding was subsequently reduced to £500,000 in the December 2025 monitoring round in line with the BRT2 programme plan for land acquisition. Current forecasted expenditure for 2025-26 is expected to fully utilise the £500,000 budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any protections in place for home owners who are impacted by structural defects at their homes, which are the responsibility of companies that are no longer operating.
answer_text: My Department’s responsibilities in respect of Building Regulations and Land Law does not cover protections for homeowners impacted by structural defects in their homes in any respect.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any actions she will take in relation to crossing the Knockbreda carriageway, Belfast, to provide a safe crossing for those with prams.
answer_text: Officials advise that along the A55 Upper Knockbreda Road, between the Castlereagh Road and the A24 Saintfield Road, there are a number of controlled crossing facilities in place, including both standalone crossings and crossings incorporated within signalised junctions, which provide safe crossing facilities for all road users including those with prams.  These are located at the Castlereagh Road/Ballygowan Road junction, Castledona Park, Clonduff Drive, Cregagh Road, the Upper Galwally/Glencregagh Road junction and the Saintfield Road junction.While there are currently no proposals for additional crossings at this location, my Department keeps the provision of pedestrian facilities under review as part of its ongoing consideration of road safety and accessibility needs.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the number of alcohol-related deaths, in each year since 2012.
answer_text: Official statistics on alcohol-specific deaths are produced annually by NISRA, using registration data collected by the General Register Office (GRO). Published statistics relate to deaths registered that are known to be direct consequences of alcohol misuse.The number of alcohol-specific deaths, in each year since 2012, can be found in Table 1 of the Alcohol-Specific Deaths 2024 tables. The methodology for selecting alcohol related deaths was revised in 2017, following a consultation led by the Office for National Statistics (ONS) to develop a UK-wide definition of alcohol-specific deaths. The new definition includes conditions known to be exclusively caused by alcohol (that is, wholly attributable causes) and excludes conditions where only a proportion of the deaths are caused by alcohol (that is, partially attributable causes).  Compared with that used previously, the new definition includes several additional wholly attributable conditions.  Details of difference is available on the Alcohol-Specific Deaths report webpage.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on his Department's plans to raise the minimum age of marriage and civil partnership to 18, through a bill to be introduced in the 2022-27 Assembly mandate.
answer_text: The Executive considered and agreed a draft Bill on 26th February 2026. I expect the Bill to be introduced in the Assembly shortly.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of the reforms to the allocation of Education, Health and Care plans to children with special educational needs and disabilities, announced in England.
answer_text: My department is currently considering the detail of the Department for Education in England’s Schools White Paper, ‘Every Child Achieving and Thriving’, and the Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND) Consultation Document, ‘SEND Reform: Putting Children and Young People First’, following their publication on 23 February. The Department for Education provided an oral briefing to senior officials from my department on 24 February 2026 on the content of the papers and have agreed to do so again as the White Paper and consultation progress.I welcome initial indications that there is close alignment between the proposed reforms in England and my SEN Reform Agenda and five-year Delivery Plan published in February 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Tariff Working Group, following the announcement by the President of the United States of America of a 10 per cent global tariff.
answer_text: I established my Tariff Working Group in April 2025 in response to the announcement of sweeping tariffs by the US President. The Group meets regularly and provides me with valuable insights to the evolving trade and tariff situation.In relation to the decision of the US Supreme Court and, in response, President Trump’s imposition of a 10% global tariff, I have written to both British and Irish Governments seeking urgent clarity on the anticipated interaction with previously negotiated tariff agreements – and highlighting the unique position of the North in that regard.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether the delay of the Belfast Rapid Transport Phase 2 Project will have any impact on newly introduced bus lane times on the Saintfield Road.
answer_text: As I have previously advised the Member, there is no delay to this phase of the Belfast Rapid Transit Phase 2 (BRT2) Project which remains on track for delivery in 2030 The recently introduced bus lane times on the Saintfield Road are entirely separate from the BRT2 Project and will not be affected by its delivery.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any budgets in place for McCloud payments, in 2026-2027.
answer_text: The current budget provision for the McCloud Remedy payments for the NI Civil Service Pension Scheme, for which my Department has direct responsibility, in 2026-2027 is £13.5 million. This allocation covers the expected costs associated with delivering the McCloud Remedy for Immediate Choice members in 2026-2027, including arrears, lump sum payments and any associated interest. This reflects the position set out in the last formally agreed Annually Managed Expenditure (AME) baseline. This will be reviewed for adequacy as part of the normal annual estimates process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of bus cancellations that have been recorded in Belfast in the last 12 months, broken down by service route.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:Metro Corridor Scheduled Journeys Failed to Operate 1 78946 1091 2 106306 1920 3 70443 1493 4 45991 702 5 41379 893 6 81622 1675 7 57031 781 8 51400 989 9 70939 1402 10 133183 934 11 108163 2983 12 51320 1032 13 5302 192 14 6369 169 Total 908,394 16,256 1.8%   Glider      G1 74268 658 G2 32626 377 Total 106,894 1,035 1.0% Please note 45.5% of the total cancelled journeys for the period provided were as a result of external factors outside of Translink’s control including road works, road traffic accidents and traffic congestion.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any engagement his Department is having with private childcare providers whose customers utilise the Tax Free Childcare scheme, around (i) aligning their invoices and billings schedules with the scheme; and (ii) not issuing late fees against parents due to any delays in the scheme.
answer_text: Administration of the Tax-Free Childcare scheme (TFC) is a reserved matter, undertaken directly by His Majesty’s Revenue & Customs (HMRC). Where issues arise relating to individual TFC payments, these should be taken up directly with HMRC. See link below:Tax-Free Childcare: What Tax-Free Childcare is - GOV.UKPrivate childcare providers are independent businesses. My Department has no authority to intervene in their commercial decisions, including invoicing practices, billing cycles or late fees.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she will provide a copy of her Department’s assessment of the (i) remaining works required; and (ii) preliminary costings, for the Malone Ridge Development, Belfast.
answer_text: I can confirm that my officials have undertaken an assessment of the works required to bring the development to an adoptable standard which includes the replacement of retaining structures, and measures to retain the road and adjacent thirdparty land.The preliminary cost estimate is approximately £180,000 However this estimate is subject to a detailed design being carried out and may increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions her Department has had with broadband providers around (i) removing or expanding data caps; and (ii) fair usage, for residents of NI wishing to use their phone in the Republic of Ireland.
answer_text: Mobile connectivity and associated services are wholly provided on a commercial basis by Mobile Network Operators (MNOs) in the North. I share the frustration of some citizens being subject to fair usage limits, however, these are commercial decisions taken by private companies. I would encourage consumers to shop around to secure tariffs that best serve their needs, as not all MNOs have a data cap or fair usage restriction for customers on this island.My officials will continue to regularly engage with the mobile industry to raise local issues to ensure citizens from the North can avail of the benefits this technology can offer.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for a completion date for the delivery of Belfast Rapid Transport Phase 2.
To ask the Minister of Finance whether he asked the Executive to agree to the Treasury's open book budget scrutiny exercise.
answer_text: The Executive has been kept fully apprised throughout the engagement with Treasury on access to the reserve in 2025-26. The Chief Secretary to the Treasury’s offer of a £400 million Reserve Claim was accompanied with an agreement to continue engagement on the Executive’s financial position beyond 2025–26. To help inform this there will be a review of departmental budgets which will provide greater clarity on the spending plans of departments, as well as providing an opportunity to highlight the challenges faced by the Executive in delivering the level of public services required.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department has considered implementing the ability to pre-book a GP appointment.
answer_text: As independent contractors, GPs manage the day to day running of their practices, including the use of technology, telephony and management of their appointment system.Each GP practice will determine the most appropriate approach to safely address patients’ needs using the range of skills available within the practice team and a variety of communication methods including telephone and face to face consultations.Many General Practices in Northern Ireland offer the ability to pre-book some appointments, including the ability to book or change an appointment using online services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when her Department plans to resurface Orchardville (i) Avenue; (ii) Gardens; and (iii) Crescent, Finaghy, Belfast.
answer_text: Details of all resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year are provided in my Department’s annual report to Council.In the meantime, my Department will continue to undertake routine inspections and actionable defects will be prioritised for repair in accordance with the Limited Service Policy in so far as resources permit.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether departmental five year business plans will be published.
answer_text: I encourage all departments to publish their finalised five year plans, however the plans belong to the individual departments and they are responsible for decisions on timing of publication.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any role departmental five year plans had in the multi year budget.
answer_text: The five-year plans are key to understanding the trajectory of each department over a rolling five-year period, covering strategic direction over the medium term, outlining the costs involved, and potential efficiencies and other opportunities to reduce spending.The plans require strategic thinking to inform what finances are needed to achieve the direction of travel and compare that against what finances are available. Departments will need to examine ways to live within their budgets, including delivering efficiencies and transforming how they deliver public services.  This will continue to be a rolling programme, and the plans will evolve over time.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail (i) the in-year Barnett consequentials notified by the UK government in the 2025-2026 financial year; and (ii) how he opted to use each consequential.
answer_text: The Chancellor’s Spring Statement and Westminster Main Estimates provided Barnett consequentials of £158.5 million Resource DEL and £2.1 million Capital DEL. The Executive agreed at Budget how this funding would be used; however, as it was not received until Westminster Main Estimates, these allocations were included on an indicative basis at Budget and formally allocated to departments at June Monitoring.   Following Budget, additional Barnett consequentials were confirmed at the Westminster Main Estimates of £50.0 million Resource DEL, £171.2 million Ring-fenced Resource DEL, and a reduction of £1.1 million Capital DEL. Phase 2 of the Spending Review provided a further £4.8 million Resource DEL and a £0.7 million reduction in Capital DEL.  These amounts formed part of the available funding allocated by the Executive in the June Monitoring round.  At the Autumn Budget, further Barnett consequentials of £18.8 million Resource DEL were confirmed, and these were included in the Executive’s December Monitoring allocations. In late January, the Treasury confirmed additional Barnett consequentials of £13.4 million Resource DEL, £15.6 million Capital DEL, and a reduction of £3.4 million in Financial Transactions Capital. These were reflected in the funding available for the further allocations outlined in my written statement to the Assembly on 6 February and were agreed by the Executive.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they have had any meetings with the leader of the DUP, regarding the budget scrutiny exercise agreed with the UK Government, as part of the reserve claim.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when she will publish her Department's response to the Inconsiderate Pavement Parking consultation.
answer_text: Following my announcement on 5 November 2025, my officials are now taking forward work to develop the necessary legislation to introduce a blanket ban across the North on parking a vehicle wholly on the footway, parking across dropped kerbs and double parking. It is my aim to introduce these changes in the latter part of 2026, subject to the legislative process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail whether her Department's commitment to extend Fáilte Ireland brands into the north, still stands.
answer_text: Yes I remain to be committed to incorporating regions of the north into all three of Fáilte Ireland’s regional brands. Ultimately this is a decision for Fáilte Ireland, and I would encourage you to express your support for this directly to Fáilte Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the reason why he has not made a statement relating to the budget scrutiny exercise agreed with the UK Government, as part of the reserve claim announced on 11 February 2026.
answer_text: On 11 February 2026, I made a Written Ministerial Statement detailing that I had successfully concluded negotiations with Treasury by securing a £400 million reserve claim for 2025-26.This reserve claim provided additional funding of £214.6 million to our schools and £185.4 million to our Health Service in 2025-26. Further to this, I also secured agreement that this reserve claim would be repaid over multiple years, thereby avoiding large reductions to Departmental budgets in 2026-27. As outlined in my Written Statement, the Executive will work with Treasury to review the Executive’s spending and pressures. This will support a fuller understanding of the significant challenges we are facing. Each department will have a crucial role in this work, as they are best placed to articulate the challenges within their areas. This process will be firmly grounded in the principles of devolution and will respect the Executive’s autonomy in making funding decisions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 39425/22-27, and; AQO 2987/22-27, to detail the bid for £4 million of resource, over the three years.
answer_text: The £4 million resource bid submitted by my department for the proposed Draft Budget 2026-29/30 process included £1.1m for 2026-27, £1.2m for 2027-28 & £1.7m for 2028-29. This was a placeholder bid related to cross-programme action areas emerging through work under the Heritage, Culture and Creativity Programme.  The action areas are referenced in the Programme’s framework document which was published in July 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline any engagement he has had with the banking roundtable, or any other groups, in relation to financial conduct.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 38865/22-27, to detail when she plans to make this statement.
answer_text: I made a statement to the Assembly on 10 March 2026 on how future residents’ parking schemes will be assessed by my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any actions she is taking to improve road safety for pedestrians in Carryduff.
answer_text: Firstly, I would like to express my sincere condolences to the family and all those tragically affected by the incident in Carryduff on 31st January 2026. Every life lost on our roads is a profound loss to the community and road safety remains an extremely high priority for my Department.  We are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and address the needs of all road users.Collisions of this nature, particularly those which result in a fatality, are formally investigated by the PSNI and my officials will work with the investigating officers to facilitate their enquiries.  When findings are shared, we will carefully consider if any engineering measures can be introduced to enhance road safety at this location.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any progress made on the delivery of Belfast Rapid Transport Phase 2, since her statement on 25 February 2025.
answer_text: On the 25th February 2025 I was pleased to announce the next steps for the Belfast Rapid Transit 2 project which will see implementation of this innovative service across the North and South of the city, bringing the benefits already established by the Glider service to communities living along this route.The Outline Business Case for the project was submitted to DoF on 25th November 2025 with an estimated delivery cost of £124.5m and this was approved on 22nd December 2025.Funding of £35m has been allocated from our Belfast Region City Deal partners, and I have committed an additional £13 million from my Department which will enable the commencement of a phased delivery. While a significant shortfall funding remains, I am committed to working with my colleagues in the executive and our Belfast Region City Deal partners to identify opportunities to address this.Following approval of the Outline Business Case, my officials are now in the process of appointing engineering consultants to progress the detailed design phase.A detailed delivery programme will be developed following the appointment of consultants however it is currently expected that, subject to securing the additional funding required, the BRT2 project could be completed by 2033.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail his Department’s position on the European's Commission revision of the Tobacco Taxation Directive.
answer_text: I am aware that last summer, the European Commission proposed a revision to the Tobacco Taxation Directive. Tobacco taxation in the UK is a reserved matter for HMT. Should new EU legislation interact with the Windsor Framework, it would be for the UK Government to engage with the EU Commission on this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his Department’s assessment of the EU's Revision of the Tobacco Taxation Directive.
answer_text: I am aware that last summer, the European Commission proposed a revision to the Tobacco Taxation Directive. Tobacco taxation in the UK is a reserved matter for HMT. Should new EU legislation interact with the Windsor Framework, it would be for the UK Government to engage with the EU Commission on this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline the progress his Department has made on creating a waste management strategy.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline his Department's budget for USA-NI250 projects.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any savings made by Executive department's since the match funding obligations, under the European Social Fund, came to an end in 2023.
answer_text: No savings were realised by Executive Departments arising from cessation of European Social Fund (ESF) matchfunding obligations in 2023.Conversely, the withdrawal of both ESF and ERDF funding created additional financial pressures across departmental budgets. Annual ESF support of approximately £8 million for the Department for the Economy’s Apprenticeships programme, and £30.6 million in ERDF support for Invest NI programmes, was not fully replaced following the end of EU structural funding. The value of lost EU funding exceeded total annual matchfunding contributions previously provided by Executive Departments in support of ESF projects. As a result, rather than generating savings, cessation of matchfunding obligations still left departments facing a significant funding gap across programmes previously supported through EU funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail her Department's position on the European Commission's revision of the Tobacco Taxation Directive.
answer_text: My Department has not carried out an assessment of the European Commission's revision of the Tobacco Taxation Directive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) when a draft Order will be laid in the Assembly, to give effect to the 2022 final recommendations of the Local Government Boundaries Commissioner; and (ii) whether this will be with modifications.
answer_text: It is not possible to take forward a draft Order in the Assembly to implement the 2022 Final Recommendations of Local Government Boundaries Commissioner, and subsequently for the Northern Ireland Office to complete and legislate for a District Electoral Area review in time for the 2027 local government elections.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 39313/22-27, to detail when he plans to bring the subordinate legislation to the Assembly.
answer_text: My Department will maintain parity with the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) when bringing forward the necessary subordinate legislation. The DWP are working with His Majesty’s Courts and Tribunals Service and the Scottish Government to establish a process for implementing Administrative Liability Orders and plan to introduce regulations to Parliament as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she will provide funding for alley gates, to reduce levels of anti-social behaviour in the Baroda Street area, Belfast.
answer_text: I recognise the concerns raised regarding anti-social behaviour (ASB) in the Baroda Street area of Belfast and the impact this can have on local communities.Responsibility for the provision and maintenance of alley gates rests primarily with local councils. Other Departments, including the Departments for Infrastructure and for Communities, may also have a role where landowner responsibilities apply.My Department has, on a small number of occasions in the past, used its powers to assist councils where alley gates have been sought by residents to prevent access due to ASB; however, this has usually been limited to interface areas and has typically preceded the adoption of the gates by the relevant council. On the limited occasions where the Department has agreed to fund such measures, this has generally been on the recommendation of policing colleagues for express and pressing community protection needs.Given the ongoing priorities within my Department’s remit and the increasing and extensive demands on the budget, the department is not in a position to provide further funding towards alley-gating. That said, my Department continues to support community safety initiatives across Belfast through funding provided, alongside the Northern Ireland Policing Board, to Policing and Community Safety Partnerships (PCSPs). PCSPs work closely with local communities, statutory partners and the voluntary and community sector to develop appropriate actions and initiatives to tackle crime and ASB and to reduce their impact.For the Baroda Street area, engagement with South Belfast PCSP may help identify what additional support or interventions may be available to address the issues being experienced locally.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any support his Department has provided to Colin Glen Forest Park, over the last 12 months.
answer_text: Over the last 12 months, my Department provided Colin Glen Trust with £9,500 to enable the commissioning of an independent financial modelling report.  In line with the terms of a Community Asset Transfer in 2018, Colin Glen Trust was granted a 999 year lease to the forest park and golf course by my Department.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs
