question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any support her Department has provided to Colin Glen Forest Park, over the last 12 months.
answer_text: Tourism NI regularly engages with Colin Glen Forest Park to promote their offering to visitors.It has featured regularly in Tourism NI consumer marketing activity, including dedicated listings on discovernorthernireland.com for the forest, the Gruffalo Guided Walk, The Colin Glen Trackman Driving Range, as well as the Forest Park, were featured in a media Familiarisation Trip to Belfast in January. Colin Glen have also availed of the wide range of Business Support opportunities offered by Tourism NI including attendance at the 2025 Meet the Buyer event, a Golf Tourism Industry Development Day, a Maximising Marketing Channels seminar and an Embracing Events Masterclass.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any steps her Department is taking to prevent and address flooding in the Stockman’s Lane area, Belfast.
answer_text: My Department responded to significant flooding across the North following the heavy rain of the previous week, combined with Storm Chandra, which brought further wind and substantial rainfall. This resulted in widespread flooding issues. Alongside our multi-agency partners, officials worked in difficult conditions to respond and to minimise disruption.A programme of work is already under way at Stockmans Lane to improve the drainage infrastructure in this area. This includes closed-circuit television (CCTV) survey work on the existing drainage system, pipe-lining repairs, and repairs to broken connections at Boucher Road and Balmoral Avenue. Work was paused before Christmas due to the annual embargo, but further works are planned to continue these improvements and enhance the performance of the drainage system.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his department will consider a review of the SEAG Transfer Test, exploring alternatives that prioritise fairness, transparency, and the wellbeing of pupils.
answer_text: My Department is not responsible for the administration or operation of post-primary transfer tests.  I would welcome your suggestions on a departmental approved test that could achieve consensus from stakeholders.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on plans to transfer his Department's land at Newforge to Newforge Sports Complex.
answer_text: Discussions remain ongoing between officials from my Department, the Newforge Community Development Trust and, indeed, other interested parties about the potential next steps for the DAERA lands at Newforge. These discussions are being taken forward on a ‘without prejudice’ basis and any potential disposal or lease of the lands in the future will follow due process.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the rationale for his Department’s £4 million bid from the UK Government’s recently announced £1.5 billion Arts Everywhere funding.
answer_text: My Department has not made a bid for Arts Everywhere funding. Barnett Consequential funding is unhypothecated and is for the Executive to decide on how it is distributed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any meetings she had with the Minister of Finance, regarding NI Reval2026, prior to its publication on 22 January 2026.
answer_text: Reval 2026 falls under the remit of DoF. I work with the Finance Minister across a number of issues and I welcome his decision to halt Reval 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any training offered to Child Maintenance Service staff involved in the resolution of disputes as a result of changes made in the Child Support Enforcement Bill.
answer_text: The Child Support Enforcement Act (Northern Ireland) 2025 provides enabling powers for the Child Maintenance Service (CMS) to make Administrative Liability Orders through subordinate legislation. The Act has not changed the legal framework, simply provided the power to do so. The required subordinate legislation to allow CMS to make Administrative Liability Orders has not yet been made. Staff training to assist with the resolution of disputes will be delivered before the use of Administrative Liability Orders comes into effect.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she was briefed, verbally or in writing, regarding the outcome of NI Reval2026, ahead of its publication on 22 January 2026.
answer_text: Reval 2026 falls under the remit of DoF. I work with the Finance Minister across a number of issues and I welcome his decision to halt Reval 2026.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any actions they are taking to support the final group of Syrians, who entered Northern Ireland as part of the Syrian Vulnerable Persons Resettlement Scheme, to gain indefinite leave to remain, in light of proposed changes to resettlement by the UK government.
answer_text: This is not a devolved matter. On 27 January 2026, Home Office confirmed that where a Syrian person has arrived here via the Vulnerable Persons Resettlement Scheme, after being referred as a convention refugee by the UNHCR and granted refugee or humanitarian protection leave to remain, the Home Office will proceed straight to considering a grant of indefinite leave to remain providing the applicant meets the relevant validity and suitability requirements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount of any funding support her Department has offered to the Bank of London.
answer_text: Invest NI’s investment team helped secure The Bank of London project and supported the company with advice and guidance. Invest NI offered financial assistance of £530,000 to support the creation of 130 jobs.The company proceeded with its investment in the North without drawing down on the financial support offered.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether he has made any assessment of the value of unused land currently owned by the Northern Ireland Civil Service.
answer_text: There is currently no centralised database containing comprehensive market value information for all vacant land owned by the NICS.Valuation data is held by asset owners, on their land and buildings portfolios, as part of their Resource Accounting responsibilities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any support his Department is offering Health and Social Care Trust trust workers, who will see their Universal Credit and Rates Rebate claims closed, due to receiving arrears payments from the recently agreed pay award.
answer_text: Universal Credit takes account of a claimant’s earnings in the assessment period in which His Majesty’s Revenue and Customs informs the Department that they have been paid. However, Universal Credit claims with a nil award due to earnings in one assessment period will not be closed immediately. Claims will only close after six consecutive assessment periods with a nil award. Administration of Rates Rebate a matter for the Department of Finance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount spent on surface repairs on the Ormeau Road, Belfast, between the Rosetta roundabout and Upper Galwally, from 16 May 2022 to date.
answer_text: My Department has spent £36,000 on surface repairs on this section of the Ormeau Road between 16 May 2022 and the present date. This figure covers routine carriageway defect repairs, including pothole and patching works.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the scale and design of the proposed road surfacing scheme on the Ormeau Road, Belfast, programmed for Summer 2026, including any applicable drawings or maps.
answer_text: My Department plans to deliver a road resurfacing scheme on the Ormeau Road, Belfast, during Summer 2026. The scheme will extend from Rosetta Roundabout to Upper Galwally and will involve removing the existing road surface to a depth of approximately 100mm. This will allow my Department to inlay new binder and surface courses to restore the structural integrity and running quality of the carriageway.A map showing the full extent of the proposed works has been deposited in the Assembly Library.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any support his Department is offering Health and Social Care Trust workers whose Universal Credit and Rates Rebate claims closed due to payment of arrears from the recently agreed pay award.
answer_text: The processing of any pay award is complex, with many different calculations required to ensure that all salary-based allowances are paid at the correct rate and that staff will receive their correct payment of arrears. Employers are not aware of who, or who is not, in receipt of income-based benefits, such as Universal Credit and Rates Rebate among the c75,000 staff in HSC. Any process would therefore require individuals to self-identify and for manual procedures to be introduced. A phasing of payments to staff from the 2025/26 pay deal has been previously suggested. However, even if this were feasible, it would require significant time for the Business Services Organisation to process and would delay the pay award significantly. Individual benefits entitlement, and the income to be considered when assessing, is established in legislation. Within that, any backdated lump sum of pay is considered as income for the month in which it is received. It is easy to consider that phasing, for example, might be beneficial, however the reality is that paying in instalments may in fact result in further detriment, as individuals near the threshold may have entitlement removed for an even longer period. Neither employers, nor even individuals, would be able to predict the outcome of such measures. It would further not be feasible for Universal Credit (UC) assessors to examine each case on an individual basis to determine the impact of apportioned payments, nor is it possible to disregard back pay, as this would require a legislative amendment, and any measure taken to do so would impact on the allocation to DfC from DWP, who are responsible for UC. Individuals in receipt of Universal Credit should seek advice from their work coach or contact the service centre on 0800 0121 331, as neither my Department, nor individual employers can offer advice on benefit entitlements. Staff are encouraged to speak to their employers regarding any financial difficulties they are facing as a result of the backdated pay award, as employers can offer advice on any financial hardship support available.  I remain of the view that the only way in which such issues may be avoided in future years is the establishment of long-term, realistic, budgetary allocations which allow for improved planning.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on residents' parking schemes.
answer_text: I have considered my officials’ recommendations in relation to residents’ parking schemes that will provide a streamlined approach for considering, assessing and prioritising requests for schemes going forward. It is now my intention to issue a statement to the Assembly as to how future residents’ parking schemes will be assessed by my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any plans her Department has to improve the cycle network in South Belfast.
answer_text: My Department is progressing a number of schemes in South Belfast as part of the Belfast Cycling Network Delivery Plan. The Department will commence the Statutory Process and public consultation for the Ravenhill Road/Ormeau Embankment Cycle Routes Traffic Regulation Order in the coming months. Preliminary design of the Dublin Road/Botanic Avenue scheme is progressing with the aim of holding initial public engagement before summer 2026. Design work to replace the existing pop-up cycle lanes on the Donegall Road will also commence imminently.Allocations for the 2026-27 financial year have not yet been made, as the Draft Budget 2026-29/30 is currently out for consultation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount her Department has allocated to cycling infrastructure in South Belfast, between 2026 and 2027.
answer_text: My Department is progressing a number of schemes in South Belfast as part of the Belfast Cycling Network Delivery Plan. The Department will commence the Statutory Process and public consultation for the Ravenhill Road/Ormeau Embankment Cycle Routes Traffic Regulation Order in the coming months. Preliminary design of the Dublin Road/Botanic Avenue scheme is progressing with the aim of holding initial public engagement before summer 2026. Design work to replace the existing pop-up cycle lanes on the Donegall Road will also commence imminently.Allocations for the 2026-27 financial year have not yet been made, as the Draft Budget 2026-29/30 is currently out for consultation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline his Department’s position on the Public Office (Accountability) Bill.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether their Department intends to reform the Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997, in the 2022-27 Assembly mandate.
answer_text: Work is ongoing to reform the Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997. A comprehensive review and public consultation have been completed. Resulting policy proposals are currently under consideration with a view to bringing updated legislation before the Assembly as part of this year’s legislative programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether civil race equality protections are being reviewed by her Department, alongside other criminal law reform.
answer_text: Civil race equality protections are provided for in the Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997. Given the Department of Justice does not have responsibility for this legislation, the Department has no vires to review it.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on the progress of the hate crime legislation for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Department’s priority is to ensure that the most impactful provisions to strengthen hate crime legislation will be delivered in this mandate. Key hate crime provisions will be delivered in the current mandate as part of the Sentencing Bill and a Victims and Witnesses of Crime Bill.  Subject to Executive approval, I intend to introduce the Sentencing Bill to the Assembly shortly. This Bill will include provisions for a new statutory aggravator model for hate crime which will modernise hate crime in law in Northern Ireland and provide a more robust system to sanction offenders.Further provisions to support victims of hate crime will be included in the Victims and Witnesses of Crime Bill which is scheduled for introduction later this year. I remain committed to delivering the best outcomes for all victims of hate crime in this mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any actions his Department is taking to improve air quality in South Belfast.
answer_text: Responsibility for air quality assessment and review is shared between DAERA and district councils. DAERA fund council air quality monitoring via the Local Air Quality Management grant. Actions taken by councils to improve air quality will be detailed in annual reports available at https://www.airqualityni.co.uk/reports/district-council-reports-1.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she has assessed the need for the reinstatement of an early morning sailing for the Strangford Lough ferry.
answer_text: I have asked officials to complete a short piece of customer engagement to gather current views on a possible timetable adjustment.  This will help inform any future consideration of changes to the service.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she will conduct an assessment of the need for further safety measures at the junction of the Blacks Road and Upper Lisburn Road, Belfast, given the proximity of three schools.
answer_text: Unfortunately, the budget that the Executive receives from London still continues to be substantially short of what is needed, and as a result, my Department does not have funding for everything my Department wants or needs to do and therefore prioritisation is required to those locations deemed most in need.Regrettably, when assessed against these competing priorities, this junction could not be considered for any further safety measures at this time.However, to tackle the issue of speeding and increase road safety generally, I recently launched a public consultation on speed limits on 14 January that will close on 22 April 2026 which seeks views on the wider use of permanent 20mph speed limits, the lowering of national speed limit on rural single and dual carriageways, and alignment of HGV speed limits with other regions.  I would encourage as many people as possible to contribute to this significant and important piece of work to help inform the actions we take in relation to speed related matters in the future.I intend to publish the findings of the consultation later in 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department has prepared any official guidance on the use of the Ulster Banner.
answer_text: The Commission on Flags, Identity, Culture and Tradition (FICT) Final Report of 2021, established under the Fresh Start Agreement, emphasises the need for broad consensus on matters of flags and symbols to avoid exacerbating community divisions.	 	The report highlights the sensitivity of such issues and recommends approaches that promote mutual respect rather than unilateral changes.	 	It is for that reason I wrote to the Commonwealth Games Association and provided guidance which is in the public domain.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they will reassess the Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997, following the rise in race-based hate crime.
answer_text: The Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (RRO) provides protection against unlawful discrimination, victimisation and harassment in areas such as employment, education and the provision of goods, facilities and services. Matters relating to hate crime are addressed through criminal law and fall within the remit of the Department of Justice. However, to ensure the RRO provides appropriate protections, an extensive review and public consultation have been completed. Policy proposals are currently under consideration with a view to bringing updated legislation before the Assembly as part of this year’s legislative programme. We will continue to monitor equality legislation within our Department’s remit and work with relevant Departments to support wider efforts to tackle racism and promote good relations across all our communities.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether the current Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 is compatible with the no diminution of rights commitment in the Windsor Framework.
answer_text: The Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 is consistent with that commitment and the European Union’s Race Directive.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail when the draft recycling strategy was (i) presented to the Executive; and (ii) discussed by Executive Ministers.
answer_text: “Rethinking Our Resources” comprises two workstreams that aim to contribute to Northern Ireland’s Net Zero ambition, improve the environment and build a better, more circular economy. The first step focused on Waste and Recycling Reforms in Northern Ireland. As part of this work, my department consulted on a range of proposals to improve recycling and waste collections across households and businesses, which was carried out in 2024 under the heading ““Rethinking Our Resources - Measures for Climate Action and a Circular Economy”. The responses helped inform our draft Departmental Response which contains recommendations aimed at driving down the volume of residual waste and improving both the quality and quantity of material being collected for recycling, to support local processing. I am seeking support from my Executive colleagues to publish the Departmental Response, to provide local councils and businesses with clarity on future direction and help inform investment decisions. However, as set out in the ‘Conduct of Executive Business Protocol’, all aspects of Executive business are regarded as confidential therefore I cannot provide any further details.The second strand of this work is “Rethinking Our Resources: Northern Ireland Resources and Waste Management Strategy” which will set the policy direction at a strategic level for the future management of resources and waste, through a range of actions and targets over the next 6 years. This strategy will encourage everyone to play their part in creating a healthier ecosystem and long-term economic resilience.I launched the new Resources and Waste Management Strategy for consultation on 15 January, and I would encourage everyone to respond to ensure their views are captured.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether his Department is considering reforming laws and regulations surrounding conveyancing and surveying, in line with recent changes in the Republic of Ireland.
answer_text: The changes are being implemented further to the Land and Conveyancing Law Reform Act 2021, an Act of the Oireachtas. Policy relating to conveyancing and surveying attracts the interest of a range of statutory and non-statutory bodies. Many of those changes in that Act are outside the remit of my Department.Substantive Irish Land Law is an area of policy responsibility which is within my Department’s remit, however it is not an area under active development. Civil Law Reform officials are currently working on the forthcoming Bill which reforms the law of Marriage and Civil Partnership, advancing proposals to reform the law surrounding Divorce as well as providing policy advice across a range of other current areas of work. The Land Registry, within Land and Property Services, are currently implementing a new digital IT solution to underpin the transformation of Land Registration services which will better serve the needs of the property market in this jurisdiction.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any plans her Department has for the introduction of grants for domestic solar panels.
answer_text: Financial support is not currently available for homeowners who wish to install solar panels. The Windsor Framework enables the extension to the North of the VAT relief for energy saving materials that was previously available only in Britain. This means that people here can apply zero rates of VAT to the installation of energy-saving materials such as solar panels.Feedback from the low carbon heat consultation in 2025 suggested that combining solar panels with a heat pump could reduce overall operating costs. This option is being considered as we take forward the development of the low carbon heat and energy efficiency support scheme in 2026. In the meantime, households may still find solar panels or solar with battery storage, a worthwhile investment offering long-term savings, even in the absence of grant support.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any legal and administrative steps his Department will be required to take in order to lift the two-child benefit cap, in line with the rest of the UK.
answer_text: A Legislative Consent Motion was laid with the Assembly on 19 January 2026 seeking endorsement for the provisions of the Universal Credit (Removal of Two Child Limit) Bill to extend Northern Ireland. Subject to the Assembly’s approval of the motion, the change will take effect for assessment periods in Universal Credit beginning on or after 6 April 2026.It is currently anticipated that relevant increases will be actioned automatically on the Universal Credit system. Officials are working through the mechanisms for this and will update stakeholders when the process has been finalised.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail when the two-child benefit cap will be lifted in Northern Ireland, following the announcement in the Autumn budget 2025.
answer_text: Subject to the Assembly’s approval of a Legislative Consent Motion for provision in the Universal Credit (Removal of Two Child Limit) Bill to extend to Northern Ireland, the change will take effect for assessment periods in Universal Credit beginning on or after 6 April 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department has any plans to construct an additional access road on Parkmore Street, Belfast.
answer_text: My Department acts as a statutory consultee to the Planning process.  No proposals to construct an additional access road on Parkmore Street, Belfast have been submitted for consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether a formal consultation process was undertaken with residents of Ormeau Road, regarding the replacement of existing street lights.
answer_text: As part of my Department’s cyclical electrical inspection and testing programme, the street lighting on Ormeau Road was identified as having reached the end of its serviceable life and was therefore prioritised for replacement. This programme ensures that available funding is directed towards those street lights that are most in need of replacement and helps mitigate any risk to the public.Before works commenced, letters outlining the works, along with relevant contact details, were delivered to all affected residents.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether his Department expects to submit legislative reform to divorce and dissolution of civil partnerships to the Assembly by the end of the 2022-27 Assembly mandate.
answer_text: Following a consultation that concluded in September 2025, my officials completed a detailed report of findings.  I have agreed policy proposals for reform, which were circulated in December 2025 to Executive Colleagues for consideration. I hope these proposals will be discussed and agreed at an Executive meeting in the coming weeks. Subject to such agreement, the draft legislation could  be introduced to the Assembly before the end of the present mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he made a bid during the Proposed Draft Budget 2026-29/30 process to enable the delivery of an arts strategy.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they will take any steps to bring Northern Ireland's legal protection against racial discrimination in line with the rest of the UK.
answer_text: A review of the Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 has been conducted as part of the Racial Equality Strategy 2015-2025, including a 12-week public consultation which formally concluded in June 2023. An analysis report was published on 5 August 2024, and policy proposals are currently under consideration. We aim to bring updated legislation before the Assembly as part of this mandate’s legislative programme.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether a formal decision was made to (i) pause; or (ii) end, reform of the Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997.
answer_text: Work is ongoing to reform the Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997. A comprehensive review and public consultation have been completed. Resulting policy proposals are currently under consideration with a view to bringing updated legislation before the Assembly as part of this year’s legislative programme.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether the differences in Northern Ireland’s racial equality protections, compared to the rest of the UK, constitute a potential violation of the Executive’s Section 75 obligations.
answer_text: We recognise the importance of reviewing our legislation to ensure it continues to offer appropriate protections.We have therefore undertaken a comprehensive review of the Race Relations Order (Northern Ireland) 1997, including a 12-week public consultation which formally concluded in June 2023. An analysis report was published on 5 August 2024, and policy proposals are currently under consideration. We aim to bring updated legislation before the Assembly as part of this mandate’s legislative programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any plans for the introduction of an animal welfare strategy.
answer_text: My Animal Welfare Pathway, published in May 2025 after extensive stakeholder consultation, sets out ten animal welfare reforms to be delivered within the remainder of the mandate. This includes a commitment to bring forward proposals on the banning third-party puppy and kitten sales, reviewing dog breeding laws and consulting on proposals to ban aversive training devices. A copy of the Pathway is available on my Department’s website available here: www.daera-ni.gov.uk/publications/animal-welfare-pathway-advancing-animal-welfare-northern-ireland-2025-27
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether there has been a review of the Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 since the Racial Equality Strategy 2015–2025 was published.
answer_text: We would refer the Member to our response to AQW 38122/22-27, which addresses this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether his Department conducted an assessment of the economic impact of the Proposed Draft Budget 2026-29/30.
answer_text: In line with the usual approach the Draft Budget 2026-2029/30 document sets out an overview of the economic context against which my proposed draft budget is set.I launched an overarching public consultation on my proposals on 6 January 2026 to allow citizens, business and communities have their say. Responses to this consultation will inform decisions taken by the Executive in agreeing a Budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the date on which he first shared his draft multi-year budget allocations with Executive colleagues.
answer_text: Following a period of political engagement, I shared a formal paper detailing proposed allocations with Executive colleagues on 23 December.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether the Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 has been reviewed since the Racial Equality Strategy 2015–2025 was released.
answer_text: A review of the Race Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 has been conducted as part of the Racial Equality Strategy 2015-2025, including a 12-week public consultation which formally concluded in June 2023. An analysis report was published on 5 August 2024, a copy of which can be found https://www.executiveoffice-ni.gov.uk/publications/consultation-review-race-relations-ni-order-1997-analysis-report.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she responded to requests for meetings from the Criminal Bar Association, ahead of the beginning of strike action.
answer_text: We have engaged, repeatedly and intensively, with the Bar and the CBA since before service withdrawal began in November 2024. I met with them recently on 5 November 2025 and it was agreed, by all parties, that the meeting was constructive. Officials had further meetings with representatives of the CBA and Bar on 28 November and 8 December 2025, when the roadmap to address remaining issues was agreed. Following the subsequent decision of the CBA to further withdraw services from 5 January 2026, I wrote to the CBA membership directly on the 5th January 2026 and, based on the additional clarity provided in their response, invited them to a meeting on 15 January 2026. That meeting was very constructive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount of funding that was requested as part of the multi-year budget process, to support the delivery of the All-Island Strategic Rail Review.
answer_text: A bid for £220m was made for funding from the multi-year budget to support delivery of the All-Island Strategic Rail Review. This included the following projects:ProjectTotal£kAntrim to Knockmore reopening25,572Belfast to Border linespeed improvements42,000Belfast to Derry enhancements44,625Craigavon New Halt and Park and Ride1,050Donegal Quay to Yorkgate enhancement500Portadown to Armagh line reopening8,413Portadown to Derry Line Reopening35,065Rail Electrification Phase 1 (Belfast to Border)43,781Rail Electrification Phase 219,049Total220,055

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the Minister of Finance's Proposed Draft Budget 2026-29/30, in relation to funding for arts and culture.
answer_text: The proposed allocation for my Department in the Draft Budget 2026-30, is significantly short of my requirements and presents a very constrained position across all areas of my Department, including arts and culture.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail a timetable for her consultation on the wider use of permanent 20mph speed limits.
answer_text: Road safety is a high priority for my Department, and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users. I launched a public consultation on speed limits on 14 January that will close on 22 April 2026. The consultation seeks views on the wider use of permanent 20mph speed limits, the lowering of national speed limit on rural single and dual carriageways, and alignment of HGV speed limits with other regions.  I would encourage as many people as possible to contribute to this significant and important piece of work to help inform the actions we take in relation to speed related matters in the future.I intend to publish the findings of the consultation later in 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the Knockbreda Road pedestrian crossing, Belfast.
To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the work of the McCloud team, in relation to the payments for Civil Servant pensions.
answer_text: Civil Service Pensions’ priority remains the provision of Remedial Service Statements (RSSs) to pensioner and partially retired members affected by the McCloud Judgement by the legislative deadline of 31 March 2027, and the majority of the McCloud Implementation team are allocated to this work. As of 31 January 2026, 70% (6,871) of RSSs have been issued.Work has commenced on processing returned Remedy Choice forms and the completion of necessary pension adjustments. Members continue to receive their existing pension pending any required adjustment, and interest will be added for any arrears due at the date of payment.Information on progress of processing returned choice forms is being developed for members and will be made available via the Civil Service Pensions website, which will be updated regularly.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the Executive Legislation Programme 2025-2026.
To ask the Minister of Justice to outline the steps she will take to resolve the dispute with members of the Criminal Bar Association over Crown Court legal aid fees.
To ask the Minister of Education for an update on his Department's planning for a multi-year budget.
answer_text: As set out in my Written Ministerial Statement to the Assembly of 6 January 2026, the Minister of Finance has issued a draft multi-year budget for Northern Ireland for the four-year period from 1 April 2026 to 31 March 2030 for public consultation.I would firstly note that this draft budget has been issued for consultation without first being agreed by the Executive. While acknowledging the significant challenges involved in setting a budget, if one were prepared to accept the devastating cuts implicit in this draft budget, as a practical matter, such a budget would be undeliverable.Education continues to face unavoidable cost pressures and rising service demands associated with delivering statutory and policy obligations that the proposed budget does not fully address.In order to live within the proposed budget allocation would require me to make savings of c£826m in 2026-27, £1.01bn in 2027-28 and £1.15bn in 2028-29. These are simply not practically deliverable.Given that c82% of the education budget is expended on staffing costs, there is no reasonable prospect of my Department being able to live within the proposed budget in the short-term without mass compulsory redundancies which would devastate educational outcomes and undermine the very fabric of our system, as well as requiring dedicated in-year redundancy funding of a similar level to the necessary savings.If this budget were to proceed and in the absence of redundancies, I would be left with no choice but to cut major schemes that families and communities rely on, including home-to-school transport, free school meals, school uniform grants, youth services and extended schools. The Northern Ireland Childcare Scheme would also become unsustainable. Even after implementing cuts of this magnitude, the Department would fall far short of the savings required. This stark reality demonstrates that the proposed budget is simply undeliverable.My Department will continue to seek to identify areas that could potentially deliver a significant reduction in the education cost base, whilst aiming to protect the overall quality of education delivery.  However, to be enacted, these measures will require the support of the Executive.Education has faced historic underfunding for many years, with an inadequate baseline that has only been sustained through in-year allocations. This structural weakness cannot continue to be ignored and we cannot continue to neglect education through more years of underfunding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on his Department's planning for a multi-year budget.
answer_text: You will be aware that Health has faced an extremely challenging year in terms of finances with a funding gap of some £615 million. I have been clear from the outset that Health could not breakeven without additional support from the Executive. While some £69 million was received to support my Ministerial Direction to implement the HSC pay award this left a residual shortfall of £140 million for pay costs alone.My department identified £300 million in savings and cost containment measures, supplemented by £25 million from June Monitoring, reducing the non-pay gap to £90 million. However, when combined with the pay shortfall, the total gap stands at £230 million. This overspend could materially impact the 2026–27 budget, as any deduction from our baseline would further constrain next year’s position.My department has been working throughout this year to deliver longer-term financial sustainability and reform of HSC services as evidenced by the Health and Social Care Reset Plan which I published in July 2025. This plan focuses on stabilisation, reform, and delivery, aiming to establish neighbourhood-centred care and drive efficiencies. While we bid for additional funding under the multi-year budget exercise to support the Reset Plan, the draft budget proposals 2026-29/30 the Finance Minister has published confirm an extremely challenging outlook for Health.Given the funding being proposed for Health, we will have generate savings internally to cover our inescapable commitments. I have advised the Finance Minister that delivering savings of this magnitude in a single year is not feasible. Therefore, we require agreement on a recovery plan over the three-year budget period. This would allow managed delivery, though it remains exceptionally challenging and not without risk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when her Department will respond to the planning application for Forge Primary School.
answer_text: My officials engaged fully with the pre-application discussions for the planning application submitted by the Department for Education for the development of a new primary school building for Forge Integrated Primary School.  The full planning application was lodged in 2025, and the Department’s pre-application advice in relation to access, movement and parking was not reflected within this submission. My officials are currently undertaking a robust analysis of the documentation submitted in support of full planning application for this site and will respond to the planning authorities consultation in the coming weeks. We are supportive of the development of the site however road safety is a high priority for my Department, and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users. We are acutely aware that children and young people are amongst the most vulnerable groups using our roads, particularly around schools and this must be integral to our assessment and response.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the amount of funding dispersed through the Sandy Row revitalisation scheme; and (ii) the number of businesses that benefitted.
answer_text: Although my Department is funding the Sandy Row Revitalisation scheme, delivery is being led by Belfast City Council.  To date, 38 applicants have received grants totalling £146,168.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the prioritisation of Forge Integrated Primary School for major capital funding.
answer_text: The design of the new build school is well progressed and the planning application for the project was submitted in June 2025.  However, due to current budget pressures, all projects on the Major Capital Works Programme are being progressed to the pre-construction stage and will only be permitted to progress to construction when there is sufficient capital budget availability.  I continue to make the case for much needed investment for my capital funding programmes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on his Department's Shared Property Management reform plans.
answer_text: I refer the member to the answer I recently gave in response to AQO 2770/22-27, which sets out the current position in relation to this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether the Holyland and Lower Ormeau Strategic Partnership Group still exists.
answer_text: The Strategic Partnership Group has not met since October 2022 and will only reconvene if strategic matters are escalated to Ministers by the council-led Wider University and Lower Ormeau Inter-Agency Group.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of the impact of fallen redwood trees in Belvoir Park Forest, on the accessibility of the forest paths.
answer_text: I understand that fallen trees, including redwood trees, affecting some of the paths in Belvoir Forest have been cleared and the path network is fully accessible.  However, recent storms have resulted in isolated windblown trees being present within some areas of the forest which are not affecting accessibility.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the annual spending on maintenance at Belvoir Park Forest, for the last five years.
answer_text: Forest Service undertakes the maintenance programmes at Belvoir Park Forest from within its overall operational budgets.  The spend incurred in each of the last 5 years for the delivery of programmed work is set out as follows. These are direct costs and do not include overheads.  Year 2020-2021 = £24,172Year 2021-2022 = £36,998Year 2022-2023 = £25,971Year 2023-2024 = £30,327Year 2024-2025 = £54,229
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the announced four per cent rise in electricity tariffs for 119,000 customers in Northern Ireland, expected in January 2026.
answer_text: Any increase in energy costs is unwelcome news for consumers.While wholesale energy prices have reduced, recent price changes are driven by adjustments to non-energy tariff components, such as network and system operation costs. These changes are essential to ensure the continued security and stability of the entire electrical system.According to the latest Quarterly Retail Energy Market Monitoring (QREMM) report from the Utility Regulator, domestic electricity prices in the North are broadly comparable to the average in Britain.Consumers are always encouraged to review their current tariffs. The Consumer Council for Northern Ireland offers a free, independent electricity price comparison tool on its website, enabling customers to compare all available tariffs across the North.  Electricity price comparison tool | Consumer Council.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on his Department's needs assessment for ADHD services, including work on shared care agreements.
answer_text: The regional needs assessment on ADHD demand, commissioned by my Department, represents an important point of departure in establishing the basis upon which a future commissioning model in support of ADHD services across Trusts might be developed. In approaching this exercise, the primary objectives are to gauge the overall demand for an ADHD service based upon a model of provision across the lifespan, and to provide detailed recommendations to inform discussion.It is envisaged that these recommendations will offer a view upon the proposed structure of a future clinical model, shared care arrangements, and the necessary steps that might be required to ensure regional consistency and equity of access, an exploration of operational feasibility and proposals associated with implementation and staffing costs. I am currently considering the recommendations; however, my Department is currently operating within a constrained budget and is required to make decisions in relation to the work that can be delivered within current resources. Any decision to commission services in the future will be based on an assessment of the level of demand for services. I hope you find this information helpful.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any external meetings he has had in relation to the review of the licensing system provided for in the 2021 Licensing Act, since February 2024.
answer_text: •	The Society of Independent Brewers and Associates and Peter Martin MLA on 9 July 2025.•	Hospitality Ulster and Brian Murphy, Binder Dijker Otte (BDO) NI on 30 July 2025.•	Free The Night on 1 October 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail whether his Department has considered adopting programmes like Safe Dates and Coaching Boys Into Men, to help address misogyny and violence against women and girls in schools.
answer_text: Developing healthy, respectful relationships is essential to helping children and young people gain knowledge, understanding and skills needed to make informed choices and behave responsibly.Opportunities to address issues the issues you highlight, currently exist within the Northern Ireland Curriculum, delivered as part of Relationships and Sexuality Education (RSE), which is mandatory for all pupils of compulsory school age.  It is a matter for individual schools to decide which organisations, programmes and initiatives they choose to support their delivery of the Northern Ireland Curriculum and it is the responsibility of each school's Board of Governors to ensure that a comprehensive, age-appropriate RSE programme is delivered, that the meets the statutory minimum content and is aligned to the school’s own RSE policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of (i) primary; and (ii) secondary, schools that (a) have; and (b) do not have, mental health services for children and young people.
answer_text: Schools are not responsible for the provision of mental health services for children and young people, this would be a matter for the Department of Health.The Education Authority HOPE Service provides school counselling or therapy for pupils in post-primary mainstream, special schools and EOTAS provisions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any steps being taken to tackle the rise in addiction to over-the-counter medication.
answer_text: My officials are aware of issues related to the misuse of over-the-counter medication, such as combination painkillers including codeine.Alongside colleagues in the Strategic Planning and Performance Group and Health and Social Care Trusts, my Department has been reviewing cases and are in discussion with the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA), who are ultimately responsible for the classification and regulation of medicines in the UK. Officials recently launched materials linked to the ‘Addiction can affect anyone’ social media campaign to highlight the harm associated with addiction to combination painkillers including ibuprofen and codeine tablets, of which Nurofen Plus is a brand. A press release Addiction can affect anyone – Department of Health was issued on the 8th December 2025 and is available here: https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/news/addiction-can-affect-anyone-department-healthOfficials have used media channels to further promote the campaign, including videos with an Addictions Services service user (which can be accessed at https://vimeo.com/1129533197/ab51fd4e80?fl=tl&fe=ec) and a senior Psychiatrist Dr Joy Watson which can be accessed at https://x.com/healthdpt/status/1986033250259890352?s=20. An animation to guide patients to support services and other resources including patient information, is available at the NI Formulary Patient Zone https://niformulary.hscni.net/patient-area/pain-topics/managing-your-pain-effectively-using-over-the-counter-otc-medicines/. In addition, these Patient information leaflets and posters are now also available in every GP surgery and Community Pharmacy in Northern Ireland to signpost to resources and services to support them in their journey to recovery.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any guidance his Department has for early-intervention for boys who (i) bully, control or threaten others; (ii) show cruelty to animals; (iii) regularly exhibit misogynistic behaviour, sexual aggression or coercion in relationships.
answer_text: Schools have a vital role to play in educating children about risks of harm, supported through appropriate guidance and preventative education programmes within the school curriculum to make children and young people aware of the risks that exist. Under the Addressing Bullying in Schools Act (Northern Ireland) 2016, schools are required to record the motivation and the outcome of all incidents of bullying. This includes identifying any specific problems, such as the behaviours referred to.Any form of harmful sexual behaviour sits within the overall context of safeguarding and child protection guidance for education settings.The legislation and guidance as mentioned applies only to interactions between school pupils.  It does not seek to address cruelty towards animals.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail whether he is taking any steps to prioritise integrated schools, which lost Fresh Start funding, in his Department's major capital works programme.
answer_text: All projects on the Major Capital Works Programme, including those which lost Fresh Start funding, are being progressed to the pre-construction stage. However, due to current budget pressures, projects will only be permitted to progress to construction as and when there is sufficient capital budget availability.  I continue to make the case for much needed investment for my capital funding programmes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of meetings he has had with (i) the Campaign for Real Ale; and (ii) independent brewers, in relation to the surrender principle, in the last 18 months.
answer_text: NoneI met with the Society of Independent Brewers and Associates once on 9 July 2025.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the number of times the Executive has discussed a multi-year budget, since February 2024.
answer_text: Executive business is confidential.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the extension of Metro bus services to Carryduff.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:Translink operates its network of services to connect communities and provide options that best meet the overall demand, whilst making the most efficient use of its existing budget and resources. Translink recognise the growth of communities and want to align its services with existing and emerging demand. From 1st September last, the Ulsterbus service provision from Carryduff to Belfast has consisted of 61 journeys per day Monday to Friday, 64 journeys on a Saturday, and 36 journeys on a Sunday on our 15, 515, 516, 518a, and 520 services.  Translink also operate 14 services Monday to Friday from Carryduff to Belfast on our Cairnshill 652 service.  These services provide a combined frequency of approximately every 30 minutes at peak times including Saturday and hourly on a Sunday through Carryduff. In recent years Translink have also introduced two late night services for the pre-Christmas period serving Carryduff on a Friday and Saturday night. This will continue for a 52-week trial after Christmas into 2026. Translink are currently reviewing the service provision in this area to assess if any modification is possible in the current funding environment, and they are also conscious there are changes on the horizon in respect of land development. These plans are in their infancy and Translink will inform stakeholders ahead of any potential changes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any steps he is taking to support migrant care workers.
answer_text: I fully recognise and value the important contribution workers from overseas make in assisting us to maintain the delivery of services as well as to our economy, public services and communities. Given the increasing demands being placed on the health service, even with locally commissioned professional training programmes, there will always be a need for international recruitment at all levels within health and social care supported by the UK immigration system. Immigration is a reserved matter and whilst I and my Department have raised our local issues through the Migration Advisory Committee, and with the Home Office directly, ultimately the UK Government will take decisions for the whole of the UK, including Northern Ireland, and is therefore beyond my and the NI Assembly’s control.  All HSC Trusts remain committed to promoting equality of opportunity and good relations.  They are also committed to celebrating diversity and treating everyone with dignity and respect.  Their aim, in line with the HSC Values, is to provide and promote inclusive, accessible and responsive services while celebrating diversity and the great benefits of shared experiences both of our staff and service users.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any action she is taking to support the uptake in electric cars, given the disparity in electricity costs between GB and NI.
answer_text: The Department for Infrastructure (DfI) is responsible for the decarbonisation of transport including electric vehicle (EV) uptake. This has included joining the Britain wide Vehicle Emissions Trading Scheme (VETS), designed to encourage the sale of zero emission vehicles. Access to EV charge point provisions across the North has increased by 89% since April 2003, resulting in over 20 charge point operators managing 738 publicly accessible EV charge points. Further support has been available through the Shared Island Fund ‘Sports Club EV Charging Scheme’ and ‘On Street Residential Charge Point’ schemes.   The latest Quarterly Retail Energy Market Monitoring Report (QREMM) published by the Utility Regulator, highlights that domestic electricity prices in the North are lower than counterparts in the South and comparable to the Britain median prices. Most EVs achieve around 80% efficiency, compared to just 20% for traditional internal combustion engines, while also offering significant savings on maintenance and repair costs.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any conversations she has had with the Minister for Communities, regarding the importance of the arts in our night-time economy, over the last 12 months.
answer_text: My officials have engaged regularly with DfC officials on the night-time economy in the past year. My Department collaborated with Tourism NI and the three Belfast Business Improvement Districts to commission research into the night-time economy. DfC officials were interviewed as part of the research and have been kept updated as the work progresses.		I was pleased that Minister Lyons supported the Belfast late-night transport pilot scheme I proposed alongside Minister Kimmins. Reliable late-night transport for Belfast can support the arts and my hope is that a successful pilot will see the scheme expanded to other regions.		My Department also has representation on Tourism NI’s Culture, Arts & Heritage Stakeholder Leadership Group alongside DfC officials. In partnership with this group, Tourism NI has developed a specific Visitor Experience Development plan to fully realise the importance of these thematic areas to the overall visitor experience. A set of actions has been developed and the group meets each quarter to review progress and identify areas for collaboration.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for an update on his Department’s work in monitoring the effectiveness of the Welsh pathway for hypermobility disorders; and (ii) whether he has given further consideration to introducing a healthcare pathway for hypermobility spectrum disorders, including hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
answer_text: At present there is no formal reporting on the prevalence of hypermobility in Northern Ireland, nor has my Department considered the effectiveness of the Welsh pathway for hypermobility disorders. At present my Department have not taken forward any work on the potential introduction of a healthcare pathway for hypermobility spectrum disorders, including hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome. Services conform to established NICE guidance as is standard with all health services in NI. We accept that the long waiting times are not optimum for patient treatment and satisfaction but the pressure our system is currently under will still allow for those with the most pressing clinical need to be seen the soonest, after thoughtful consultant-led triage. In this way we are assured that the current service is satisfactory but accept the potential for improvement in the future.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when her Department intends to commence work on repairing the potholes on Ballyskeagh Road, Belfast.
answer_text: Details of the resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year are included in my Department’s Annual Report to Council.Officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any actions he is taking to support families of year 7 children born between 1 July and 30 August, who would otherwise be eligible for Tax-Free Childcare, and the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme.
answer_text: The eligibility criteria and functionality of the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme (NICSS) is aligned with His Majesty’s Revenue and Customs (HMRC) Tax Free Childcare (TFC) Scheme.  This alignment, and use of existing government systems and information, ensures the NICSS has robust oversight and avoids the excessive expenditure required to develop a bespoke verification system for Northern Ireland.    Under the current HMRC rules, eligibility for TFC ends on 1 September after a child’s 11th birthday (this is extended until the child’s 16th birthday for those with special educational needs). No changes are planned to how we operate the NICSS, however, should there be changes to the eligibility for TFC, these changes will automatically be reflected in the NICSS. I am committed to listening to stakeholders and will consider this issue as part of the consultation on the draft Executive Early Learning and Childcare Strategy. My priority is to ensure the Strategy reflects real-life needs of working families and delivers support that parents and providers can endorse.  My department will continue to monitor and review provision to ensure support is focussed on families most in need.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any steps he has taken to protect Civil Service impartiality, since February 2024.
answer_text: The principles of impartiality in the Civil Service are enshrined in its Code of Ethics. The Code establishes the foundational standards and expectations of conduct for all civil servants, including ensuring that their actions and decisions remain free from political influence and are guided by objectivity, integrity, and professionalism. Since February 2024, my department has issued several reminders to all civil service staff on the importance of reading and abiding by the Code of Ethics, the most recent of which was in December 2025. My department developed a new corporate induction e-learning programme, which is now mandatory for all new entrants to the Civil Service. This launched in February 2025 to include more prominent learning on the Code of Ethics. In addition, since March 2025, a refreshed in-person corporate induction is offered to all new staff, which includes a session on Ethics and Values in Civil Service Leadership, specifically covering the Code of Ethics and the Nolan Principles.  My department also ensures the Code of Ethics is regularly reviewed, with minor amendments made in 2025 to clarify arrangements for civil servants who work for Ministers in another administrations.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether their considerations of the findings set out in the UK Covid-19 Inquiry Module 2C report on core decision-making and political governance will include the need for Executive reform.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail any action he is taking to assist in reducing waiting times for dermatology appointments at the Ulster Hospital.
answer_text: The Trust are maximising clinical capacity within its core services, along with engaging through the DoH Waiting List Initiative and Waiting List Reduction Schemes to increase availability of capacity and reduce waiting times for patients.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether he has met members of the Fiscal Commission, since taking office.
answer_text: The independent Fiscal Commission produced its final report in May 2022 and presented it to the Finance Minister at that time, Conor Murphy. Having fulfilled its terms of reference, the Commission ended as an ongoing entity shortly after this.Therefore, I have not met with the members of the Commission in that capacity since taking office in February of this year, however I continue to utilise the Commission’s comprehensive final report and its suite of recommendations to inform my department’s work on a full Fiscal Framework.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when she will announce the next steps, following the consultation on developer contributions for wastewater infrastructure.
answer_text: On 21 March I launched a consultation on developer contributions for wastewater infrastructure. The consultation ran for 14 weeks and sought views on options for a possible pathway to introduce developer contributions – either on a voluntary basis or in the form of a compulsory levy – which may help to fund wastewater upgrades to unlock the capacity of our sewerage network. 293 responses to the consultation were received.I am currently considering my decision on the way forward on developer contributions with a view to announcing this in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail spending on public transport, funded by developer contribution, in each constituency, in each of the last four years.
answer_text: Translink does not hold information on public transport spending funded by developer contributions. Any such expenditure arising from planning agreements is managed at local council level under Section 76 of the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011. Details of spending by constituency over the last four years would be held by the respective councils.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail whether his Department responded to the consultation on Developer Contributions for Wastewater Infrastructure.
answer_text: My Department did not provide a formal response to the Department for Infrastructure’s consultation on Developer Contributions for Wastewater Infrastructure.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any action she is taking to reduce incidents of speeding in Belfast.
answer_text: Road safety is a high priority for my Department, and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users. I intend to launch a public consultation in the new year on a number of speed related matters. The consultation will seek views on the wider use of permanent 20mph speed limits, the lowering of national speed limit on rural single and dual carriageways, and alignment of HGV speed limits with other regions.  When the consultation is launched, I would encourage as many people as possible to contribute to this significant and important piece of work.The enforcement of the current speed limit within Belfast City Centre is the responsibility of the PSNI and concerns regarding driver behaviour such as speeding should be directed to the PSNI using the non emergency 101 phone number.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they have spoken to the Head of Civil Service regarding adherence to the Civil Service Code of Ethics following the Education Minister's visit to Israel and the occupied territories.
To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the amount spent on overtime for PSNI officers, in each of the last five financial years.
answer_text: Any expenditure incurred by the PSNI on overtime is an operational matter for the Chief Constable, for which he is accountable to the Policing Board.You may wish to contact PSNI directly to request this information.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the rationale for the deployment of Armed Response Vehicle PSNI officers at the 2025 Belfast Christmas market.
answer_text: The utilisation of policing resources, including the deployment of Armed Response Vehicles, is an operational matter for the Chief Constable, who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board. As I am committed to respecting the operational independence of the Chief Constable and the role of the Policing Board, I would suggest you direct your question to the Chief Constable or the Policing Board

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any steps his Department is taking to support charities who work with those awaiting an ADHD or autism diagnosis.
answer_text: Charities, as part of the community and voluntary (C&V) sector, play a vital role in supporting individuals awaiting an ADHD or autism diagnosis. Action 17 of the Mental Health Strategy commits to “fully integrate the community and voluntary sector in mental health service delivery with a lifespan approach, including the development of a protocol to make maximum use of the sector’s expertise”.  The Strategy envisages the C&V sector as true partners who are fully integrated in ensuring improved outcomes for the population and fully included in the planning, development and delivery of mental health services. In response to MHS Action 17, the Department has recently undertaken a review of the size, scale and capacity of the C&V sector to identify what actions are required to fully integrate the sector into the delivery of mental health services.  It is anticipated that that report will be published in the coming weeks, at which point consideration will be given to how best to implement the proposed actions, subject to budget availability.All charities which make a contribution towards the strategic objectives of the Department had an opportunity to apply for the Core Grant Funding Scheme for 2026/27, which will see a minimum of 64 organisations receive a financial contribution towards their core fixed costs next year.In relation to ADHD, there is currently no adult ADHD service in Northern Ireland. The ADHD needs assessment, commissioned by the Department of Health in early February 2025 has now been received, I and my officials are now considering the findings, with a view to identifying next steps and arranging publication thereafter.  The role of the Community and Voluntary sector has been taken into consideration within the report. In terms of autism support, the Department continues to engage with autistic people and the community and voluntary sector through the Department’s Autism Forum established in 2021, which is co-chaired by an autistic advocate, to consider issues and challenges on a thematic basis.  The Forum comprises of people with lived experience of autism and the community and voluntary sector organisations who represent them. The Department also continues to attend regional Autism Fora and Stakeholder Groups on a quarterly basis to enable continued engagement on the aims and improvements to be achieved in the Autism Strategy.  More recently, my Department has had significant engagement with the voluntary and community sector in the development of the Learning Disability Strategic Plan to finalise the draft Learning Disability Service Model and Children with Disabilities framework.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (ii) any meetings with Ministers of Education, or their officials, from outside the UK or Ireland, and (ii) whether these were official meetings.
answer_text: Since February 2024, I have engaged extensively with Education Ministers and senior officials from outside the UK to share best practice and strengthen international collaboration.In March 2025, I represented Northern Ireland at the International Summit on the Teaching Profession in Iceland and in May 2024 and 2025, I attended the Education World Forum in London. Both events provided valuable opportunities to network and exchange ideas with Ministers from a range of countries, though some were on the margins of the events.In addition, I have held bilateral meetings with the New Zealand Minister for Education on several occasions and during a visit to Israel last year, I met with senior officials from the Israeli Department of Education.

answer_text: In this period, I have been at one meeting - to help launch the ‘Reconnected Belfast’ project in Alexandra Park.Departmental officials engage regularly with key stakeholders, elected representatives and community partners regarding interface issues as a regular part of our community engagement activities. I have outlined in the table below the meetings held by my Interfaces Team regarding the interface at Alexandra Park, Belfast with external stakeholders between February 2024 and 20 November 2025. question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when the construction on the (i) exterior; and (ii) carpark, of Belfast Grand Central Station, will be complete.
answer_text: The remaining works at Belfast Grand Central Station, including the exterior construction and new public car park, are scheduled to be completed by Autumn 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the implementation of the Domestic Abuse (Safe Leave) Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.
answer_text: I plan to have the necessary legislation in place before the end of the current Assembly mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department plans to carry out an assessment of Cromac Street, Belfast, for resurfacing.
answer_text: Details of the resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year are included in my Department’s Annual Report to Council.Officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the active travel improvements between St. Ita's Primary School, Belfast, and Cairnshill park and ride facility.
answer_text: My Department recently completed a scheme on Alderwood Hill aimed at improving the pedestrian access between St Ita’s Primary School and Cairnshill Park and Ride which included the installation of pencil bollards to prevent inconsiderate parking on footways, creating a safer walking corridor for pupils; a zebra crossing to enhance safe access to the school and the provision of an additional layby to support traffic progression.This scheme has been designed to integrate with the future Lisburn & Castlereagh City Council project to deliver an offline footpath between Cairnshill Park & Ride and Alderwood Hill as part of their Carryduff Greenway proposals.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any actions her Department is taking to ensure that the Driver and Vehicle Agency is notified when an individual is deemed unfit to drive by a medical professional.
answer_text: The Driver & Vehicle Agency (DVA), as the licensing authority, is required to ensure that driving licences are issued only to individuals who meet the prescribed medical fitness standards. Under Article 9 of the Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1981, applicants for a driving licence are legally obliged to declare any medical condition that may affect their ability to drive safely.Where a driver makes such a declaration, or concerns are raised by another source, including a medical practitioner, DVA initiates medical investigations to determine fitness to drive. These investigations may involve reviewing medical reports, seeking further information from healthcare professionals, or referring the individual for specialist assessment. Decisions are made in line with UK medical standards set out in Assessing Fitness to Drive – A Guide for Medical Professionals.While the legal duty to notify rests with the driver, DVA relies on guidance issued by the General Medical Council (GMC) and professional ethics to ensure doctors act when necessary. GMC guidance requires doctors to advise patients of their duty to inform DVA and, where a patient refuses or lacks capacity and poses a serious risk, to disclose relevant information directly to DVA without consent in the public interest.The Driver & Vehicle Agency (DVA) continues to work closely with key stakeholders, including the British Medical Association (BMA NI), OptometryNI and General Practice Northern Ireland (GPNI), to increase awareness and improve processes within the existing legislative framework.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any steps his Department is taking to ensure that the Driver and Vehicle Agency is notified when an individual is deemed unfit to drive by a medical professional.
answer_text: The Department for Infrastructure has a statutory duty to be satisfied as to the medical fitness to drive of an applicant for, or holder of, a driving licence, under the Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1981.  Standards and guidance for medical professionals in relation to medical fitness to drive are contained in the publication: Assessing fitness to drive: a guide for medical professionals - GOV.UK (www.gov.uk) which is issued by the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency. Doctors and other healthcare professionals have a duty to follow all relevant professional standards and guidelines, including those referred to within the DVLA guidance relating to fitness to drive. The relevant professional standards for doctors are set by the General Medical Council which are available at:  Confidentiality: patients' fitness to drive and reporting concerns to the DVLA or DVA - professional standards - GMC (gmc-uk.org).My officials have engaged with DVA and with GPNI which brings  together a multi-disciplinary working group to provide the most up to date and relevant information for Northern Ireland Primary Care. As a result a webinar was developed and delivered in October 2025 to GPs. The webinar prompted a discussion on a range of related topics and now forms part of an information library on this issue and is available not just to GPs, but to other medical professionals.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill has been drafted.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department will assess the junction of the A49 and B6 for safety improvements.
answer_text: Road safety is a high priority for my Department, and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users. I can advise that my department has already introduced a number of measures to improve road safety at this location, and officials are developing a preliminary design to change the layout of this junction.The development of this scheme will involve detailed design, accommodation works, land acquisition, funding and a separate procurement process. We are also considering what additional traffic management measures could be introduced to improve road safety in the short term.
