question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the work her Department undertakes to ensure Invest NI, and other business support opportunities, are made available and visible to new communities.
answer_text: As an inclusive society everyone should have an equal chance to succeed and as a Department we recognise the importance of, and continue to raise awareness of, the contribution played by ethnic minorities in the economy and how their contribution can be maximised. 	A key focus of my economic plan is to ensure that everyone across the North has access to good jobs.		This includes those who face barriers in accessing employment opportunities and those from ethnic minority backgrounds.		On 19 February 2025, I met with the EMEEN Network (the Ethnic Minority Employment and Entrepreneurship Network) to discuss key findings from the inaugural Ethnic Minority Employment & Careers Conference hosted by Invest NI with a focus on how best maximise the economic contribution of our ethnic minority workforce. I subsequently invited Mr Steve Lazars (founder of the EMEEN network) to join my Economic Vision Engagement Forum.		In recognition of the skills shortages and labour challenges currently facing businesses the Department is collaborating with community stakeholders and colleagues across government, including Invest NI, Department for Communities, The Executive Office and councils, to support businesses to identify and recruit from within alternative talent pools including the refugee and minority ethnic communities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline what actions his Department has taken to ensure artists have adequate facilities to work from, for example, studio spaces.
answer_text: The Arts Council, as an arms-length body of my Department, supports the maintenance of creative spaces through its funding streams.  Belfast City Council is undertaking work on Creative Workspace Provision in Belfast/Greater Belfast.  This work is ongoing with the Arts Council engaged as a key partner.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will answer (i) AQW 27565/22-27, (ii) AQW 26723/22-27, (iii) AQW 26217/22-27, and (iv) AQW 26583/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 27565/22-27 was answered on 13 October 2025.AQW 26723/22-27 was answered on 25 July 2025.AQW 26217/22-27 was answered on 16 December 2025.AQW 26583/22-27 was answered on 26 June 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 24409/22-27, for an update on the research exercise in demand for ADHD assessment and diagnosis.
answer_text: The ADHD needs assessment is not yet complete.  The initial estimated completion date was end June 2025 and considerable work has already been progressed. However, due to a desire and demand for further stakeholder engagement, information gathering and analysis which will ensure a robust evidence base and proposals, it is likely that the work will now be completed by September 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer (i) AQW 26970/22-27; and (ii) AQW 26969/22-27.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the publication of an application and assessment process for residents' parking schemes.
answer_text: I am currently considering my officials’ recommendations in relation to the development of an application and assessment process for residents’ parking schemes and it is my intention to make an announcement as to how future schemes will be assessed by my Department in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail how funds from any underspend in the Sandy Row Revitalisation Grant Scheme will be used.
answer_text: The Sandy Row Revitalisation scheme is ongoing.  At this time, no underspend has been identified.  Any underspend in this scheme will be considered and prioritised within the overall DfC allocation alongside existing commitments and priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail whether the Legislative Consent Motion for the Data (Use and Access) Bill was in his day one brief.
answer_text: ANSWERA Legslative Consent Memorandum for the Data (Use and Access) Bill was not included in the First Day brief. Officials provided a written briefing on this on 11 March 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail when he was first briefed (i) verbally and (ii) in writing, on the Legislative Consent Motion for the Data Use and Access Bill.
answer_text: ANSWERThe first written briefing I received on the Legislative Consent Motion for the Data Use and Access Bill from my officials was on 11 March 2025. I was briefed verbally on this motion on 22 May 2025 by my officials.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department has engaged with (i) Tourism Ireland; (ii) Tourism NI; or (iii) the Irish Government ,on plans for the European Year of the Normans.
answer_text: My Department has not had any direct engagement with Tourism Ireland, Tourism NI or The Irish Government on plans for the European Year of the Normans.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the monitoring and evaluation structures which have been put in place in relation to the Sandy Row revitalisation scheme.
answer_text: The monitoring and evaluation structures in place for the Sandy Row revitalisation scheme are those set out in the Departmental guidance for all grant funded schemes.  The Performance Output Measures as set out in the Contract for Funding are monitored by my department through the completion of a quarterly monitoring form and/or monitoring visit.  A project evaluation will be undertaken within 12 months following the completion of the scheme in line with project evaluation management guidance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail how many civil servants, on a full-time equivalent basis are currently accommodated in modular or mobile office space within the Northern Ireland Civil Service.
answer_text: My Department currently has 6 mobile or modular buildings used to accommodate staff.  DoF does not hold details of the number of civil servants that are accommodated in these buildings however it is likely to be a small proportion of staff. This number will reduce further as my Department continues to phase out these buildings under the NICS Office Estate Strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the road works at Knockbracken Healthcare Park will conclude.
answer_text: The developer led road improvement scheme at the junction of Saintfield Road will provide significant road safety and traffic progression improvements by widening both the northbound and southbound A24 Saintfield Road carriageway and provide improvements to the signal-controlled junctions at the Old Saintfield Road and Knockbracken Road.The scheme is currently progressing in line with the programme and it is anticipated that it will be completed the end of July 2025 subject to favourable weather conditions.My officials will keep the public informed of any change.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline any work his Department has undertaken to review bullying policies at its arm's-length bodies.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the steps being taken to find an alternative to the use of pesticides on the transport networks, as part of the All-Island Pollinator Plan.
answer_text: My Department supports the All-Ireland Pollinator Plan through ongoing actions aimed at phasing out pesticide use on transport networks. Officials are taking this forward with their counterparts in DAERA aligning with the technical guidelines for maintaining roadsides for pollinators and participating in the Pollinator Monitoring Scheme.My Department continues to implement and promote its policy on roadside verge management (grass cutting), placing a greater emphasis on environmental protection and enhancement.  The new policy means reducing grass cutting in order to increase the environmental value of road verges by promoting rewilding and improving the available biodiversity resource. The policy was reviewed and introduced in 2022 with support from Ulster Wildlife https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/sites/default/files/publications/infrastructure/verge-management-%28grass-cutting%29-dem-193-22.pdf During the last 6 years, Translink has decreased their use of pesticides through the reduction in areas requiring treatment and the use of new droplet technology which greatly reduces drift. Pesticides are used only where absolutely necessary to restrict vegetation growth and to ensure safety which typically applies to immediate ballasted tracks, designated walkways and also on occasion spraying to combat the spread of non-native invasive species.Alternative treatment options like the use mechanical weed control, biological controls and organic mulches amongst others are reviewed on an ongoing basis as part of my Department’s drive for continuous improvement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for a progress report on the (i) short term; (ii) medium term; and (iii) long term, delivery of the Belfast Cycle Network Delivery Plan 2022-31 scheme.
answer_text: Significant progress has been made on the majority of short term schemes in the Belfast Cycling Network Delivery Plan.  Street lighting has been provided on the urban section of Comber Greenway and sections of Forth Meadow Greenway and Lagan Gateway have been delivered in partnership with Belfast City Council. The scheme on Lagmore Avenue is now in construction, and West Belfast Greenway Phase 1a is being programmed to commence in early Autumn. Work is also almost complete on the upgrade to the existing facilities on Stranmillis Embankment.Design is substantially complete on the Montgomery Road, Sydenham Greenway Phase 1, Island Street, and Durham Street schemes, all of which are currently at varying stages of the statutory process related to legislating the proposed cycle routes before construction can commence.Design is ongoing, or in preparation to commence, on a further eight schemes, and officials are working hard to bring as many of these forward as possible for public engagement later this year.  Future schemes will be progressed with consideration to the emerging Eastern Transport Plan and other significant changes that have occurred in Belfast in recent years, not least the relocation of Ulster University and the increased numbers of students now living in the city centre.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on implementing recommendations from the Stranded Dreams report.
answer_text: The ‘Stranded Dreams’ report highlights important perspectives of young asylum seekers and refugees on the importance of English language skills and education in supporting them to integrate into society here. The report sets out a range of proposed actions for the Executive and Educators to consider. My Department is currently undertaking work to develop an English for Speakers of Other Languages policy to align with the Refugee Integration Strategy. The policy aims to support asylum seekers and refugees to acquire English language skills as quickly as possible, to enable their progression in education and ultimately employment. This ongoing departmental work will help to support the proposals set out in the Stranded Dreams report for more efficient and effective pathways to English language skills.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer (i) AQW 26970/22-27; and (ii) AQW 26969/22-27.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 27288/22-27, when her officials will finish appraising the consultation responses.
answer_text: I can advise that officials have recently received the results of a further consultation from Belfast City Council. The results of the survey will be considered in the coming weeks, as resources permit, to determine the way forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she has (i) any departmental role in the operation of the Legal Services Oversight Commissioner; and (ii) been briefed on the role.
answer_text: Policy responsibility for the establishment and operation of the office of the Legal Services Oversight Commissioner currently vests with the Department of Finance.  While not responsible for this policy area my Department will take account of the role when considering the existing regulatory framework and how current practice can be enhanced to provide greater assurance on the delivery of publicly funded legal services.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 27063/22-27, to provide a timeline of when his draft Departmental response will be brought to the Executive.
answer_text: Pursuant to AQW 27063/22-27, my officials are currently finalising the draft response.  I intend to bring this to my Executive colleagues in the coming weeks.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the roadworks on the Saintfield Road, at its junction with the Knockbracken Road, will be completed.
answer_text: The developer led road improvement scheme at the junction of Saintfield Road will provide significant road safety and traffic progression improvements by widening both the northbound and southbound A24 Saintfield Road carriageway and provide improvements to the signal-controlled junctions at the Old Saintfield Road and Knockbracken RoadThe scheme is currently progressing in line with the programme and it is anticipated that it will be completed the end of July 2025 subject to favourable weather conditions.However, the work is subject to favourable weather conditions. My officials will keep the public informed of any change.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail if discussions are continuing on funding for the PSNI recovery plan and the proposal to increase police officer numbers to 7000 by 2028.
answer_text: My Department has completed its review of the PSNI Workforce Recovery Outline Business Case and is satisfied with the case made, subject to affordability. I will be meeting the Justice Minister to discuss this matter. Given the wider budgetary implications of the proposed increase in police officer numbers at an estimated cost of £206 million over five years, this will be a matter that the Executive will need to consider.  In my view, this is best done in the context of discussions around the upcoming multi-year Budget, which will follow the announcement of the Spending Review outcome.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department has made any plans to participate in the European Year of the Normans 2027.
answer_text: My Department's Historic Environment Division has drafted a programme relating to our relevant State Care Monuments for potential inclusion in European Year of the Normans 2027. This potentially includes a family fun day at Dundrum Castle, walk and talks led by archaeologists at State Care sites; and pop-up exhibitions [all subject to resource]. Potential larger projects would require more significant dedicated staffing resource and funding. A key output from European Year of the Normans 2027 may be the inclusion of a number of our relevant State Care Monuments in any resultant European Cultural Route.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when their Department will publish the Together: Building a United Community T:BUC review.
answer_text: We are currently considering recommendations from the T:BUC Review and these will shape the timeline for the development of a refreshed approach to Good Relations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount her Department has paid to Amey Consultancy in relation to (i) active travel; and (ii) road traffic schemes, since 2022.
answer_text: My Department’s expenditure paid to Amey Consultancy from the 2022/23 financial year to the end of 2024/25 financial year (2024/25 figures are provisional) is;approximately £3.5m relating to spend on active travel that also includes development work on Lagan Footbridge and Strabane Footbridge.approximately £7.6m relating to road schemes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount spent to date on the Knockbreda Road pedestrian crossing, Belfast.
answer_text: I can confirm that the total costs for Knockbreda Road pedestrian crossing at present are £16,996.51. This includes £15,488.47 in consultant fees for design work and safety audits and £1,508.04 for the NIE connection

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department has made a 2025-26 allocation to the Housing Executive for the Social Housing Development Programme.
To ask the Minister for Communities whether his department has made a 2025-26 allocation to the Housing Executive for the Social Housing Development Programme.
answer_text: As part of the Executive’s 2025-26 opening allocation, my Department received a specific allocation of £100m for social housing. This will be used to meet inescapable new build housing commitments from previous years. As detailed in the DfC budget 2025-26 Equality Impact Assessment, in addition to the £100m RRI, I have allocated £63.0m to New Build Social Homes. This allocation will allow in the region of 900- 1,000 New Build Social Home starts in 2025-26. It is recognised that this is still significantly short of the target of 2,000 and a bid will be submitted in-year to the Executive to address the shortfall.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to list the most recent percentage of Full-Time Equivalent days spent working from home by staff across the Northern Ireland Civil Service, measured on an annual basis.
answer_text: I refer the member to the response provided to AQW 15617/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether the 2022 guidance on protecting biodiversity in grass verges remains in operation.
answer_text: My Department maintains approximately 45,000km of grass verges on the road network and our approach aims to maintain the right balance between road safety and the control of grass and noxious weeds, while placing an emphasis on environmental protection and enhancement. In December 2022 and to strengthen our approach, my Department issued a new policy on verge management across the road network to protect the environment.  The policy remains in operation and increases the Department’s focus on protecting wildlife and promoting biodiversity when managing roadside verges and supports the actions needed to comply with the Climate Change Act (NI) 2022.In compliance with the policy, a single swathe is cut along verges on the strategic road network, on heavier trafficked rural roads and at sight lines and junctions twice per year for road safety purposes.  On lighter trafficked roads the cutting frequency is reduced to once per year and is scheduled towards the end of the growing season.  My Department does not undertake cutting to facilitate private or commercial entrances.We are working with local Councils to further increase the number of ‘Don’t Mow Let It Grow’ sites and I am keen that my Department takes forward biodiversity enhancement projects, such as rewilding, tree planting and the creation wildflower meadows, on the road network to promote pollinator friendly management of our soft estate and improve the available biodiversity resource.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline (i) the Arts Council of Northern Ireland budget; and (ii) any other core arts funding, provided by his Department in each of the past ten years, broken down by (a) Capital DEL and (b) Recourse DEL.
answer_text: The Arts Council distributes core arts funding on behalf of my Department.  The total amount of funding the Arts Council has received in each of the past ten years is as follows:YearResourceCapitalTotal2015-16  £10,852.00£115,000£10,967,0002016-17£10,718,000£363,000£11,081,0002017-18£10,620,000£643,000£11,263,0002018-19£10,472,000£1,703,000£12,175,0002019-20£11,620,000£1,740,000£13,360,0002020-21£34,765,000£2,615,000£37,380,0002021-22£16,157,000£1,592,000£17,749,0002022-23£12,369,000£1,321,000£13,690,0002023-24£11,804,338£1,300,000£13,104,3382024-25£12,469,000£1,303,000£13,772,000							This includes all funding provided to Arts Council by my Department, including for Covid support schemes. It does not include the National Lottery money disbursed by the Arts Council. The indicative opening budget allocation 2025-26 for the Arts Council is £10,292,000 resource and £1,405,000 capital.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether any Artificial Intelligence tools were used in the production of the incorrect Land and Property Services rates demand letters issued in Spring 2025.
answer_text: ANSWERArtificial Intelligence tools were not used in the preparation or issuance of rate bills.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she made a bid for funding in relation to the All-Island Strategic Rail Review for the 2025-26 Budget.
answer_text: As highlighted in AQW 23473 22-27, the Department continues to work with our colleagues in the Department of Transport, supported by the European Investment Bank (EIB), to consider the recommendations in the Review and identify those rail interventions which could be well advanced or delivered over the next decade to 2035.  The outcomes from this EIB work will help inform which projects to prioritise.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether he will publish multi-year budgets for Executive departments following the Comprehensive Spending Review.
answer_text: Work is already underway within my department in laying the groundwork for multi-year budgets including the Budget Sustainability Team supporting departments to develop their five-year budget plans.  Following the outcome of Spending Review announcement on 11 June 2025, my officials will commission a multi-year budget information gathering exercise across all NICS departments.This information, along with departments 5-year budget plans will form the basis of the recommendations that I will bring to the Executive in the Autumn covering a three-year Resource and four-year Capital budget period in line with the Spending Review.Subject to Executive agreement, I intend to publish a draft multi-year budget, and following a full 12-week consultation, seek Executive agreement for a final multi-year budget in advance of the new financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail whether the £105.7 million Reinvestment and Reform Initiative Borrowing for NI Water outlined in Budget Table 4.4 Capital DEL earmarked allocations is incorporated in the £335 million allocated to water and sewerage in the Department for Infrastructure's Capital DEL budget.
answer_text: I have not taken any decisions on the 2025-26 Budget. I will consider allocations in line with my priorities, the wider Executive PfG commitments and equality considerations, following closure of my Department’s Equality Impact Assessment consultation on 5 June 2025.I can advise that the £105.7m Reinvestment and Reform Initiative Borrowing for NI Water is included in the figures totalling £335m under ‘Water and Sewerage’ in the Main Estimates.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to confirm the percentage increase in Capital DEL allocation for waste-water investment between 2024-25 and 2025-26.
answer_text: My Department allocates Capital DEL funding to NI Water for all investment in water and sewerage services, with the split of this between water and sewerage services generally being an operational matter for NI Water. My Department provided NI Water with a Capital DEL allocation of £349.1m in 2024-25, which was over 40% of my Department’s final Capital DEL budget. This included an in-year ring-fenced funding of £19.5m to fund targeted action to release wastewater constraints, unlocking 2,300 new properties.  I have not taken decisions on my Department’s Budget 2025-26 outcome as these are subject to equality considerations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether a text of the draft Anti-Poverty Strategy was circulated to the Executive in advance of the meeting on 15 May, or subsequently.
answer_text: I circulated a paper to my Executive Colleagues on 31 March seeking agreement on the content of the draft Executive Anti-Poverty Strategy. This paper included a draft Anti-Poverty Strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any consideration he has given to issuing guidance to local councils in relation to the prioritisation of planning applications that (i) exist as part of a Disabled Facilities Grant application; and (ii) relate to household adaptations connected to disability.
answer_text: (i) My department, along with officials from the Department of Health and the Housing Executive are taking forward a review of (NIHE-administered) private sector grants, including the Disabled Facilities Grant. One of the outcomes of the review is to make grant funding quicker to access.   (ii) The Housing Executive have confirmed that they are not experiencing any difficulty with planning in respect of major adaptations to their stock.How quickly planning applications are assessed is an operational matter for the eleven District Councils, with the Department for Infrastructure responsible for the NI planning system including relevant legislation, regional policies and strategies and supporting practical guidance.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail how many public appointments have been made during the period in which the role of Commissioner of Public Appointments has been vacant, which would otherwise have been subject to scrutiny by the Commissioner.
answer_text: The last Commissioner for Public Appointments NI (CPANI) was in post until May 2021. Since that time, 10 CPANI regulated public appointments have been taken forward by The Executive Office. Regulated public appointments are conducted in line with the CPANI Code of Practice for Ministerial Appointments. A new CPANI has now been appointed. Claire Keatinge took up the role on 22nd August 2025

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline any costs related to the rebuild of the wall which collapsed at Mount Vernon Park during Storm Darragh, borne by either (i) the Department for Communities; (ii) the Northern Ireland Housing Executive (NIHE), or (iii) any organisation funded by the Department for Communities or the NIHE.
answer_text: None None   None

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she is aware of any concerns being raised over the North South Interconnector travelling over ground.
answer_text: Planning permission was granted for the North South Interconnector in September 2020.  I am aware that multiple objections and representations were raised to the proposal during the processing of the planning applications and that a public local inquiry was held in June 2016 and February 2017 to discuss issues of concern, including the option of undergrounding.  These issues were reported on by the Planning Appeals Commission and were known to the then Infrastructure Minister, Nicola Mallon in making her determination of the applications. My department is legally obligated to comply with the determination made that that time by Minister Mallon.More recently, a challenge to the discharge of several planning conditions has been raised through the Courts.  These proceedings are ongoing.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail how many constituents in Belfast South and Mid Down are currently waiting for (i) a Disabled Facilities Grant application decision; and (ii) a planning approval for a household adaptation related to disability.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has provided the following information:“There is currently one case in Belfast South and Mid Down constituency where a full Disabled Facilities Grant application, with supporting documents, has been received that is waiting for a decision on the application.”Neither the Department nor NIHE hold this information.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the safety assessments her Department conducted when reaching the decision to delay the next safety inspection of the (i) Ravara Road, Ballygowan, and (ii) Ballycloughan Road, Saintfield, until October 2025, given her Department advises this road should be inspected every six months and that the last inspection took place in May 2024.
answer_text: My officials reached this decision due to staff shortages in the division.Ravara Road, Ballygowan, and Ballycloughan Road, Saintfield both underwent comprehensive safety inspections in May 2024, October 2024 and May 2025 during which actionable defects, in accordance with my Department’s Limited Service policy, were identified and instructed for repair.While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.In the interim, officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health which organisations have requested to make use of lands at Knockbracken Healthcare Park in the last twelve months.
answer_text: Knockbracken Healthcare Park comprises land owned by the Department and the Belfast HSC Trust. The Department is not aware of any formal request to make use of the DoH owned land in the last twelve months.  The Department is currently engaged in a process of pre-disposal due diligence ahead of formally disposing of its surplus land in accordance with the Land and Property Services (LPS) guidance for disposal of public sector property in Northern Ireland. Given the significant size and strategic location of this site, the Department is collaborating with several central and local Government partners to determine the best way to proceed with the disposal process. Early engagement with the Asset Management Forum chaired by the LPS has identified potential alternative public sector interests/uses for this site.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the delivery of an overground North South Interconnector as per planning approvals.
answer_text: The then Infrastructure Minister granted planning permission for the northern section of the Interconnector on 14 September 2020. NIE Networks is required to comply with this planning permission and it is doing so.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the current discrepancy between financial support in place for final year medical students from Northern Ireland studying in England compared with the financial support available to English students studying in England through NHS England.
answer_text: Health and Student Finance are devolved matters. It is therefore up to the Northern Ireland Executive to determine the support provided to NI-domiciled medical students studying at university in either Northern Ireland or Great Britain. Policy reflects the resources available to each administration and their assessment of workforce supply priorities.The support arrangements vary markedly across the UK depending upon where the individual is ordinarily resident. For example, English medical students pay significantly higher tuition fees overall towards their primary degrees than their Northern Ireland peers.  My Department currently pays tuition fees for final year Northern Ireland domiciled students undertaking a primary medical degree at a UK Medical School. In addition, students may also be eligible for a means tested bursary, which is household income assessed.My officials are reviewing the breadth of current funding provided in support of healthcare related courses which lead to a career within HSC. This review is intended to inform options for a public consultation. I would hope to be in a position to launch that consultation in late summer/early autumn.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she has made any representation, written or verbal, asking for the North South Interconnector to be underground.
answer_text: The planning permission, which was granted by the then Infrastructure Minister, Nichola Mallon in 2020 is legally binding on me and my department. As Minister, I do not have any discretion in relation to this.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the disposal of lands at Knockbracken Healthcare Park.
answer_text: Knockbracken Healthcare Park comprises land owned by the Department and the Belfast HSC Trust. The Department is not aware of any formal request to make use of the DoH owned land in the last twelve months.  The Department is currently engaged in a process of pre-disposal due diligence ahead of formally disposing of its surplus land in accordance with the Land and Property Services (LPS) guidance for disposal of public sector property in Northern Ireland. Given the significant size and strategic location of this site, the Department is collaborating with several central and local Government partners to determine the best way to proceed with the disposal process. Early engagement with the Asset Management Forum chaired by the LPS has identified potential alternative public sector interests/uses for this site.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she has asked for any monitoring of the impact of the electronic travel authorisation scheme on tourism in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: We will continue to monitor the impact of the Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) Scheme on the industry here. My Department and officials from Tourism Ireland and Tourism NI have been working alongside industry representatives and key stakeholders regarding the potential impact on our tourism sector from the introduction of the ETA Scheme. This has included close engagement and discussions with representative bodies including the Northern Ireland Tourism Alliance (NITA) and the Inbound Tour Operators Association (ITOA).The implementation of the ETA scheme is already having a damaging impact on the local tourism sector. NITA is reporting the impacts on its members, especially tour operators, with tours and trips being cancelled and the North being removed from itineraries due to the requirement for visitors to have an ETA. NITA and ITOA are jointly working with other industry stakeholders to collect and collate data to further assess the impact that the requirement for an ETA is having on businesses offering travel to the North. This will add to its evidence base to support its call for an exemption for tourists travelling from the South into the North. In addition, Tourism Ireland will continue to monitor key overseas markets in relation to ETA.  This includes a Sentiment Tracker Survey which indicated that 1 in 5 people (coming from North America) and 1 in 4 (coming from Europe) are likely not to travel due to an ETA.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for a progress update on the Knockbreda Road pedestrian crossing, including an indicative date for installation.
answer_text: I can confirm that as of 12 May 2025 my officials received the data from the consultation carried out by Belfast City Council. Officials will appraise the data from this further consultation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 24329/22-27, to detail when she intends to make any decisions relating to her 2025-26 departmental budget.
answer_text: I intend to make decisions relating to my Department’s 2025-26 Budget after I have considered the responses to my Department’s Draft Budget 2025-26 Equality Impact Assessment consultation which closes on 5 June 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his TransformED NI programme has ever been discussed by the Executive
answer_text: TransformED has not been discussed by the Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether the main rates of reoffending have fallen or risen since the devolution of policing and justice powers.
answer_text: The Department does not retain reoffending data for the period prior to devolution.  Difficulties in accessing information, changes to recording systems and technical problems in merging information from relevant legacy systems have resulted in a lack of available information prior to this.Therefore, comparisons are only available between the first available cohort in 2010/11 and the most recent cohort for 2021/22 and these have recently been provided to you in response to AQW 23815 22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on the Rethinking Our Resources: Measures for Climate Action and a Circular Economy in NI Consultation.
answer_text: Analysis of responses to the Rethinking Our Resources consultation has helped inform a draft Departmental response, which my officials are currently finalising.It is my intention to bring this to my Executive colleagues to seek their support in moving forward together to not only meet our legal targets for recycling, but to also help drive green growth and develop our circular economy in Northern Ireland.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether the Environmental Governance Review Panel has provided an interim report to his Department.
answer_text: The independent panel leading the Review of Environmental Governance in Northern Ireland recently provided me with an update on their progress.   		Over the coming weeks the independent panel will be working towards finalising the interim report with a view to delivering their full report in summer 2025.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail: (i) how many Full-Time Equivalent staff are working on the TransformED programme; and (ii) the cost of these staff.
answer_text: The current staffing numbers for the TransformED Team is one Deputy Principal. The cost of this member of staff is £44,338.00There are also a range of staff within other teams working on actions from the TransformED Delivery Plan as part of their duties.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, pursuant to AQW 24415/22-27, whether she intends to bring forward any policy proposals to address Strategic Lawsuits Against Public Participation in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I have no plans at present to bring forward any policy proposals to address strategic lawsuits against public participation in Northern Ireland, but my Department is keeping the matter under review alongside other departments with a relevant interest.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current provision for parenteral nutrition in paediatrics including (i) the number of specialist (a) nurses; (b) consultants; (c) pharmacists; and (d) dietitians, currently employed; and (ii) whether this aligns with the British Association for Parenteral and Enteral Nutrition guidelines on the provision of care.
answer_text: The paediatric parenteral nutrition (PN) service is only provided at Belfast HSC Trust as part of the Regional Paediatric Gastroenterology Service.The staff as shown in Table 1 are involved in the provision for parenteral nutrition however for the majority of these staff, it is only part of their overall role in providing paediatric gastroenterology care.Table 1 Staffing (WTE)Paediatrics  (a) Specialist Nurses2.5(b) Consultants3(c) Pharmacists0.8(d) Dietitians2.2  Total8.5The British Association for Parenteral and Enteral Nutrition (BAPEN) does not make staffing level recommendations for children.  BAPEN, however, recommends a nutritional support team should manage complex nutrition support for patients including those on Parenteral Nutrition.  It recommends a multi-disciplinary team that should include a Dietitian, Consultant, Pharmacist and Nutrition nurse. Belfast Trust Nutritional team complies with this recommendation with a multi-disciplinary team of consultants, specialist dietitians, nutrition nurses, pharmacy and medical members.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to confirm whether he held any discussions with the Economy Minister on the issue of University funding prior to Wednesday 7 May 2025.
answer_text: ANSWERWe are fortunate to have excellent universities here. I have met with local universities on a number of occasions since taking up office. I have had a number of engagements with the Economy Minister to discuss a range of issues including discussions on university funding prior to Wednesday 7 May 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current provision for parenteral nutrition for adults including (i) the number of specialist (a) nurses; (b) consultants; (c) pharmacists; and (d) dietitians, currently employed; and (ii) whether this aligns with the British Association for Parenteral and Enteral Nutrition guidelines on the provision of care.
answer_text: Parental Nutrition (PN) is a way to receive nutrients by bypassing the digestive system.  It is administered intravenously either temporarily or permanently when a patient’s condition means they cannot use their digestive system.  Parenteral nutrition can be supplementary (partial or complete (total parenteral nutrition/TPN).Staffing at Belfast HSC TrustTable 1 Belfast HSC TrustStaffing (WTE)Adults  (a) Specialist Nurses0(b) Consultants0.5 (*GI)(c) Pharmacists0(d) Dietitians0  Total0.5*GastroenterologyThe staff as shown in table 1 are involved in the provision for parenteral nutrition however for the majority of these Belfast Trust staff, it is not the sole focus of their role.Belfast Health and Social Care Trust manages Types 1, 2 and 3 Intestinal Failure and Parenteral Nutrition in Adults. The Belfast Trust has an integrated team for Intestinal Failure (incorporating Home PN) and Parenteral Nutrition with all members crossing both areas.  It is difficult to separate the two service resources but the team comprises:Medical		 	0.5 WTE Dietetics			4.4 WTENursing			5.0 WTEClinical Psychologist 	0.5 WTEPharmacy			1.0 WTE220 Patients attend weekly Intestinal Failure clinics including 80 Home Parenteral Support patients.Nursing staff for adult services are part of General Surgery inpatient services at Belfast City Hospital.(i) 	Staffing at Northern HSC TrustIn NHSCT there are no Consultants, Specialist Nurses, Dietitians or Pharmacists that solely work in Parenteral and Enteral Nutrition.  At Antrim Hospital there is a Parenteral Nutrition Ward Round for adults twice a week but there is no dedicated team for PN within the hospital setting.  There is no MDT ward round for those receiving PN on the Causeway hospital site.For adult care, the current pharmacist provision is funded within the job role of the critical care clinical pharmacist(s) and specialist technical services team for the aseptic dispensing of parenteral nutrition.  There are no guidelines that describe the specific pharmacist resource required for provision of parenteral nutrition.A 0.6 WTE dietitian covers PN and attends ward round as part of their caseloadStaffing at Southern HSC TrustTable 2Southern HSC TrustStaffing (WTE)Adults  (a) Specialist Nurses 0(b) Consultants0.02(c) Pharmacists 0.72(d) Dietitians 1.0  Total1.74The Southern HSC Trust has no dedicated Parenteral Nutrition (PN) team.  In adult services, there are 2 Consultant Medical staff (a Consultant Chemical Pathologist and a Consultant Intensivist) who contribute to the multi-disciplinary Nutrition support team within the Trust although one post is vacant (from May 2025) and is currently undergoing recruitment.  Both Consultant Medical Staff are involved in a once weekly multidisciplinary ward round which goes around all the patients on parenteral nutrition in the wards on the Craigavon Area Hospital site and also provides a virtual (telephone advice) service to Daisy Hill Hospital as required (very rarely nowadays).

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department will reduce the speed limit on the Saintfield Road, between the junction of Mealough Road and Carryduff roundabout.
answer_text: My officials have carried out a speed limit review of this location in line with our policy.The assessment considered evidence such as the speed of existing traffic, the road traffic collision data and the degree of frontage development. It indicated that the existing 50mph speed limit is appropriate for this section of road at this time. Consequently, we have no plans to reduce the speed limit at this location

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when the drafting of the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill will begin.
answer_text: My Department has begun to instruct the Office of Legislative Counsel on the drafting of the Bill.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline what steps he is taking to provide NI Water with multi-year funding.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail how much funding has been provided by the Executive Office to Ballymac Friendship Centre, in each of the past five years.
answer_text: Funding provided by The Executive Office to Ballymac Friendship Trust from 1st April 2020 to 7th May 2025 in each of the past five years was as follows:2020/2021£98,7062021/2022£113,6472022/2023£156,6402023/2024£78,5772024/2025£33,702Total£481,272The Department of Finance Fraud Investigation Service (FIS) has investigated allegations concerning the Ballymac Friendship Centre. A full brief of the case including all the allegations and FIS enquiries and findings is currently with the PSNI Economic Crime Unit for assessment. question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she has sought a briefing on progress in the investigation into the murder of Martin O'Hagan.
answer_text: I have not sought briefing on this matter. I am conscious that there is an open investigation into Mr O’Hagan’s murder, as well as an ongoing investigation linked to the murder being carried out by the Office of the Police Ombudsman (OPONI). I am committed to respecting the operational independence of both the PSNI and OPONI, therefore, it would not be appropriate for me to comment on or otherwise involve myself in this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement she had with stakeholders in relation to her Good Jobs Employments Rights Bill proposals prior to her statement on Monday 28 April 2025.
answer_text: In the week I made my announcement, I met twice with business, trade union and other stakeholders to update them on my proposals for the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill. My predecessor Conor Murphy also engaged extensively on the proposals with business, trade union and other stakeholders, across multiple sectors, including retail, hospitality, manufacturing and the voluntary and community sector. He met them initially in February 2024, and during the consultation period. Both he and I have met frequently with stakeholders to discuss wider Departmental issues, including these proposals. All stakeholders had the opportunity to respond to the extended consultation held by my Department.  91 responses were received from businesses and their representative organisations, including 25 industry representative bodies with a combined membership of over 18,000 individuals and businesses. Submissions were received from 19 trade union bodies which together have a membership of around 375,000 workers.  My officials also met with numerous business, trade union and other stakeholders, before, during and following the consultation.  Over 30 events took place, with hundreds of people attending. As we move forward to refine my proposals, I want to build on that constructive engagement. I am happy to continue to meet with all stakeholders to discuss this important work.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) the number of parking spaces at the Agri-Food and Biosciences Institute (AFBI) on Newforge Lane; (ii) the average number of staff who access the AFBI site at Newforge Lane on a weekly basis; (iii) the average number of vehicles that access the site on a daily basis; and (iv) the total number of visitors who have accessed the site in 2024.
answer_text: I am advised by the AFBI chief executive that: there are 238 parking spaces at the Agri-Food and Biosciences Institute (AFBI) on Newforge Lane;the number of AFBI staff with Newforge as their place of work is 276 however AFBI staff operate under a hybrid working policy so on any one day there will not be this number of staff coming on site;the average number of vehicles that access the site on a daily basis is circa 100 to 130; andthe total number of visitors who accessed the site in 2024 is circa 400.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether the (i) First Minister; and (ii) deputy First Minister has stayed in a five-star hotel while on a Ministerial visit during the 2022-2027 Assembly mandate.
answer_text: Ministers stay in a various types of accommodation depending on price, availability and business need.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in relation to the Stranmillis embankment cycling lane, whether ring-fenced active travel money was spent on road resurfacing on the adjacent carriageway.
answer_text: Active Travel funding has not been spent on carriageway resurfacing recently completed on Stranmillis Embankment between Ormeau Road and Southview cottages.The condition of the carriageway adjacent to the active travel improvements at the location was such that it had been prioritised for resurfacing and a decision was taken to undertake this work at the same time as the pedestrian and cycling improvement to make best use of available resources and minimise disruption to road users. The carriageway resurfacing element of the works was funded from my department’s Structural Maintenance budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether JobStart 50+ will be funded in the 2025-26 budget.
answer_text: The JobStart 50+ Scheme was funded by HM Treasury’s Labour Market Evaluations and Pilots fund (LMEP) as a test and learn pilot scheme. Unfortunately, budget for this Treasury funded pilot programme has not been recurrent in 2025-26. I am in the process of agreeing my Department’s 2025-26 Budget allocations. In allocating my Budget, one of my priorities is employment support, to help people overcome barriers faced in moving into, retaining and progressing in employment.  My Budget allocation plans will be announced in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether any services have been procured from Palantir by the Northern Ireland Civil Service.
answer_text: ANSWERAccording to records held in eTendersNI, no services have been procured from this company by a Civil Service Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he plans to keep Purdysburn Ambulance Station open.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Ambulance Service (NIAS) Purdysburn Ambulance Station, located within Knockbracken Healthcare Park, is on land owned by my Department. Currently this land is under review, to determine if it is surplus to requirements.This review is considered appropriate given the current condition of the facilities at the Purdysburn site. The existing infrastructure does not provide adequate accommodation for NIAS staff due to the deteriorated condition of the building. Recent assessments, following storm damage, have deemed the structure unsafe. Given its age and condition, substantial capital investment would be required to refurbish it to acceptable standards.At present, there is no established timeline for the relocation of Purdysburn Ambulance Station. NIAS is actively seeking suitable alternative locations. It is of paramount importance that any relocation does not detrimentally impact NIAS response times, and ideally the new location should potentially enhance NIAS response capabilities while also providing superior quality accommodation for NIAS staff.Once potential alternatives have been thoroughly assessed, consultations will be conducted with NIAS staff, their representatives, local elected officials, and the public as appropriate. My primary objective remains the provision of efficient and effective ambulance services, ensuring the safety and well-being of both our ambulance service staff and the communities they serve.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on road safety improvements at St Ita's Primary School, Belfast.
answer_text: Design work on the scheme is complete, and construction will be programmed with the department’s term contractor. Subject to contractor availability, we expect work to take place during the summer holidays, although confirmation is reliant on final agreement on a small number of land issues.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 14447/22-27, to detail any plans his Department is making to improve the current provision for adoptees to access healthcare records pertaining to their birth parents and any medical conditions that might impact them.
answer_text: To assist those who have been adopted to access their adoption record, including any information it contains relating to the health records of birth parents, my Department issued ‘Access to Birth Records and Adoption Agency Case Records for Adults Impacted by Adoption to Health and Social Care Trusts and Adoption Agencies – Practice Guidance’ (https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/publications/access-birth-records-and-adoption-agency-records-adults-impacted-adoption-practice-guidance) in November 2023.This guidance is currently being reviewed, and it is anticipated that more emphasis will be given to encouraging birth families to provide as much information as possible at the time of the adoption.  It is also intended to include further guidance for adoption agencies which are contacted by birth family members, to encourage those individuals to consider sharing medical information not known at the time of the adoption.  Collectively, it is anticipated that these will improve the health information accessible by adoptees.In addition, through the ongoing development and implementation of a digital health and care record system, the Department is working to ensure that a continuous record of care both pre- and post-adoption is maintained, while at the same time ensuring that all necessary steps are taken to respect the privacy of the adopted person and their family.  Furthermore, The Adoption and Children Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 introduces a new legal framework for disclosure of information. When implemented, it will provide for a new and more consistent approach to access to information held in adoption agency records and by the Registrar General about adoptions which take place after the Act comes into operation. For adoptions, which took place before the relevant provisions of the Act come into operation, it will introduce intermediary services to assist adopted adults and their birth parents to obtain information about their adoption (including health information) and to facilitate contact between them and birth relatives. Work is ongoing to implement the provisions in the Act on a phased basis, and new regulations will be subject to consultation. In the interim, the current Adoption Agencies Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1989 will continue to apply, which provide adoption agencies with the discretion to disclose information, as appropriate, in accordance with the published practice guidance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any steps her Department is taking to help alleviate residents' parking concerns on Ulsterville Avenue, Belfast.
answer_text: I have asked my officials to expedite the development of an application and assessment process for residents’ parking schemes to ensure that any requests can be appropriately considered. This process will take into account the findings and recommendations of the report and will provide a streamlined approach for considering, assessing and prioritising requests going forward. It is my intention to make an announcement over the coming months as to how future schemes will be assessed by my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the steps he is taking to reduce the number of people contracting tuberculosis.
answer_text: While the overall rate of tuberculosis (TB) remains low in Northern Ireland (4.5/100,000 population in 2024; 86 cases), there is evidence of an upward trend in cases since the pandemic (2.7/100,000 population in 2022, 70 cases). We need to remain vigilant to the serious risks it can pose. The UK has signed up to the World Health Organisation (WHO) TB elimination targets by 2035, committing to support an annual reduction in TB incidence and transmission.To help achieve this, my Department is working with the Public Health Agency, HSC Trusts and other partner organisations to improve prevention:through increased awareness amongst the public and health professionals;testing individuals from high-risk groups for latent TB infection;optimising BCG vaccine uptake for eligible individuals; detection and control of TB, through optimising timely diagnosis and treatment; and contract tracing to rapidly identify and treat contacts of cases and interrupt ongoing transmission, especially for those at higher risk of getting TB.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he plans to review the eligibility criteria for the (i) BCG; and (ii) pneumococcal vaccines.
answer_text: My Department follows the recommendations for eligibility as set out by the Joint Committee on Vaccination and Immunisation (JCVI), an independent, expert scientific advisory committee which advises the 4 UK Health Departments on all vaccination and immunisation matters.I am not aware of any plans by JCVI to review the eligibility criteria for the BCG or pneumococcal vaccination programmes. Should JCVI recommendations change, my Department will consider their updated advice.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any plans to introduce scalp cooling treatments for patients receiving chemotherapy.
answer_text: Cold caps/scalp cooling treatments are not routinely commissioned in HSC NI.  I am advised that they are not part of the NICE recommended care pathway for treatment of chemotherapy/SACT-induced alopecia hair loss.  The routine commissioning of this treatment can only be considered based on the long-term evidence of efficacy, available funding and the relative priority of this service in the context of other service pressures and service development proposals, and the overall HSC financial position.The commissioning position on Scalp Cooling can be reviewed on receipt of any emerging UK evidence review and provided this is in line with NICE recommendations for cancer patients.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the Women's Health Action Plan.
answer_text: Work to develop a Women’s Health Action Plan is ongoing.  My Department recently hosted a number of online engagement events with women's representative groups and healthcare professionals and will be reviewing the input received over the coming weeks to help inform the priorities in the action plan.  My Department also provided funding to Derry Well Women, who in partnership with Queen’s University Belfast, developed a Northern Ireland Women’s Health Survey as Phase 1 of a large-scale public listening exercise with women.  I launched this survey in November 2024 with the aim of hearing directly from women about their health priorities and barriers to accessing services.  The survey closed on 31 March 2025.  The Department will consider the early findings from this exercise as work develops on the Action Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail his Department's actions for supporting women who have been impacted by vaginal mesh complications.
answer_text: The Department of Health have recurrently funded the Regional Uro-Gynae Mesh Centre by £516,000 per annum. This service delivers:A surgical pathway involving outpatient clinics and inpatient theatre lists with input from Consultants in Uro-Gynae, Urology, Plastics and Colo-Rectal.A non-surgical pathway involving a multi-disciplinary team with input from Physiotherapy, Psychology and Consultant Anaesthetists in the Pain Service.To reduce waiting times in this specialist service the Department of Health, through the Strategic Planning and Performance Group, have offered non-recurrent funding to deliver increased capacity throughout 2025/2026 and they are working this through with colleagues in BHSCT.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the outpatient waiting list for vaginal mesh removal.
answer_text: The following information has been provided by Belfast Health and Social Care Trust.At present there are 52 patients on the New Outpatient Waiting List, (with the longest waiter as at February 2024).  The waiting time is approximately 13 months.There are 50 patients on the review waiting list (with the longest waiter July 2024).  The waiting time is approximately 7 months.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the average wait time for vaginal mesh removal surgery.
answer_text: The following information has been provided by Belfast Health and Social Care Trust:There are currently 37 patients on the surgical waiting list.  There are 3 patients planned for surgery May and June 2025.  The waiting time is approximately 3 years.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for his assessment of the effectiveness of current legislation to regulate housing developers, in terms of providing consumer protections, rights and remedies for buyers.
answer_text: My Department has responsibility for the substantive land law in this jurisdiction and so I can answer in respect of the rights and remedies provided by the substantive law. 	The basic principle of conveyancing is that a purchaser enters a contract to purchase property at their own risk. Unless a vendor misleads a purchaser, the purchaser has no legal recourse. The statutory and common law remedies of misrepresentation, misstatement and mistake can bring some relief for an aggrieved purchaser. Additionally, the Law Society’s Home Charter Scheme sets out procedures for solicitors when acting in building developments which are designed to give an additional layer of protection for those involved.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any consideration she has given to traffic calming measures at St. Joseph’s Primary School, Carryduff.
answer_text: Road safety remains an extremely high priority for my Department and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and address the needs of all road users.  I am pleased to confirm that additional warning signs and school road markings on the approaches to St Joseph’s Primary School have been included in a programme of works to be completed as soon as resources permit. In addition, an assessment for a pedestrian crossing will also be undertaken.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will answer AQW 22668/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 22668 22-27 was answered on 24 March 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, pursuant to AQO 1245/22-27, for an update on the awarding of a contract for the new IT land registration solution.
answer_text: Contract considerations for the new IT Land Registration solution whilst at an advanced stage have not yet concluded.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will publish the social inclusion strategies.
answer_text: Following consideration of the work previously carried out to develop the Social Inclusion Strategies, alongside unprecedented pressures on resources across Executive departments, I decided to move forward on development of the Strategies on a phased basis, with work beginning, and now well progressed, on the Anti-Poverty and Disability Strategies. The draft Anti-Poverty Strategy received Executive approval and will go out to consultation in early June. I plan to submit a draft Disability Strategy to the Executive in the next few months.Further detail on the timetable for the development of the Gender Equality and Sexual Orientation Strategies will be confirmed in the near future.Final decisions on content, the timing of consultation and publication of the Strategies will be subject to Executive agreement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any engagement he and his officials have had with members of the (i) LGBTQI+ Strategy Co-Design Group; and (ii) Gender Equality Strategy Co-Design Group.
answer_text: My officials engage with former members of the Co-Design Groups on an ad-hoc basis on LGBT/Gender Equality issues.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on his Department’s work on bringing forward legislation to regulate the transfer of common parts during the sale of apartment blocks.
answer_text: Scoping work by the Civil Law Reform team in my Department commenced in January to review the regulation of property management companies, service charges and other aspects relating to shared property and its management. As apartments are a major part of the shared property sector, consideration to how they are owned and managed will form part of the scoping review. The scoping review has two main strands – stakeholder engagement and a review of the law in comparative jurisdictions. When I receive the report, I will consider how to progress this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the total cost of his visit to the United States of America in November 2024.
answer_text: I did not travel to the United States in November 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the outcomes from his trip to the United States of America in November 2024.
answer_text: I did not visit the United States of America in November 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of (i) meetings; and (ii) internal briefings that have taken place on the work of Intertrade UK.
answer_text: My officials have had two meetings with colleagues in TEO and the NIO regarding engagement to date on, or with, InterTrade UK. No internal briefings have taken place on the work of InterTrade UK.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she has (i) received; and (ii) requested any briefings on her Department's participation in UK Strategic Lawsuits Against Public Participation Taskforce.
answer_text: The SLAPPs Taskforce was convened for twelve months by the Department for Culture, Media and Sport (DCMS) in September 2023, during the period when the Assembly was suspended. In the absence of a Minister, at the invitation of DCMS, the Permanent Secretary nominated a Departmental official to attend Taskforce meetings. The Taskforce’s last meeting was in March 2024, shortly after my reappointment as Minister. Since my return to office, I have been briefed on SLAPPs more generally, including being made aware of the Taskforce.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she has (i) considered; and (ii) proposed any additional legislation to tackle paramilitarism in the last five years.
answer_text: My Department hosts the cross-Executive Programme on Paramilitarism and Organised Crime (EPPOC) which the Executive has agreed to extend for an additional two years to March 2027. EPPOC is an ambitious, multi-disciplinary and transformational change programme working to deliver the NI Executive’s priority of achieving safer communities, resilient to paramilitarism, criminality and coercive control.  Tackling paramilitarism, and its links to organised crime, requires a robust justice and law enforcement response, as well as measures to build confidence in the justice system. The establishment of the Paramilitary Crime Taskforce - a co-located law enforcement response to tackle criminality related to paramilitary groups - has ensured a robust criminal justice response to paramilitary gang activity.While the Assembly has sat for only 26 months in the last five years, I have introduced new legislation to deal with hate crime, stalking, and domestic abuse. My Department introduced Unexplained Wealth Orders (UWO) in June 2021, through the Criminal Finances Act 2017 of which the National Crime Agency are in the lead. Other asset recovery tools are used successfully in Northern Ireland on an ongoing basis, including applications by Police and the National Crime Agency for Account Freezing Orders and Listed Asset Orders, which were also introduced by the Criminal Finances Act 2017. All of these are directly relevant to addressing paramilitary harm.  In addition, I am introducing new measures in the Justice Bill which is progressing through the Assembly to provide additional tools for law enforcement to tackle those who, at any level, are involved in the commission and undertaking of serious and organised crime.  This follows a review of specific organised crime legislation in other jurisdictions and a consultation on proposals that was carried out between July and October 2020.  This new legislation creates two new offences: one of participating in the criminal activities of an organised crime group; and another of directing the criminal activities of an organised crime group.  There are also a range of measures in the Westminster Border Security, Asylum and Immigration Bill, and the Westminster Crime and Policing Bill which I am seeking to extend to Northern Ireland, subject to the legislative consent process.  This range of new legislation will further strengthen the ability of law enforcement agencies and the courts to address serious and organised crime.  Legislation, however, is just one way of addressing paramilitarism. There are many other ways in which EPPOC have been successful in supporting people and communities who are vulnerable to paramilitary influence and harm. A range of commitments are being delivered collaboratively by government departments, statutory agencies and partners in the voluntary and community sector. The initiatives funded under phase 2 of the Programme focus on helping those facing paramilitary harm presently, as well ensur

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any engagement she has had with the PSNI about the resumption of uploading crime and incident statistics to the Police UK website by the PSNI.
answer_text: The reporting of information in relation to offences reported to police is a matter for the Chief Constable, who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board.  I am committed to respecting the operational independence of the Chief Constable and the role of the Northern Ireland Policing Board.You may wish to contact the PSNI directly for further information.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 12092/22-27, to detail her Department's plans for actions to assist in dealing with trees which pose a risk to property and are located on private land.
answer_text: As confirmed in AQW 12092/22-27, trees planted on unadopted land are the responsibility of the relevant landowner who is responsible for ensuring that such trees and hedges do not endanger or obstruct road users.  Where my Department detects any roadside trees of concern, owners / occupiers are identified and requested to undertake the necessary action. The removing or cutting the trees or hedges to address any issues in relation to public safety is the responsibility of the owner of the trees.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will consider extending the High Hedges Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 to cover trees.
answer_text: The High Hedges Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 applies to hedges that are formed by a line of two or more evergreen or semi evergreen trees or shrub, that rise to a height of more than two metres above ground level. I have no plans to review this legislation at present.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any steps he is taking to support artists who have faced cuts to their funding.
answer_text: Decisions on funding for the arts, as with all the other valuable calls for investment, are made within the overall challenges of the public finance position.  Once allocations for 2025-26 have been finalised, the Arts Council of Northern  Ireland will be able to address the priorities set out in its business plan and make individual decisions within its allocation on grants to the arts sector. The Heritage, Culture and Creativity programme of work which I announced in July 2024 is developing a suite of policies, including for the arts, which will provide a solid base on which to make the case for further investment in the arts sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount of funding that will be allocated to the implementation of the All-Island Strategic Rail Review in the 2025-26 Budget.
answer_text: I have not taken any decisions on the 2025-26 Budget. Once a final budget is agreed, I will consider allocations in line with my priorities, the wider Executive PfG commitments and equality considerations, following closure of my Department’s Equality Impact Assessment consultation on 5 June 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the projects that will be delivered with the additional £112.6 million capital budget provided to her Department in the 2025-26 Draft Budget.
answer_text: I have not taken any decisions on the outcome of the draft 2025-26 Budget for my Department.My Department has launched a Equality Impact Assessment Consultation on the draft Budget and this is available through the following link: DfI Draft Budget 2025-26 Equality Impact Assessment Consultation | Department for Infrastructure.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail his Department's plans to increase funding for the arts sector.
answer_text: The 2025/26 budgetary allocations for which I am responsible, including funding for the arts sector through my allocation to the Arts Council, are subject to final determination.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of applicants that applied to the Apprenticeship Inclusion Challenge Fund between 30 October 2024 and 6 December 2024.
answer_text: My Department received 25 applications to the Apprenticeship Inclusion Challenge Fund between 30 October 2024 and 6 December 2024; ten under the ‘women’ cohort; nine under the ‘disability’ cohort; and six under the ‘place’ cohort.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the implementation of the Skills Fund.
answer_text: Minister Murphy launched the Skills Fund in July 2024 with an initial allocation of £11.5m. This funded 23 individual projects, supporting 9,000 people and over 600 companies to access skills in economic relevant areas. Promoting skills development remains a key priority for me and I am working with my officials to finalise the interventions for the 25-26 financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when her Department expects to complete the Programme for Government 2024-2027 target to expand Ulster University’s Magee campus to accommodate up to 10,000 students.
answer_text: The Magee Expansion Taskforce final report and action plan, launched 16 December 2024, provides a comprehensive and costed plan to expand Ulster University’s Magee campus to accommodate up to 10,000 students by 2032. Good progress has been made already, including the identification of 16 capital projects, acquisition of key pieces of land, an additional 500 student places in academic year 2024/25 and tuition fee loan access for Graduate Entry Medical School students from 2025/26.  Work has also commenced on marketing and other enabling projects to support the delivery of additional students.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of students her Department aims to be enrolled in the Ulster University's Magee campus by the end of the 2022-2027 mandate.
answer_text: The Magee Expansion Taskforce report sets the goal of 10,000 students at Ulster University's Magee campus by 2032. A significant capital build programme is required to accommodate this number of students. That capital development will take a number of years but as much progress as possible will be made in the meantime.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when she will present draft proposals on the (i) Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill; and (ii) Good Work Charter to the Executive for approval.
answer_text: I anticipate that my policy proposals following the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill consultation will be published in Spring 2025.With respect to the Good Work Charter, my Department in partnership with the Labour Relations Agency has scoped various options for charter development and is preparing a draft consultation. The consultation will be followed by engagement with key stakeholders in co-designing and refining the Charter. I intend to bring the Charter to the Executive for approval as per our commitment in the Programme for Government.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of whether the Legal Services Oversight Commissioner should reside within her Department rather than the Department of Finance.
answer_text: There are advantages and disadvantages to responsibility for the Legal Oversight Commissioner vesting in my Department rather than the Department of Finance. Responsibility for the Commissioner is however incidental to the primary issues of ensuring service users have confidence they are receiving high quality advice and representation and they can raise issues of concern where that has not been achieved. The vast majority of users report the services they receive are helpful, necessary, and provided with care but I am keen to explore how governance structures around legal aid can be used in combination with the vital regulatory roles of the Law Society, Bar Council, Department of Finance and the Commissioner to increase the confidence of service users further still.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the policies she proposed for the Programme for Government 2024-2027 to further tackle paramilitarism.
answer_text: My Department’s policy in relation to tackling paramilitarism is reflected in the work delivered by the Executive’s Programme on Paramilitarism and Organised Crime (EPPOC), which is hosted by the Department of Justice.
