question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the criteria he will use to determine the areas that will receive a Department for Infrastructure-led residents' parking scheme in the next 12 months.
answer_text: On 29 November 2024 my Department published a report on residents’ parking. It outlines a number of conclusions and recommendations for future schemes and highlights the need to ensure effective engagement with residents and local businesses. Following the publication of the Residents’ Parking Review Report, I have asked my officials to expedite the development of an application and assessment process for associated schemes, taking into account the findings and recommendations of the report and providing a streamlined approach for considering, assessing and prioritising requests for residents parking schemes going forward.  In the interim, my Department will continue to develop schemes already commenced such as in the Bogside in Derry and will give consideration to schemes in the vicinity of the new Ulster University campus in Belfast and the Magee campus

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when his Department will begin the review of the Energy Strategy.
answer_text: The Energy Strategy Mid-Term Review commenced in January 2025 and will complete within the calendar year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he has made any representations in support of the establishment of an EU Commission office in Belfast.
answer_text: This is a matter for The Executive Office.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the amount each Executive Department has been spent on advertising via (i) Meta; (ii) X and Twitter; and (iii) TikTok in each of the last three years.
answer_text: The annual amount spent on advertising via (i) Meta; (ii) X and Twitter; and (iii) TikTok in my Department for 2021-2022, 2022-2023 and 2023-2024 are set out below.  2021-22£2022-23£2023-24£Meta13,60410,9337,094X and Twitter2,1611,566-TikTok2,9101,685-It is for each Minister to record and report the spend in their respective departments.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he has any plans to consult on opening one or more national parks in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The absence of a sitting Executive for two years resulting in a constrained mandate has a real impact upon the making of primary legislation, alongside the current fiscal environment and budget allocations.  As a result whilst I do not have any current plans to consult on opening one or more national parks in Northern Ireland I would, however, draw the Member’s attention to the commitment in the Environmental Improvement Plan to complete a landscape policy/legislation review by 31 March 2027 (subject to resources) which will review the Northern Ireland specific approach to landscape designations.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any engagement he has had with Executive colleagues about ensuring the Education Maintenance Allowance provides adequate support to students and families to facilitate his plans to extend the compulsory leaving age for education to 18.
answer_text: In October 2024, I announced my intention to bring forward legislation to ensure that young people remain in education or training until they are 18 years old.  Through this means, we can create a minimum offer for all young people, particularly our most vulnerable, ensuring they have equity of opportunity and a structured pathway of support and guidance.My officials have begun to engage with colleagues in a range of Departments including the Department for the Economy. This is an important part of the policy development process as we take this important change forward. Discussions regarding the Education Maintenance Allowance will form part of those discussions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 18164/22-27, whether there is an estimated time frame for the completion of the work currently progressing.
answer_text: PHA continue to progress the work to assess the actions required to offer antenatal screening for a range of fetal abnormalities and inherited conditions, in line with the UK National Screening Committee recommendations. While there is currently no estimated time frame for completion of this work, I have asked PHA to progress it as quickly as possible.  Once the work is completed, the assessment will be considered by the Northern Ireland Screening Committee before seeking my approval for any proposed changes

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the steps the Law Society is required to take to ensure solicitors are upholding their professional responsibility to get and keep indemnity insurance.
answer_text: Article 63 of the Solicitors (Northern Ireland) Order 1976 Order provides for the Council of the Law Society to make regulations relating to indemnity against claims in respect of any description of civil liability incurred. Those regulations, the Solicitors Indemnity Insurance Regulations 2014, are the operational responsibility of the Law Society and are available on the Law Society’s website.The Law Society takes the issue of indemnity insurance extremely seriously and there are a number of ways in which it ensures every solicitor is covered, including it being a condition attached to the validity of a solicitor’s practicing certificate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether the Law Society holds a register detailing the company with which each solicitor has their indemnity insurance policy.
answer_text: I refer the Member to the response to AQW 20378/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the mitigation measures his Department is putting in place to ease disruption caused by ongoing roadworks at the Knockbracken Road junction, Belfast.
answer_text: The developer led road improvement scheme at the junction of Saintfield Road and Knockbracken Road commenced on 6 August 2024. The scheme will provide significant road safety and traffic progression improvements by widening both the northbound and southbound A24 Saintfield Road carriageway. This will allow for improvements to made to the signal-controlled junctions at the Old Saintfield Road/Knockbracken Health Care Centre and Knockbracken Road to include fully controlled right turn facilities within the junction layout.The work is being carried out in phases and includes road widening, construction of a retaining wall, utility diversions, new footpath, and enhanced pedestrian facilities. The construction work in phases 3 and 4, which commenced on 6 January 2025, required a change to the traffic management arrangement and this has resulted in some increased delays and congestion at this location.  In response to this increased congestion, we have modified the traffic signal phasing at the junction to help reduce delays.  We will continue to monitor the scheme.I recently announced the reopening of facilities at Cairnshill Park and Ride on a trial basis and I would encourage its usage as an alternative to commuting by car, helping to drive down congestion and emissions, change travel patterns and protect our environment.

DoH Expenditure on Arts-related projects and initiatives (£000)2021/222022/232023/24267267260The Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG) within my department has allocated £242,000 per annum to Arts Care NI. Arts Care have delivered a range of art related projects and initiatives within the five Health and Social Care Trusts including music, visual arts, clown doctors, dance, multi artform, creative writing, digital media, and production.Core Grant funding has been provided to Everyday Harmony Music Therapy which helps people whose lives have been affected by injury, illness, or disability by supporting their psychological, physical communicative and social needs.Because Music Therapy does not depend on the ability to sing, play an instrument, or even speak, it is a particularly effective clinical intervention for people who have difficulty communicating verbally.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether there is a duty on the Law Society, in cases where a solicitor loses their license to practise, to inform clients of that solicitor of the disbarment and signpost them to legal representation.
answer_text: Article 17 of the Solicitors (Northern Ireland) Order 1976 requires the Law Society, where a solicitor's practising certificate is suspended, to place a notice of that suspension for publication in the Belfast Gazette. It further provides, at Article 37, that where the name of a solicitor is removed from or struck off the roll or a solicitor is suspended from practice, that solicitor must satisfy the Society there are suitable arrangements relating to their clients.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they last requested advice on the process for recruitment of a Commissioner for Public Appointments.
answer_text: We have approved the submission from officials regarding the process to appoint a Commissioner for Public Appointments, and the competition will be launched shortly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of enforcement tickets that have been issued for entering a yellow box without being able to exit in Belfast city centre since (i) January 2024; and (ii) October 2024.
answer_text: The enforcement of yellow box junctions rests with the PSNI Road Policing, Traffic Management Unit, who can issue Fixed Penalty Notices for this offence.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any action he took to ensure the appointment of the chair of the Education Authority complied with the Code of Practice for Ministerial Public Appointments in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The appointment of the Chair of the Education Authority was made following an open, transparent and merit-based competition conducted in accordance with the Code of Practice issued by the Commissioner for Public Appointments for Northern Ireland (CPANI).CPANI allocated a suitable Independent Assessor to sit on the selection panel, who provided independent scrutiny as part of the appointment process to help ensure that the appointment was open, transparent, based on merit and commanded public confidence. The Independent Assessor was fully involved throughout the selection process to ensure that the Code of Practice was adhered to.I was also provided with CPANI guidance, both before the competition launched and also before I was invited to consider the candidates recommended as suitable for appointment, which assists Ministers with decisions in relation to public appointments.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the anticipated revenue raising from the short-term measures listed in the Rating Policy Strategic Roadmap as a percentage of (i) overall revenue from all locally raised rates; and (ii) the stock of unpaid rates.
answer_text: As I noted in my statement on 9 December the short term measures that will be the subject of consultation are aimed at enhancing the progressivity of the tax and not revenue raising alone. Nonetheless in the context of rate relief provision £9M is a significant amount, for example representing just under half of the annual spend on the Small Business Rate Relief scheme which supports 30,000 businessesThe anticipated revenue across both central and local government from the short-term measures listed in the Rating Policy Strategic Roadmap is estimated to be £9M. As a % of overall revenue from locally raised rates, the net assessments (after deducting all reliefs, etc.) was £1,510 million for 2023/24, which means that £9M would provide a % figure of 0.59%.As a % of the “stock of unpaid rates”, the value of rates that remain payable changes daily and remains subject to ongoing billing, collection and recovery procedures. £9M equates to 53.25% of the amount written off as uncollectable for legal, legislative and administrative reasons during 2023/24.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQO 1282/22-27, whether the First Minister misspoke when she referred to the Executive Legislation Programme 2024 as the Executive's legislative programme for 2024-25.
answer_text: Executive Legislation Programmes usually announce the Bills which will be introduced across the forthcoming full session of the Assembly.  However, to provide early notice to the Assembly of legislative intentions for the 2024/25 session, the Programme announced in our May statement included only those Bills which were considered capable of introduction before the end of 2024.  The passage of those Bills already introduced and of those still to be introduced will clearly extend into 2025, and together with the additional Bills we will be announcing in the New Year, will form the composite Programme for 2024/25.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any engagement he has had with the Department of Health and Social Care in relation to the proposed clinical trial on the impact of puberty blockers on those under 18 years old.
To ask the Minister of Finance whether she has asked the Minister for Infrastructure to provide advice on funding options for NI Water.
answer_text: I met the Infrastructure Minister to discuss the Funding of Waste Water Infrastructure on 7 October 2024.At that meeting, Minister O’Dowd provided an update on Waste Water developer contributions and we discussed the capital budget and constraints on funding generally.As Executive colleagues, the Infrastructure Minister and I have discussed Water funding regularly.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether medical and scientific advice relating to the decision to extend the ban on selling and administering puberty blockers to those under the age of 18 will be published.
answer_text: Under section 129(6) of the Medicines Act 1968, there is a requirement for Ministers to consult with an expert group on proposals to implement a permanent order.  Before the permanent prohibition order in relation to gonadotrophin-releasing hormone (GnRH) analogues was introduced, the Department and the UK Government sought the advice of the Commission on Human Medicines (CHM), given that their role is to advise Ministers on the quality, safety and efficacy of medicinal products. To this end all written responses received to the consultation were shared with, and considered by the CHM, with some organisations also making representations directly to the CHM as part of the consultation process.   A final report was produced by the CHM, which outlines its advice and views on the proposals.  The CHM report’s recommendations will be published as part of the consultation response.  Currently, there is no plan to publish the wider CHM report.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any support available for domestic households who wish to install a heat pump.
answer_text: At present, financial assistance is available through the Northern Ireland Sustainable Energy Programme (NISEP), for fully supported, and part funded schemes, for the installation of energy efficiency measures and heat pumps, with most of the funding targeted at lower income customers. My department is providing additional funding to NISEP to support energy efficiency programmes in the current financial year and exploring the potential to further enhance support for heat pump installations alongside energy efficiency programmes over the next two financial years.Future support is being consulted on through my department’s consultation; ‘Support for low carbon heating in residential buildings’ which was launched on the 8th of October and closes 31st December. The views provided will help my department in the development of policy in this area to inform options regarding the design, rules, and guidance of future support for low carbon heat, which will include heat pumps.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether her Department will have any role in enforcing the ban on puberty blockers being bought by, or prescribed to, those under the age of 18.
answer_text: I can confirm that my Department will have no role in enforcing the ban on puberty blockers being bought by, or prescribed to, those under the age of 18.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any action his Department will take against those who violate the recently extended puberty blocker ban.
answer_text: My Department’s Medicines Regulatory Group is responsible for key aspects of medicines control in Northern Ireland. It is statutorily empowered to ensure compliance with legislative requirements arising from various statutes, including the Medicines Act 1968 and the Human Medicines Regulations 2012. Where evidence or information suggests unlawful practice is occurring, or a breach of the Medicines Act or Human Medicines Regulations is identified, my Department’s Medicines Regulatory Group will consider a range of enforcement options proportionate to the circumstances to achieve compliance.  These can include, for example, through the issuing of warnings or advice, referral to professional bodies, or prosecution.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of the current waiting times for street light repairs.
answer_text: For the current financial year I have been able to allocate funding to enable my Department to provide a full street lighting maintenance service, with outage repairs generally being carried out within the established target of 5 working days.My Department has now replaced approximately 80% of its sodium lighting stock with LED units which, as well as delivering significant reductions in electricity consumption and carbon production, has greatly reduced the number of street lighting outages on the network, due to the longer operating lifetime of LEDs. I will continue to seek funding to convert the remainder of the network at the earliest opportunity.Members of the public can report street lighting outages online, by telephone or in writing by e-mail or letter. Full details of how to raise a report are provided on the NI Direct website. Report a street light fault | nidirect

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQO 1144/22-27, when he will publish his plans for Belfast Rapid Transit Glider Phase 2.
answer_text: Work on the feasibility of extending to Glengormley and Carryduff has been finalised and I commissioned work on the delivery options that could be implemented, utilising the £35m funding envelope from the Belfast Region City Deal. The Outline Business Case is being finalised in line with these feasibility reports and delivery options. When my officials have concluded this work, I will be in a position to make a decision.I welcome the £35m contribution from Belfast Region City Deal and recognise that this significant investment will bring enhancements to the city centre and wider public transport network as we seek to promote sustainable travel options and improve public transport journey time reliability.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment on the loss of earnings for businesses in the immediate proximity of Botanic Train Station, following its temporary closure over the summer.
answer_text: Essential engineering works took place on the railway line between Belfast and Lisburn, which necessitated the closure of the line from 3rd July until 13th October 2024. These works were crucial to complete rail infrastructure works, safety testing, commissioning and training that had to be completed to facilitate the opening of the Belfast Grand Central Station.  Passengers using the Lisburn Line stations, including Botanic station, were advised to use the regular scheduled Metro and Ulsterbus Lisburn Road services and valid rail tickets were accepted on all these services. To help mitigate the temporary closure, Translink also introduced enhanced bus timetables with more services made available on all Metro services and popular Goldliner routes across the North.My Department is not in a position to assess any change in earnings to local businesses as a specific result of the temporary closure of the Botanic Train Station.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of the Strangford Lough ferry service, particularly in relation to (i) reliability; and (ii) the price of the service.
answer_text: (i) My Department provides the Ferry Service every day of the year except Christmas Day with over 22,000 scheduled crossing per year. This financial year to date at least one vessel has been available for 99.97% of these sailings.(ii) My Department continues provide a subvention of approximately £1m per year towards to the ferry service. When compared to other similar short ferry crossings on the island, then the prices are very competitive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when his Department will proceed with a pedestrian crossing at Knockbreda Road, Belfast, adjacent to the rear of Cherryvale playing fields.
answer_text: I can advise that my officials previously wrote to Belfast City Council asking them to carry out further consultation with the residents most effected by the proposed pedestrian crossing location close to Mount Eden Park. I understand that this consultation will commence early in the new year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when the draft Budget 2025-26 will be published.
answer_text: It is my intention to bring a draft Budget 2025-26 to the Executive for agreement later this month. The draft Budget 2025-26 will be published following Executive agreement.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of people accessing GP out-of-hours services in (i) the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust; and (ii) all Health and Social Care Trusts as a whole in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Please find below details regarding number of people accessing GP Oout of Hours (OOH) services (i) the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust; and (ii) all Health and Social Care Trusts as a whole in each of the last five years. YearBelfast Health and Social Care Trust GP OOHsAll 5 GP OOHs Providers2019/2020110,076524,3202020/202182,540411,0302021/202291,569468,5242022/2023104,497512,3982023/2024 103,003497,313GP OOH services may also manage non-clinical, non-intervention calls, during the out of hours period. These calls are not reflected in the clinical activity figures provided.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he has made any proposal to the North South Ministerial Council, as per Article 14 (b) of the Protocol on Ireland/Northern Ireland, to raise issues relating to the all-island economy as per Article 11 of the protocol.
answer_text: No.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they support the Minister of Finance bringing forward her paper on rating policy changes for Executive discussion.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the 2024 legislative programme.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the total cost of the Maze Long Kesh Development Corporation since its creation.
answer_text: The total expenditure for the Maze Long Kesh Development Corporation from 2012/13 to 2023/24 is £23.1m, of which £9.5m is capital. The expenditure of the Development Corporation is set out in their published Annual Report and Accounts, currently available up to 2022/23.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether the Executive remains committed to establishing an independent Environmental Protection Agency, as set out in New Decade, New Approach.
answer_text: We understand that the Minister for Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs has appointed an independent panel to consider the creation of an independent Environmental Protection Agency.As was conveyed during Topical Questions on 25 November, we anticipate the production of that report and will consider proposals with Executive colleagues when the panel reports.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the basis on which the independent panel reporting on environmental governance is independent.
answer_text: The independent panel was established with a clear mandate to provide an impartial and objective review of environmental governance, ensuring that recommendations are based on evidence and expert judgment rather than any particular political or institutional agenda.The panel will provide its own assessment and findings and DAERA will not be seeking to influence these in any way. As Minister, I am committed to upholding the independence of the panel and respecting its experts’ recommendations and I will carefully consider their recommendations. The panel has been selected from a diverse range of fields based on their experience and expertise which will reduce the risk of bias on undue influence from any single group.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of undergraduate entrants from Northern Ireland to universities in (i) England; (ii) Scotland; (iii) Wales; and (iv) the Republic of Ireland in each of the last five academic years.
answer_text: The table below sets out the Norths domicile undergraduate enrolments at higher education institutions broken down by location of institution from 2017/18 to 2021/22 (the most recent year for which data is available).2017/182018/192019/202020/212021/22North of Ireland37,76037,75037,57038,35039,165England10,83510,81010,19510,01010,075Scotland 3,7103,5903,4953,2603,160Wales530500470420450South of Ireland1,0751,1401,2851,2551,250

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when she will publish the Financial Provisions Bill.
answer_text: ANSWERIt is my intention to bring a Financial Provisions Bill to the Assembly as soon as the necessary preparatory work has been completed. Currently the Bill is being drafted by the Office of Legislative Council. Once this work has been completed, I will bring a draft Bill and Explanatory Memorandum to the Executive and seek agreement that the Bill is introduced to the Assembly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will publish the Independent Review of the Alcohol Premises Licensing System in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The independent review of the licensing system including surrender principle has been completed. The final report was received in the Department on Friday 15 November and my intention is to publish the report as soon as practicably possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department is planning to offer a multi-year budget to Sure Start services following the UK Government public spending audit.
answer_text: The Executive is still in the process of agreeing individual departments’ allocations for 2025-26. However, it should be noted that Education faces unavoidable cost pressures and rising service demands associated with delivering statutory and policy obligations that the current budget allocation will not fully address.Given these budgetary pressures, and the lack of an agreed budget allocation for 2025-26 or beyond, it is not possible to offer multi-year budgets to Sure Start or any other service. When my Department’s 2025-26 budget allocation is agreed, I will commence consulting on my Department’s budget to inform my decisions regarding funding for services for 2025-26, including Sure Start services.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether the independent review of environmental governance will be published by his Department.
answer_text: The panel will undertake their independent review of environmental governance and report back with their recommendations by summer 2025. After considering the panel’s recommendations I intend to identify a preferred way forward, which I will then take to the Executive before these are presented to the Assembly.I will ensure the report and my preferred way forward is promptly published and Assembly members updated.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 15569/22-27.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the number of joint meetings they have held to discuss EU relations.
answer_text: We have attended the following meetings where EU Matters or Windsor Framework were discussed: 1. Meeting with the German Ambassador to the UK 2. Meeting with French Ambassador to the UK 3. The thirteenth meeting of the UK-EU Withdrawal Agreement Joint Committee. 4. Meeting with Secretary of State for Northern Ireland and the Minister for the Cabinet Office covering a range of issues, including the Windsor Framework and EU relations. We have also discussed the impact of Brexit on NI at various engagements and meetings such as; North South Ministerial Council, East-West Council, British-Irish Council, Council of Nations and Regions, Inter-Ministerial Group, meetings with the PM, the SOS NI, and with the Taoiseach and Tánaiste.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether proposals for (i) carbon budgets; and (ii) a climate action plan will be brought to the Executive before the end of 2024.
answer_text: The first three carbon budgets for the period (2023-2037) for Northern Ireland were agreed by the Executive on 21 November 2024. I will be taking steps to lay the regulations, that set the carbon budgets, before the Northern Ireland Assembly for their debate and approval as soon as possible. My department has made significant progress working across all departments to take forward Northern Ireland’s first Climate Action Plan. Once the carbon budget Regulations have been approved, I aim to bring the draft Climate Action Plan to Executive colleagues, seeking their approval to publish the Plan for a 16-week consultation, as required by legislation.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an overview of her Department’s strategy for utilising the Financial Transactions Capital allocated to the Executive in the 2024-2025 financial year.
To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of whether further investment is needed in the Northern Ireland Football Fund to reflect increased costs.
answer_text: As I have previously stated, the funding set aside for the Northern Ireland Football Fund falls far short of the current estimate of costed need.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the number of civil servants that routinely work in mobile or modular accommodation on the Civil Service estate.
answer_text: ANSWERMy Department has 9 mobile or modular buildings which are currently used to accommodate staff.  Given the roll out of agile working and workstyle agreements, Department of Finance could not advise how many civil servants routinely work in those buildings however it is likely to be a small proportion of staff.Under the NICS Office Estate Strategy, it is planned to phase out the ongoing use of such buildings to accommodate staff over the next 4 years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why Translink Metro buses do not routinely open middle doors to expedite passenger exit.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and can confirm that all of the new battery electric vehicles across the Metro network have double doors. Prior to the introduction of the electric vehicles, Translink undertook a risk assessment of the suitability of double door operation.  Due to infrastructure challenges, mainly at stops outside of the City Centre, for reasons such as length and topography, it would not be safe to deploy this function automatically at all stops. Drivers have been trained to open the middle door where it is safe and appropriate to do so. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

answer_text: In September 2020, the Driver and Vehicle Agency (DVA) introduced a new online Commercial Licensing System (CLS) which enables the numbers of taxi vehicle licences to be broken down into local council areas. question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any discussions he has had with (i) his officials; and (ii) the Department of Education in relation to how children, who have been moved a significant distance from their school due to the need to access Housing Executive temporary accommodation, can be supported to minimise disruption to their education.
answer_text: I have not directly engaged with my officials or the Department of Education on this matter, however issues relating to homelessness, including children and temporary accommodation, are discussed at a strategic level at the DfC led Homelessness Strategy Steering Group, which is attended by the Department of Education. Beyond this forum, discussions in relation to individual cases, are a matter for the Education Authority and their contact with the Northern Ireland Housing Executive local teams.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any discussions he has had with (i) his officials; and (ii) the Department of Education in relation to how children, who have been moved a significant distance from their school due to the need to access Housing Executive temporary accommodation, can be supported to minimise disruption to their education.
answer_text: I recognise the additional challenges faced by children, who have been moved a significant distance from their school as a result of entering Northern Ireland Housing Executive temporary accommodation, in accessing education.A range of support is available through schools, the Education Authority (EA) and other organisations, to help assist children who are in this position to access education. The supports available from the EA include a telephone help desk supports for parents and guardians who require advice around how to find school places.  The EA’s Admissions teams, Educational Welfare and, specifically for Asylum Seekers, Roma and Traveller families, the EA’s Intercultural Education Service, can also assist families find school places at their nearest school with available places. Furthermore, School Transport Officers are also available to offer advice and guidance when families are eligible for home to school transport solutions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the UK Government has approached his Department to discuss a request for United States of America pre-clearance facilities, as per the Command Paper, Safeguarding the Union.
answer_text: My officials have discussed with the Department for Transport (DfT) the possibility of extending pre-clearance services to the North. Civil aviation is a reserved matter, but the British government has committed to entering exploratory talks with the United States Administration on the options for introducing pre-clearance from Belfast International Airport to destinations in the USA. My Department will do all it can to facilitate those discussions.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they have had any briefings on the Castlereagh Foundation.
answer_text: The Executive Office is taking forward the necessary arrangements to implement the provisions of the Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022 (The Act) for which it is responsible, including the establishment of the new bodies, namely: The Office of Identity and Cultural Expression;The Irish Language Commissioner; andThe Commissioner for the Ulster Scots and the Ulster British tradition.The Act also provides for a further body, the Castlereagh Foundation, which can be established by either the Office of Identity and Cultural Expression or the Secretary of State. We have been briefed by our officials on our responsibilities under the Act.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will commit to bring Northern Ireland into line with the rest of the UK in providing pregnant women with access to first trimester antenatal screening for foetal abnormalities.
answer_text: In Northern Ireland, antenatal screening for fetal anomalies, including neural tube defects using ultrasound scanning, is offered to all pregnant women as part of routine antenatal care.Work has been undertaken to assess the actions required to offer antenatal screening for a range of fetal anomalies and inherited conditions, in line with the UK National Screening Committee recommendations. This work is currently progressing and will inform any future policy decision.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the number of times the Interim Public Sector Transformation Board has met since it was established.
answer_text: The interim Public Sector Transformation Board has met nine times since it was established.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether the Executive Legislation Programme 2024 will be delivered in full.
answer_text: Subject to the consent of the Assembly, it is the Executive's intention that the Bills included in the Executive's initial Legislation Programme for 2024/25 announced on 23 May will be delivered. We intend to make a further statement to the Assembly on the full 2024/25 Programme which will include an update on those Bills already announced.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 17385/22-27, for an estimate of the number of applications for egress or entrance onto a protected route have been granted in the last five years.
answer_text: Accesses onto protected routes are granted, subject to satisfying appropriate planning policy considerations, by the planning authority through the planning permission process. As previously advised in AQW 17385/22-27, details of such approvals are not readily accessible as the planning portal is not searchable by protected route status.  The Department therefore does not have a reasonable basis upon which to provide a reliable estimate of the number of applications for egress or entrance onto protected routes for the requested period without disproportionate cost.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he has attempted to bring a Climate Action Plan to the Executive for discussion in 2024.
answer_text: My department has made significant progress working across all departments to take forward Northern Ireland’s first Climate Action Plan. I am fully committed to delivering a Climate Action Plan that is transformational, evidence-based and informed by stakeholders.I have issued a paper to my Executive colleagues to seek agreement on the draft regulations for the first three carbon budgets and the 2030 and 2040 emissions reduction targets for Northern Ireland. Once the carbon budget regulations have been agreed by the Executive, I aim to bring the draft Climate Action Plan to Executive colleagues as soon as possible seeking their approval to publish the Plan for a 16-week consultation, as required by legislation.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an estimate of the amount of outstanding capital funding required to complete the Strule Shared Education Campus.
answer_text: The overall estimated Department of Education (DE) cost of the Strule Campus is £375m.The latest estimate is that up to £314m capital investment is required to complete construction of the campus.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 17448/22-27, to detail the advice he sought which indicated that permitting Class A and Class C taxis to use bus lanes would reduce congestion.
answer_text: As I indicated in my answer to AQW 17448/22-27 I am aware of the level of traffic congestion at certain times within Belfast City Centre and I have asked for this action to be taken as I believe it will mitigate some of the disruption the travelling public are experiencing.The proposal to permit Class A and Class C taxis to use certain bus lanes in the vicinity of and for the duration of the roadworks needed to facilitate the new Belfast Grand Central Station will be undertaken as an Experimental Traffic Control Scheme so that the benefits of the new arrangements can be assessed, and this will help inform future policy decisions in this area.During the scheme we intend to seek the views of all users on how the arrangement is working.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he intends to take any action in relation to pavement parking.
answer_text: My Department has several powers to prohibit parking wholly or partly on a footway at a number of specific locations and can also introduce footway parking bans. Footway parking is generally prohibited along urban clearways and rural clearways, as well as alongside the controlled areas at Zebra, Pelican and Puffin crossings.  The recent introduction of the Footways (Prohibition of Waiting) Order (Northern Ireland) 2023 allows my Department to enforce against vehicles found parked on footways alongside bus lanes, bus stops/stands, mandatory cycle lanes and mandatory ‘School Keep Clear’ markings. The legislation adds to the list of specific areas where footway parking is prohibited and is a positive step forward in deterring inconsiderate pavement parking.  In the last twelve months, 1,932 Penalty Charge Notices (PCNs) have been issued for footpath specific contraventions. My Department also continues to promote the importance of parking responsibly and legally, encouraging the public to consider other road, footway and cycle lane users when parking and is currently collaborating with Belfast City Council and the PSNI on a pilot scheme to address parking issues in Belfast, known as the ‘Park with us in mind’ initiative. This initiative aims to reduce poor parking behaviour by outlining the importance of proper parking, to ensure bins can be collected on time and other essential services vehicles, including ambulances and the Fire Service, can access streets in an emergency. However, I recognise that inconsiderate pavement parking is an ongoing issue, and my officials are currently exploring potential next steps for my consideration.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 15328/22-27, for a timeline for the current review of the Together: Building a United Community Strategy.
answer_text: A final draft of the Together: Building a United Community review is currently under development by officials, and recommendations are expected to be submitted shortly. The recommendations of this review will inform the timeline for the development of a refreshed approach to Good Relations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether misogyny or gender-based hate will be included as a category of hate crime in planned upcoming reform of hate crime legislation.
answer_text: Policy development work to assess the suitability of the inclusion of sex/gender as a protected characteristic in hate crime legislation is ongoing. This work includes assessing the suitability of the inclusion of misogyny in the hate crime framework.Officials are monitoring the UK’s government proposals on the inclusion of extreme misogyny (including online) as a form of terrorism and the creation of a separate law to tackle "the spectrum of misogynistic conduct” in Scotland.  Any future policy decisions on gender/sex/misogyny will require consideration in the wider context of the TEO-led VAWG work and Strategy.Legislation alone will not address the impact of misogynistic behaviour. A cross departmental holistic approach focused on prevention and education and the need to build resilience to deal with the consequences of misogyny needs to be prioritised.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, in relation to his Department's social inclusion strategies, to detail the amount spent to prepare and create the (i) Gender Strategy; and (ii) Sexual Orientation Strategy.
answer_text: The information sought is not readily identifiable. The work to date on the development of the strategies has been an integral part of the delivery of a range of functions of the Department. It is therefore not possible to disaggregate these costs and apportion a defined spend on such work.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether the outstanding recommendations of the Gillen Review in relation to reform of the reasonable belief defence in sexual assault and abuse trials will be implemented during the 2022-2027 mandate.
answer_text: I support Sir John Gillen’s conclusion in his Review of the Law and Procedures in Serious Sexual Offences that legislative steps need to be taken to underpin the key principle of consent as defined in Article 3 of the Sexual Offences (Northern Ireland) Order 2008: “A person consents if he agrees by choice, and has the freedom and capacity to make that choice”. The Review made a number of recommendations as to how this might be achieved, including amendment of the definition of what constitutes reasonable belief in consent. It remains my intention to develop relevant policy and legislative proposals with a view to their inclusion in a Victims Bill, which I plan to introduce in the Assembly in Spring 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether the outstanding recommendations of the Gillen Review in relation to education for juries will be implemented during the 2022-2027 mandate.
answer_text: The Gillen Review recommendations relating to jury education fall into two main categories – those relating to educating jurors about their legal responsibilities including their use of social media during trials and those that relate to raising juror awareness of rape myths.The Review recommended that the judiciary should give clear directions to jurors in relation to their use of social media during trials. In response, the Lady Chief Justice’s Office (LCJO) has advised that guidance on this matter is provided in the Judicial Bench Book which contains specimen directions for judges to give to the jury. Judges have also received specific training on this matter. LCJO has also confirmed that, as standard, judges give clear and concise directions to jurors on their legislative obligations on the first day of the trial, and as required at the conclusion of each day’s hearing.In addition, the Department of Justice, in conjunction with the LCJO and the NI Courts and Tribunals Service (NICTS) has published a booklet that sets out guidance for jurors on their legal responsibilities, contempt of court and on the laws around their use of social media during and after trials. All sworn jurors are required to read the booklet prior to the commencement of the trial and posters highlighting this information are in place in all courthouses. The booklet is also available on the Department’s website at this link - Your legal responsibilities as a juror | Department of JusticeThe jury booklet is also available on the Council for the Curriculum, Examinations & Assessment (CCEA) Hub, which provides resources to support teachers. The availability of the booklet has been highlighted in CCEA’s quarterly e-bulletin to all teachers as well as on their social media channels. The Department of Education has been requested to promote this resource as part of the Learning for Life and Work curriculum which aims to encourage greater civic responsibility and includes modules on juries, jury responsibilities and contempt laws.We assess the review recommendations relating to education for jurors on their legal responsibilities to be complete.In relation to raising juror awareness of rape myths, LCJO has again advised that the judiciary have received updated training in this area and that they provide oral directions on rape myths at the start of sexual offence trials; these instructions are straight-forward and cover everything required. LCJO has further advised that judges will also intervene during the trial as appropriate.The Gillen Review also recommended that the NICTS and LCJO should consider the research and recommendations of Professor Cheryl Thomas in relation to the extent that jury myths influence jury verdicts and, if appropriate, introduce a video to jurors in serious sexual offence cases here in Northern Ireland, similar to that to be piloted in England. Professor Thomas has advised that for various reasons, the pilot of the ‘rape myths video’ in England has

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the next meeting of the Living with Water Programme Board.
answer_text: The next meeting of the Living With Water Programme (LWWP) Board is scheduled for 19th December 2024. As reported to Executive colleagues and the Committee for Infrastructure in October 2024, due to the increase in the estimated costs to deliver the Living With Water in Belfast Plan, from £1.4bn to £2.1bn, my Department carried out a review of the affordability of the Plan. The recommendations from the review, which I have accepted, are that the Belfast Plan still needs to be delivered, however, projects in the Plan will be taken forward as business as usual within allocated annual budgets and not as part of a structured programme. I am committed to delivering the objectives of the Belfast Plan, however the original timescale for delivery, estimated at 12 years, is no longer viable. It was always accepted that additional funding would be required to deliver the Plan.  In the New Decade New Approach agreement, Living With Water was identified as a beneficiary of increased funding from the British Government’s infrastructure revolution, under the title of “Turbo Charging Infrastructure”. It is regrettable that this additional funding has not been provided to date, by the British Government. The LWWP Board Meeting on the 19th December will therefore be the last meeting, as currently constituted. It will be replaced by a stakeholder oversight group for the Belfast Plan and reporting arrangements will be put in place to monitor achievements against the Plan objectives.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the thoroughfares in Belfast which have generated the greatest rates revenue for Land and Property Services in each of the last five years.
answer_text: I refer the Member to AQW 17670/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he has made representations to the Minister of Finance to plan for a multi-year Executive Budget which provides for ring-fenced funding for farm payments.
answer_text: The existing funding for farm payments continues to be earmarked by HM Treasury until 31 March 2025 and it is important to provide certainty on this funding after that in light of the change announced by the Chancellor on 30 October 2024.  I shall be continuing my engagement with the Minister of Finance and Executive colleagues as part of the ongoing Budget 2025-26 exercise to seek that this funding is earmarked by the Executive following the end of earmarking by HM Treasury.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs
