question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for her assessment of the prevalence of Strategic Lawsuits against Public Participation.
answer_text: There are no official figures held on numbers of Strategic Lawsuits Against Public Participation (SLAPPs) cases in this jurisdiction. SLAPPs relate to several areas of law and policy including privacy, data protection, environmental protection, and defamation. The Department of Finance has a policy interest in SLAPPs to the extent that they relate to defamation. The number of defamation claims in this jurisdiction in any given year is typically small and there is no specific data available to collate the number, of defamation-related SLAPPs, though it is probable that these are relatively few in number. It is for the courts to determine if any particular claim amounts to a SLAPP. Department of Justice officials sit on the UK SLAPPs Taskforce which is facilitated by the Ministry of Justice. The Taskforce brings together government, journalism organisations, civil society and legal services professionals, and is intended to co-ordinate a response that will complement measures to tackle economic crime-linked SLAPPs.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department holds information on the number of (i) non-UK; and (ii) Irish citizens working in Health and Social Care.
answer_text: Information on citizenship or nationality of Health & Social Care employees is not available from the Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his Department’s assessment of the sectors with the greatest shortages in qualified staff.
answer_text: The latest economic evidence shows that the local labour market continues to remain tight with a high demand for workers, unemployment remaining historically low and many sectors facing recruitment challenges.  The Employer Skills Survey 2022 provides a key source of intelligence on the latest skills challenges facing employers locally.  The report found that the industries with the highest level of skill-shortage vacancies were Transport and Storage, Construction, Primary Sector and Utilities and Manufacturing.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of meetings he has had in relation to the Tribeca Belfast development since coming into office.
answer_text: I have not yet had any meetings in relation to the Tribeca Belfast development since coming into office.My officials are scheduled to meet with Belfast City Council officers on 02 July 2024 after which I will be briefed on this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the actions he is taking to support (i) onshore; and (ii) offshore wind energy.
answer_text: The Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 established the 2030 target of at least 80% of electricity consumption to come from renewable sources. Essential to meeting this target will be the establishment of a support scheme for renewable electricity.Departmental officials are currently working on a scheme design that will incentivise investment in larger scale renewable electricity projects, including established technologies such as onshore wind, to ensure consumers pay a fair price for electricity produced locally.The High-Level Design for the scheme was published in April this year with the final design due for completion by the end of 2024 and first auction anticipated in early 2026.In order to meet the targets established in the Climate Change Act, our energy system must also diversify to harness an increasing amount of indigenous and clean energy sources. I recognise the importance of offshore renewables for our net zero future and my Department, in collaboration with other departments and key stakeholders, have developed an Offshore Renewable Energy Action Plan (OREAP) to enable offshore renewable energy development in the north’s marine area from 2030.	Incentivising the necessary investment in offshore projects will continue to be part of the development process for the support scheme and work being undertaken by the OREAP Steering Group.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail his Department's current plans for the completion of the Belfast Streets Ahead public realm scheme.
answer_text: My Department has plans to deliver two projects under the Belfast Streets Ahead Public Realm programme - Belfast Streets Ahead Phase 3 in the north of the city centre and Belfast Streets Ahead Phase 5 in the south of the city centre.  Both these projects have been impacted by the Department for Infrastructure (DfI) plans for the next phase of the Belfast Rapid Transit scheme and by the Eastern Transport Plan (ETP) 2035. My officials are currently liaising with DfI officials to find a way to deliver the public realm improvements, aligned with the ETP requirements, alongside DfI’s Belfast Rapid Transit works to avoid nugatory work and expenditure, as well as disruption to city centre users.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the value of rates relief on properties in North Street, Belfast, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The total value of rate relief awarded to properties in North Street, Belfast, in each of the past five rating years is detailed in the table below. This includes all rate relief awarded to properties in North Street, Belfast: Small Business Rate Relief, Charitable exemptions, Non-Domestic Vacant Rating, and Business Rates Holidays during Covid-19.Rating yearTotal rate relief awarded2019/20£313,9472020/21£467,2882021/22£420,7982022/23£228,1502023/24£418,021

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what assessment he has made in relation to the (i) quality; and (ii) safety of reservoirs.
answer_text: My Department does not undertake inspections of reservoirs for which it has no management role or responsibility. However, an assessment was undertaken in 2015/16 at the request of the then Agriculture and Rural Development Committee. It was identified at that time that of the 179 controlled reservoirs here, 45 out of the 105 who took part in the audit were found to be in poor or very poor condition.The quality and safety of reservoirs is the responsibility of reservoir managers. I intend to bring forward legislation to further commence the Reservoirs Act (NI) 2015 in the Autumn of 2024 to enable my officials to administer a regulatory framework in relation to the safety of controlled reservoirs here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the engagement he has had in relation to A Bolder Vision for Belfast in the last three years.
answer_text: In July 2022, I attended the ‘A Bolder Vision for Belfast – Joint Ministerial Group’ alongside the Minister for Communities, the Chief Executive of Belfast City Council, and officials from across the three organisations. Since then, officials in my Department have continued this collaborative working relationship with Belfast City Council and the Department for Communities, with my officials actively involved in steering groups and partnership groups with both organisations.You may also be aware that the Eastern Transport Plan (ETP) 2035 is currently under development and is being integrated with the Belfast City Council’s Local Development Plan, process. The visioning principles set out in the Bolder Vision aligns with the policy context and objective setting that the ETP is being developed and measured through.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment on whether targets in the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 are deliverable in the absence of an operational North South Interconnector.
answer_text: The second North South Interconnector is an important tool in delivering the Energy Strategy and the Climate Change Act targets.  My department is working with SONI and NIE Networks on delivery of the second North South Interconnector, which is expected to be completed in 2028.  I recently met with my counterpart in the South, Minister Ryan, from the Department of the Environment, Climate and Communications, to discuss progress and the importance of delivery of the North South Interconnector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when Translink will roll-out contactless ticketing throughout its network.
answer_text: The implementation of contactless ticketing is part of a muti-facet integrated ticketing project. Contactless ticketing is available on NIR, Glider and Metro services. Translink is currently implementing contactless ticketing on NIR Ticket Vending Machines and Ulsterbus/Goldliner services. This is due to be completed by the end of the summer.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when (i) North Street car park, Belfast; (ii) 43A Rosemary Street, Belfast; and (iii) 25-29 North Street, Belfast were added to the rating list.
answer_text: North Street car park, Belfast.The carpark at N38 North Street, Belfast was added to the Valuation List on 22/4/2024 with effect from 1/4/2023. That is the publication date of the current Valuation List.	LPS first digitised its Valuation Lists in the early 1990’s. Dates against some properties which are shown below as 22/3/1991 were in fact in existence in the old paper records before that date. They are held at the Public Records Office NI (PRONI). 43A Rosemary Street, Belfast.The club at 43A Rosemary Street, Belfast has been in the Valuation List since 22/3/1991. 25-29 North Street, Belfast.There are five separately assessed rateable properties at this address.The shop at 25 North Street, Belfast has been in the Valuation List since 22/3/1991.The shop at 27 North Street, Belfast has been in the Valuation List since 22/3/1991.The shop at 29 North Street, Belfast has been in the Valuation List since 29/1/2003.The 1st – 3rd floor offices at 29A North Street, Belfast have been in the Valuation List since 2/8/2001.The shop at 29B North Street, Belfast has been in the Valuation List since 22/3/1991.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of the feasibility to introduce night-time public transport in Belfast.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:“Nightmover late night services are usually in place from late November through to Christmas. During this period, patronage is strong as a result of the nighttime economy and as we ramp up marketing and promotional activity.If permanent night time services were to be delivered under Translink’s normal fare structure, we would require additional financial support or "pump priming" to commence the service and bridge the gap until it began to generate sufficient revenue to sustain itself. It is useful to note that, in similar contexts, public transport operators have observed a growth in passenger numbers not just on the newly introduced night-time services, but also on the service network more widely. In other words, a positive ripple effect in use of public transport.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.In addition to the operational response from Translink, my Department is not currently in a position to provide additional funding for these additional services.  As a result, the introduction of any services must be commercially viable.My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government. The value of our infrastructure cannot be overstated, and I will continue to work with Executive colleagues to ensure appropriate investment is made.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail when the Womens Centres Childcare funding for the Windsor Women’s Centre for 2024-25 will be confirmed.
answer_text: Now that my department’s budget allocation is known, I have tasked my officials to work at pace to ensure that all funded community groups are updated as soon as possible on their indicative allocations for the 2024/25 year.  These allocations remain indicative as they are subject to the outcome of the Department’s budget consultation exercise which was launched today.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will consider additional traffic calming measures at the Annadale and Ormeau Embankment junction to account for additional traffic coming from Dunnes Stores.
answer_text: A number of traffic calming measures, including central hatching and right turning pockets, are in place at the Annadale and Ormeau Embankment junction. I can advise that there are no plans to alter the current road arrangement at the Annadale and Ormeau Embankment junction.One of the best ways to ensure safety on our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050, is by changing road user behaviour. As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe. The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will reconsider his Department’s response to residents' request to paint road markings in Malone View.
answer_text: The Department receives many requests for carriageway markings, and it is therefore important that my Department prioritise its constrained resources to those roads where there are significant problems or difficulties. Within residential areas, where traffic flows are generally low and largely local, drivers should be aware of the road layout. Markings to indicate the traffic management in an area are normally restricted to junctions with major roads or to sites where there are traffic progression issues. An assessment of Malone View area, which is subject to largely local traffic, indicated that there is good visibility for opposing drivers using these streets and at their junctions, and did not suggest a requirement for road markings.A review of collision history in Malone View Avenue also did not highlight any safety concerns in the area. In these circumstances the Department has no plans to provide road markings in Malone View area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what action his Department is taking to progress the North South Interconnector.
answer_text: My Department has received applications from SONI Ltd, to consider necessary (i.e. compulsory) wayleaves in respect of the North South Interconnector. Engagement with landowners affected by these applications is ongoing.I also recently met with my counterpart in the south, Minister Ryan, from the Department of the Environment, Climate and Communications, to discuss progress on both sides of the border.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the average number of people deemed homeless in the South Belfast constituency in each of the last five years.
answer_text: This information is not available in the format you have requested.The biannual Northern Ireland Homelessness Bulletin July - December 2023 was published on 14 March 2024 and contains information on a range of areas relating to homelessness and is available here: https://www.communities-ni.gov.uk/publications/northern-ireland-homelessness-bulletin-july-december-2023 Please refer to Accompanying Table 2.3, in the above link, which details households accepted as homeless by Local Government District.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the redevelopment of Casement Park, in time to host UEFA Euros 2028.
answer_text: My Department has worked with key partners in the UK Government and local stakeholders, including UCGAA and the Irish FA, to develop a programme to construct the Casement Park stadium to meet UEFA’s EURO 2028 requirements. In addition, officials in my Department have been working collaboratively with CPD to establish new procurement arrangements utilising the Cabinet Office Crown Commercial Services Framework for major construction projects. A shortlist of contractors has been established and tender documents are prepared and ready to issue.As I am sure you are aware, the funding required to redevelop Casement Park is significantly more than the current funding package. There has been extensive engagement with NIO, DCMS and HMT regarding the funding shortfall. Clarification is now needed from UK Government on the timing of any funding commitment.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the current rights of private renters.
answer_text: The rights of private tenants are protected in legislation, including the Private Tenancies (Northern Ireland) Order 2006 and the Rent (Northern Ireland) Order 1978. My Department is progressing a programme of Private Rented Sector (PRS) reform which is aimed at improving the safety, security, and standards of the Sector. A key component of this work is achieving a balance between the rights and responsibilities of landlords and tenants.Recent changes to private tenancies law under Phase 1 of this programme that have increased the protections for tenants, include:A limit on deposits that can be requested and held;Requirements for certain information to be provided on commencing and during a tenancy; andMeasures to increase fire safety. Ongoing work that will further enhance these protections, includes the development of regulations on electrical safety and to allow the introduction of much longer notice to quit periods. In parallel, as part of Phase 2 reform, my Department has commissioned research to aid considerations on potential letting agent regulation to prevent exploitation of tenants, including through the charging of a wide range of fees.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department is on track to meet its target of supplying 100,000 homes by 2037.
answer_text: The ambition to deliver ‘100,000 homes’ outlined in the draft Housing Supply Strategy, aimed to provide a long-term framework for delivery of the supply we need. The Strategy is based on a whole system approach which means delivering on this ambition will ultimately depend on all those involved in the housing system working collaboratively to fix it. The intention of the strategy is for it to be an Executive one, due to the range of issues impacting supply that cut across the remit of several Executive Departments. This includes issues such as infrastructure, planning and skills. Therefore, in order to meet this ambition the Executive must prioritise housing and ensure this is matched with sufficient funding.I am working to bring a finalised strategy to the Executive for its consideration as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline her Department’s engagement with the Independent Commission for Reconciliation and Information Recovery.

To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he has engaged with his counterpart in the Department of Further and Higher Education, Research, Innovation and Science on proposed changes to the Central Applications Office points system, in order to ensure that changes are implemented to benefit applicants from Northern Ireland by September 2024.
answer_text: It is a priority for me to improve student mobility across Ireland.  Prior to his becoming Taoiseach, I had a positive discussion with Simon Harris TD in his then capacity as the responsible Minister on steps we can take to reduce barriers across the island.I have reached out to my counterpart, Minister Patrick O’Donovan TD, with a view to continuing that dialogue.  I intend to explore the matter raised by the member as part of that engagement.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what steps he is taking to overhaul environmental governance and enforcement following the findings of the BBC Spotlight programme broadcast on 4 June 2024.
answer_text: I aim to enhance environmental governance in Northern Ireland. I want to establish a regime that is fit for purpose, taking into account the new landscape for environmental oversight, including the Office for Environmental Protection's role and requirements in the Environment Act 2021 and Climate Change Act (NI) 2022. I have asked my officials to explore governance models that would provide greater independence and enforcement, and we are seeking views and opinions through a call for evidence.The Water (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 serves as the legislative framework in Northern Ireland, governing the protection of the aquatic environment. The Order also provides powers to take enforcement action against uncontrolled discharges which are a risk of pollution to the aquatic environment.A person guilty of an offence under Article 7 of this order is liable on conviction to imprisonment for a term not exceeding three months or to a fine not exceeding £20,000 or to both. My officials are also setting up a Lough Neagh Task Force following confirmation of the budget availability. The additional resource funding of £1.6m will fund in the region of 28 posts which will be split across the regulation and enforcement functions to undertake river inspections to identify sources of pollution, provide additional staff to take forward enforcement across a range of water quality issues alongside additional inspectors on the ground to complete cross compliance inspections.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department has engaged with Royal County Down Golf Club on the value for money of the Sports Sustainability Fund grant awarded during the pandemic.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Sport NI, an arm’s length body of my Department.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“Sport NI awarded £4,208,286 under the Sports Sustainability Fund to Golf Ireland, of which £1,562,452 was subsequently used by Golf Ireland to support Royal County Down Golf Club. Sport NI has not engaged with Royal County Down Golf Club on the value for money of the Sports Sustainability Fund grant awarded during the pandemic.The standard terms and conditions of award required the applicant (Golf Ireland) to provide data to enable Sport Northern Ireland to complete a Post Project Evaluation (PPE). A programme level PPE was carried out in March 2022. No PPE was carried out for individual awards to National Governing Bodies or to the individual clubs that then received funding from the National Governing Bodies.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact Sport NI directly.  Making direct contact with Sport NI will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.I have made arrangements for this response including a copy of the Sports Sustainability Fund Post Project Evaluation report to be placed in the

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of meetings he has had with representatives of Moy Park since taking up office in February 2024.
answer_text: I have met with Moy Park representatives on four occasions:Meeting with Retailers to discuss Portal Controls on 25 March 2024Engagement at Balmoral Show on 15 May 2024Visit to Moy Park on 22 May 2024Visit to Tully Biogas Plant, Stream BioEnergy on 22 May 2024
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the continued subsidy of non-existent long haul flights through the block grant adjustment to pay for zero-rated long haul air passenger duty.
answer_text: My officials are in the process of reviewing strategic routes and policy levers to support our air connectivity. This review will enable me to make a more informed assessment of the continued need for zero-rated long haul APD.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of (i) doctors; (ii) nurses; and (iii) allied health professionals that have left employment in Northern Ireland for jobs in the Republic of Ireland in each of the last two years.
answer_text: My Department does not have access to records of healthcare employees in other jurisdictions outside of Northern Ireland, and staff are not obliged to tell their employer of the details of any onward employment on leaving. Therefore it is not possible to provide an accurate response to this question.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when he will increase fines for repeated breaches of pollution rules, after it decreased in October 2022.
answer_text: I have instructed my officials to commence the process of reversing the changes made to the Cross-Compliance penalty regime in 2022.Guidance for farmers has been published for 2024 and applications for subsidy, using the current rules, have been submitted. Accordingly, these changes will take effect from the 2025 scheme year.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when Northern Ireland Civil Service staff will receive their agreed pay rise.
answer_text: The details of the 2023 pay awards for industrial and non-industrial staff were published online on 22 April 2024 here: 2023 Pay Award | Department of Finance (finance-ni.gov.uk). Those bulletins advise that, subject to satisfactory testing, the 2023 pay award for non-industrial staff will be paid this month. For industrial staff, payment was made in May for weekly paid staff and will be made in June for those industrial staff who are paid monthly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department has engaged with Belfast City Council with a view to re-establishing the Renewing the Routes Programme to support economic development in the city's neighbourhoods.
answer_text: Belfast City Council has not approached the Department for Communities regarding a further Renewing the Routes Programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how many meetings he has sought with the Home Secretary, or other UK government ministers, to discuss the effects of the electronic travel authorisation on tourism and the wider Northern Ireland economy.
answer_text: I am concerned about the impact of the introduction of the British Government’s Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) Scheme on our tourism sector. For that reason, I wrote to Tom Pursglove MP, the Minister for Legal Migration and the Border at the British Home Office on 26 April 2024 to seek an urgent meeting to discuss my concerns and to find solutions that would protect our tourism industry such as an exemption for tourists crossing the land border into the north. I copied this letter to Chris Heaton-Harris MP, the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland and Julia Lopez MP, the Minister of State at the Department for Culture, Media & Sport.I have had no response to my letter, and we are now into the pre-election period ahead of the British General Election. I intend to write again to the relevant British Home Office Minister when one is appointed after the election next month.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for an update on the designs for new canoe steps at Lagan Valley Park; and (ii) for an indicative timeframe for when the steps will be replaced.
answer_text: A topographical survey of the existing canoe steps at Shaw's Bridge, Belfast has been commissioned and design options are currently being developed. Once a suitable option is identified, it is anticipated that work will commence and be completed by Spring 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether there was any discussion of maximising the opportunities of dual market access for farmers and food producers at the North South Ministerial Council Agriculture meeting on 29 May 2024.
answer_text: This particular matter was not discussed during the meeting of the North South Ministerial Council in Agriculture Sectoral format on 29 May 2024. The joint communique from the meeting is available Joint Communiqué Agriculture.pdf (northsouthministerialcouncil.org).
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice when she will bring forward proposals to increase the age of criminal responsibility.
answer_text: I have been clear about my commitment to raising the minimum age of criminal responsibility (MACR) to bring Northern Ireland more into line with international standards, including directives from the UN Committee on the Rights of the Child.  Increasing MACR would reinforce the message that young children who offend need support, guidance and help, not criminalisation and punishment.  Evidence also clearly demonstrates that early formal contact with the justice system often results in poorer outcomes for individuals in the long-term, which of course not only impacts negatively on them but also on their victims, families and communities.  To change the law in this area, Executive support is required and previously has not been forthcoming. To further inform that discussion, my Department carried out a public consultation to seek views on increasing MACR from its current age of 10 years – the lowest in Europe.  This consultation took place between October and December 2022, with responses demonstrating overwhelming public support for an increase.  Over 83% of respondents agreed that the age should be increased beyond 10 years, with the majority supporting an increase to 14 years.  My officials have developed several options for progressing this issue, based on views expressed during the consultation, and I intend to share a paper with my Executive colleagues to seek their views on my proposals.  As any increase in MACR requires legislative change, it will not be possible for me to progress it in the absence of Executive approval.  In the meantime, we will continue to implement the successful policy to divert children of all ages – but in particular younger children – from the formal criminal justice system and deal with them through early intervention and community support.  This is in line with the actions set out in my Department’s Strategic Framework for Youth Justice.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when he will bring forward proposals to expand the Environmental Farming Scheme.
answer_text: The eighth tranche for higher level of the Environmental Farming Scheme has recently closed for applications.  These applications are currently being assessed and applicants will be notified by the end of June if they have been successful. Tranche 8 agreements will commence from 1st January 2025 and will last for five years. No further intake tranches to EFS are planned as it will be replaced by a successor scheme.Future Agri-environment support will be offered through Farming with Nature package, which is currently under development as part of my Department’s new Farm Support and Development Programme. The existing EFS Group projects will continue to provide advisory support to EFS agreement holders, and this will help to provide a smooth transition to Farming with Nature.The Farming with Nature Package will address the significant potential for farm businesses and land managers across all land types in Northern Ireland to make vital positive contributions to tackling environmental impacts and to be properly recognised by society for doing so.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on when a Programme for Government will be published.
To ask the Minister for the Economy when he will bring forward proposals to devolve the power to set minimum wage and replace it with the Real Living Wage.
answer_text: I am keen to maximise the uptake of the Real Living Wage here. To that end, my Department has been engaging with the Real Living Wage Foundation and I expect to announce the outcome of this engagement in due course.The Department has also commissioned research on the implications of setting a new minimum wage level(s), were the powers to be devolved. As a reserved matter, it would be necessary to seek the views of the Executive on any proposals.Should the Executive agree to seek the devolution of National Minimum Wage powers, it would be for the First and deputy First Ministers to formally seek the British Government’s agreement to make any necessary legislation under the Northern Ireland Act 1998.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether a Bill of Rights will be included in the Programme for Government.
answer_text: We are working at pace to develop a Programme for Government. The finalised Programme requires agreement from all Executive Ministers.		We are looking forward to sharing a draft with the Assembly in the near future.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department has engaged with Belfast City Council in relation to providing financial support for their Vacant to Vibrant Capital Grant Scheme.
answer_text: Belfast City Council did not approach the Department for Communities for financial support for their Vacant to Vibrant Capital Grant Scheme.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the number of occasions the construction of Casement Park has been discussed at an Executive meeting since the restoration of the institutions in February 2024.
answer_text: The issue of Casement has been raised a number of times at the Executive, including its inclusion in the PfG reflecting that it continues to be a flagship project.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when guidance for the NI Civil Service in relation to the pre-election period for the 2024 UK General Election will be published.
answer_text: Guidance on the conduct of civil servants and special advisers in the Northern Ireland Civil Service during the pre-election period was issued by the Head of the Civil Service on 23 May.  The guidance has also been published on the Executive website.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he will provide funding for a temporary variation in Rosetta Primary School Nursery Unit to meet demand.
answer_text: Temporary Flexibility (TF) is available to address a shortage of pre-school places for children in their final pre-school year (ie target age children) within an area, which cannot be met by any other pre-school provider as part of the Pre-School Education Programme.Based on the latest data provided by the Education Authority’s Pre-school Education Group (EA PEG), there are sufficient places available in alternative pre-school funded settings within 2 miles of Rosetta Primary School Nursery Unit for the 2024/25 academic year. On this basis, Temporary Flexibility is not required.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he has brought forward an Environmental Improvement Plan to the Executive.
answer_text: I am continuing in the process of seeking Executive approval for the draft Environment Strategy and its adoption as Northern Ireland’s first Environmental Improvement Plan (EIP). I have been engaging with Executive colleagues to address any remaining issues on the EIP and it is my intention to bring forward a further paper for consideration as soon as possible.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will increase fines for repeated negligent breaches following their reduction to 15 per cent of Basic Payment Scheme payments by his predecessor.
answer_text: Changes made to the Cross-Compliance penalty regime in 2022 removed the requirement to treat as intentional any breach of the same requirement within three calendar years of the previous penalty being capped at 15 percent.  The penalties themselves for individual breaches were not reduced. As set out in legislation, 15% is the maximum penalty which can be applied to any repeated negligent breach, with any resultant financial penalty being applied to all area-based scheme payments, not just the Basic Payment Scheme.I am currently considering the matter and will make a decision in due course.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether dual market access was specifically discussed at the North South Ministerial Council on Trade and Business Development meeting on 26 April 2024.
answer_text: Dual Market Access was not discussed at the NSMC Trade and Business Development meeting on 26th April 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of the feasibility of acquiring and transferring ownership of the bed and soil of Lough Neagh to public or community ownership.
answer_text: The scope of my officials’ current work is focussed on the blue green algae and water quality issues, not the Lough’s ownership. In terms of future ownership of the Lough Neagh bed and soil, I welcome the recent National Lottery Heritage Fund award with respect to the Lough Neagh Heritage Resilience Project, led by the Lough Neagh Partnership. This will enable an investigation into the feasibility of acquiring and transferring ownership of the bed and soil to public and / or community ownership. I await the outcome of this work.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when he will publish a Nutrients Action Plan.
answer_text: The Nutrients Action Programme (NAP) is implemented through The Nutrient Action Programme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2019.  It came into operation on 8 April 2019 and remains in force.A review of the current NAP is underway. The review and a public consultation on proposed revisions to the NAP will be published in the coming months. It is envisaged that revised NAP Regulations could be introduced in early 2025.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when he will publish (i) the Environmental Improvement Plan; and (ii) a review of the Nutrients Action Programme.
answer_text: I refer the Member to the answer I gave to AQW 10962/22-27.A review of the current Nutrients Action Programme is underway.  This review will be published along with a consultation on proposals for the next Nutrients Action Programme after the summer.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the Health and Social Care Trust policy in relation to transgender patients and same sex wards; and (ii) whether he plans to review this policy, following the recent announcement from the UK Secretary of State for Health and Social Care.
answer_text: My Department is aware of the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC), England’s consultation on its NHS Constitution, which ends on 25 June 2024.  DHSC will use the feedback from the consultation to work out how best to reflect proposed changes in the Constitution. My Department will consider any potential implications it may have in respect of the provision of gender identity services in Northern Ireland, including any changes required regarding same sex wards.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will remove Ulster Defence Association flags illegally erected on departmental property outside Bests Hill, Newtownbreda.
answer_text: The display of a flag of a proscribed terrorist organisation is a criminal offence that may be capable of investigation by the PSNI, and I would encourage anyone with information in regards the display of such items to contact the PSNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for her assessment of whether it would be more appropriate for the Department of Justice to have responsibility for civil law rather than her Department.
answer_text: All Departments have civil law functions. My Department is responsible for law reform in certain areas of civil law, including marriage and civil partnership, private family law, the law of tort, and land law. Many of these areas are linked to other Departmental functions. I consider it is appropriate for my Department to retain responsibility in these areas.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the current funding situation for the Lagan pedestrian and cycle bridge.
answer_text: The £24.5m Lagan and Pedestrian Cycle Bridge is partly funded through the Belfast Region City Deal, with £12.1m being secured through this deal.Having decided on indicative budget allocations (subject to a consultation on the equality impacts) my officials can now consider the detailed programmes of work across the Department’s areas of responsibility, including the additional funding required for the Lagan and Pedestrian Cycle Bridge.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of whether it would be more appropriate for her Department to have responsibility for civil law rather than the Department of Finance.
answer_text: At present, my assessment is that the current allocation of responsibilities is generally appropriate, and any possible transfer of functions would not be a priority for Executive or Assembly consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the measures being implemented to reduce speeding on the A24 Saintfield Road, aimed at improving road safety and ensuring compliance with the speed limit of the road.
answer_text: I can advise that enforcement of speed limits is the responsibility of the PSNI. Incidents of speeding or inappropriate driver behaviour along the Saintfield Road should be reported directly to the PSNI.While the Department has no current plans to carry out changes to the Saintfield Road, a developer-led scheme at the junction with the Knockbracken Healthcare Centre and Knockbracken Road is due to commence.The road also has an Average Speed Camera system in place between the Carryduff Roundabout and Primrose Hill, which is the responsibility of the Northern Ireland Road Safety Partnership.  One of the best ways to ensure safety on our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050; is by changing road user behaviour.  As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe.  The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) to detail his Department's plans to address and complete the repairing of potholes and other related road defects on the A24 Saintfield Road; and (ii) when this work will be completed.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to austerity by the British Government which has meant that it has had to introduce a limited service maintenance policy, meaning that only the highest priority road defects are repaired. My officials inspect the Saintfield Road on a regular basis and prioritise the repairs of potholes and other road defects in accordance with this policy.My Department is finalising its funding allocations, following confirmation of the 2024/25 budget settlement. Details of the planned works programmes will then be completed and included within the Department’s Annual Report to the local councils. The value of our infrastructure cannot be overstated, and I will continue to work with Executive colleagues to ensure appropriate investment is made.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the actions his Department is taking to end sewage pollution.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Environment Agency (NIEA) regulates discharges to the water environment under the Water Order (as amended) 1999. A regulatory regime is in place, through the issue of Water Order Discharge Consents. These Consents require dischargers to treat sewage to a level that can be sustained by the receiving environment and to maintain their treatments to avoid pollution.Discharge consents are issued to single dwellings, private sewage infrastructure serving residential / commercial properties, and to NI Water. NIEA uses a risk-based approach to monitor these consents and where non-compliances are detected, NIEA will take action in line with the Department’s enforcement policy.My Department will continue to conduct targeted studies to identify the sources at failing sites and will work with others to develop appropriate measures for implementation to improve water quality.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she will revisit the decision taken in relation to the pay and grading review for school support staff.
answer_text: The Executive agreed its Budget for 2024-25 on 25 April 2024, and in agreeing this, it also agreed that I would write to the Treasury to seek approval to reprofile some of the funding for future years provided in the financial package to enable implementation of the pay and grading review for this vital group of staff.Following a formal request, and confirmation of the cost, from the Education Minister, I wrote to the Chief Secretary to the Treasury on 17 May 2024 to formally request this.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail her discussions with the Minister of Education in relation to ensuring funding for the pay and grading review for school support staff.
answer_text: I have engaged with the Education Minister regarding the funding to implement the outcome of the pay and grading review of non-teaching support staff, both in correspondence, and when we met to discuss the Executive’s Budget for 2024-25.Recognising the importance of these workers, some of whom are amongst the lowest paid in the sector and doing vital work to support our children and young people, the Executive agreed to seek approval from Treasury to reprofile some of the funding provided for future years as part of the financial package, for this purpose.On 10 May 2024, the Education Minister confirmed the amount of funding required to be reprofiled.  Following my, and my officials’ engagement with the Treasury during the week, I wrote to the Chief Secretary to the Treasury on 17 May 2024 to formally request the reprofile.  I also confirmed this to the Education Minister on the same date.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether any company in receipt of Invest NI funding has (i) manufactured (a) munitions; and (b) a component part of a munition; and (ii) provided any support service, for the State of Israel.
answer_text: Invest NI is also not aware of companies in receipt of funding that manufacture either munitions or a component part of a munition or are providing any support service for the State of Israel.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail his discussions with the Minister of Finance in relation to ensuring funding for the pay and grading review for school support staff.
answer_text: I have engaged with the Minister of Finance regarding the funding to implement the Pay and Grading Review, both in correspondence, and when we met to discuss the Executive’s budget for 2024-25.Recognising the importance of these workers, some of whom are amongst the lowest paid in the sector and doing vital work to support our children and young people, the Executive agreed to seek approval from HM Treasury to reprofile some of the funding provided for future years as part of the financial package, for this purpose.On 10 May 2024, I confirmed with the Minister of Finance the amount of funding required to be reprofiled.  I have since received confirmation that the Minister of Finance and her officials’ have engaged with the HM Treasury and have written to the Chief Secretary on 17 May 2024 to formally request the reprofile.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the community arts projects in South Belfast that have received funding in each of the last five years.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Arts Council of Northern Ireland.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:	“For the purposes of the response, we have provided details of all Arts Council awards to arts organisations in Belfast South.  We have done so on the premise that all Arts Council awards will have public benefit for the whole community. A summary of the award number and value is below for both Exchequer funded and National Lottery funded awards:Finance YearExchequerLotteryNo ValueNoValue2023/2424£3,747,19854£1,222,6742022/2331£3,627,62361£1,621,6202021/2223£826,49635£1,241,2922020/21149£11,032,83538£1,069,5492019/2047£487,36055£1,839,783TOTALS274£19,721,512243£6,994,918Details of both Exchequer funded, and National Lottery funded awards are attached.”Due to the volume of information provided, I have arranged for this information requested to be placed in the Assembly Library.This issue is also an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“Please see the table below, which details the funding provided by the Housing Executive to community arts projects in South Belfast, in each of the last five years:2023/24    Aug-23Shaftesbury Community Recreation Centre LORAGFestival of the River 2023£9,590.00 Sep-23East Belfast EnterpriseConnecting Communities Through Art - Family Fun Day£4,210.00 Oct-23An DroicheadFéile na Samhna 2023 (Halloween Festival)£3,510.00    £17,310.00 2022/23    Aug-22LORAGFestival on the River£4,836.93 Aug-22South Belfast AlternativesSouth and East Belfast Cultural Festival£4,060.00 Aug-22Belfast Asian Women's AcademyBelfast Diversity Day£1,360.00 Sep-22Apex Housing AssociationMini Mela Dunmurry£1,345.00 Nov-22East Belfast EnterpriseConnecting Communities Through Art£10,000.00Re-Imaging Nov-22Belfast City Mission (Island Street)Half-Term Fun & Friendship£1,179.50    £22,781.43 2021/22    Sep-21AlternativesSouth & East Belfast Cultural Festival£1,985.00 Nov-21Lagan Village Youth and Community GroupMe and My art£200.00 Feb-22Belfast City Mission (Island Street)February Fun & Friendship£1,935.99    £4,120.99 2020/21    Feb-21Cluan Place Replacement of ArtworkCluan Place Replacement of Artwork£2,400.00    £2,400.00 2019/20    Jul-19Ballymac Friendship TrustChalk Art Extravaganza£4,570.00 Feb-20ACT Initiative (South Belfast)Looking at the Pass£4,950.00Re-Imaging    £9,520.00   Total: £56,132.42” For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Arts Council and the Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with the Arts Council and the Housing Executive will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, further to her comments in the Assembly Chamber on Tuesday 14 May 2024, whether the operational matters covered in the Intergovernmental Agreement on Cooperation on Criminal Justice Matters are the responsibility of her Department.
answer_text: The Intergovernmental Agreement on Cooperation on Criminal Justice Matters (IGA) was established on 26 July 2005. It allows for the Ministers of the Northern Ireland Executive and the Government of Ireland responsible for criminal justice matters to meet at least annually to facilitate effective co-operation and co-ordination on criminal justice matters. It is not an accountability mechanism; rather, it allows for enhanced cooperation in areas of mutual interest. The Joint Agency Task Force was established under the Fresh Start Agreement in 2015. It is led by senior officers from the Police Service of Northern Ireland, An Garda Síochána, the Revenue Commissioners and His Majesty’s Revenue and Customs. Its objective is to work collectively to tackle cross-jurisdictional organised crime within existing operational frameworks. As per the terms of the Fresh Start Agreement, the Joint Agency Task Force is required to provide a six-monthly report on its work to the Ministerial meetings under the IGA. Article 1 (2) of the Intergovernmental Agreement places an obligation on Ministers to provide periodic reports on the Ministerial meetings on Criminal Justice Cooperation to their respective Ministerial colleagues in the Northern Ireland Executive and the Government of Ireland.  Whilst the Department of Justice is not operationally responsible for the Joint Agency Task Force, I see value in reporting to my Ministerial colleagues the positive

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail who is responsible for trees planted on unadopted land.
answer_text: Trees planted on unadopted land are the responsibility of the relevant landowner who is responsible for ensuring that such trees and hedges do not endanger or obstruct road users.  My Department undertakes regular inspections across the road network. Where these inspections identify any roadside trees of concern, owners / occupiers are identified and are requested to co-operate in removing or cutting the trees or hedges to address any issues in relation to public safety.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department continues to fund a Belfast City Council Intervention Officer for the Holyland and Lower Ormeau area.
answer_text: My Department’s contribution to funding for Belfast City Council’s Intervention manager for the wider University & lower Ormeau area ended on 31 March 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for a timeline of plans to transfer the land owned by his Department at Newforge for the development of the sports complex.
answer_text: Discussions have taken place between DAERA officials and representatives from the Newforge Community Development Trust about its interest in DAERA owned land at Newforge Lane. The future need for these lands is currently being considered as part of a Strategic Review of the Agri-Food and Biosciences Institute’s land and property holdings. The Review is ongoing and any decision making around the potential disposal of DAERA lands, emanating from the Review, will follow due process.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the date of the most recent meeting of the Holyland and Lower Ormeau Strategic Partnership Group; and (ii) when the group will next meet.
answer_text: The Strategic Partnership Group last met on 26 October 2022.  The Group will meet again if required to consider any strategic issues escalated from the Wider University and Lower Ormeau Inter-Agency group.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when she will introduce legislation to put the Fiscal Commission on a statutory footing.
answer_text: I have written to Executive Colleagues with a draft Executive Paper and policy document detailing the content of a Fiscal Council Bill that will place the Council on a statutory footing. Prior to the restoration of the Executive, my Department working with the Office of the Legislative Counsel, had undertaken significant preparatory work in relation to the drafting of this Bill. Therefore, it is my intention, subject to Executive agreement, that the formal drafting of the Fiscal Council Bill can be quickly completed, and this important legislation progressed in the Assembly.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, further to her comments in the Assembly Chamber on Tuesday 14 May 2024, whether she is committed to introducing a stand-alone hate crime bill.
answer_text: The loss of two years of Assembly business time and constraints on resources has resulted in the delivery of a more limited Legislative Programme than had been planned in 2022. In this context, there may be insufficient time and resources to accommodate the introduction of a stand-alone Hate Crime Bill. I remain committed to bringing forward foundational hate crime provisions as part of the Department’s legislative programme for this mandate and I am working through the detail of how that might be best achieved. There are a number of legislative vehicles available to achieve change in this regard, including through the proposed Sentencing Bill and proposed Victims Bill. I am committed to ensuring new hate crime legislation will be in in place by the end of the mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she has asked her officials to examine whether criminal justice policy changes or law reform can assist in tackling paramilitarism.
answer_text: A strong, effective criminal justice system is an essential component of wider efforts to address paramilitarism and organised crime. In addition to work carried out by my Department on criminal justice policy, the Executive Programme on Tackling Paramilitarism and Organised Crime (‘The Programme’) uses an evidence-based, public health approach to tackling paramilitarism. This includes: reviewing and evaluating practice across over 100 Programme interventions; looking to other jurisdictions to draw upon policy and reform initiatives, and; supporting my Department’s core business by sharing learning and providing evidence of need to support policy development. Since 2016, the Programme has been developing evidence of what works to address paramilitary harm and has also provided financial investment to support specific criminal justice initiatives and reforms. Delay has been a longstanding issue.  In January 2023, the Criminal Justice Board (CJB) prioritised a new programme of work targeted at reducing avoidable delay in the system. This programme takes a holistic view of the system, attempting to reduce demand and free capacity to enable a proportionate response to offending behaviours.  The Programme previously provided funding to the Public Prosecution Service to support committal reform, has invested in restorative practice and justice initiatives to improve outcomes for offenders and victims, and has also supported wider policy efforts focused on rehabilitation and management of terrorist offenders.  The Programme has also shared learning and insights from projects in relation to ongoing policy work on hate crime and new organised crime offences. More generally, the Programme has been the sole funder of the Safeguarding Board’s Trauma Informed Practice Project. This has resulted in enhanced understanding of trauma across the criminal justice system, how it links to offending behaviour and how it can be addressed effectively. Finally, the Programme is the sole funder of the PCTF, a tri-agency body set up in 2016 to tackle criminality linked to paramilitary groups. An example of the benefits of this approach was noted earlier this month when the NCA (one of the PCTF agencies) used an Unexplained Wealth Order (UWO), the first of its kind in NI. We will continue to look for opportunities to enhance the criminal justice response to paramilitarism and organised crime, drawing on evidence and best practice.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he has any plans to improve (i) signage; and (ii) access at Belfast City Airport for those seeking to use Sydenham train station.
answer_text: In August 2023 Translink erected new signage directing passengers on the platform at Sydenham towards the George Best Belfast City Airport and there are ongoing discussions between Translink and the airport around the erection of a departures screen displaying bus and rail times.  In addition, signage has been improved on bus shelters to further encourage the use of public transport.  My officials will continue to liaise with colleagues in Active Travel regarding the Sydenham to Holywood Walking, Wheeling & Cycling Route and with Translink in relation to their Belfast City Airport Connectivity Project.My Department is committed to doing all that it can to ensure all public services are inclusive and accessible, especially for those with disabilities and young families. My officials have recently undertaken a study on the feasibility of providing DDA compliant ramps to facilitate improved access to/from Sydenham Halt, which has shown this would require complex changes to the bridge that would take time to develop and would also require significant capital investment.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any (i) meetings she has had; and (ii) briefings she has received in relation to the activities of Intertrade UK.
answer_text: I attended the East-West Council meeting on 26 March 2024. InterTrade UK was on the agenda and I received written briefing from my officials prior to me attending. Neither I, nor the interim Minister for the Economy has had any other meetings or briefings in relation to the activities of Intertrade UK.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she, or her predecessor, (i) has requested any official briefings; and (ii) held any meetings in relation to consumer financial services in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Neither my predecessor nor I have requested any official briefings specifically on consumer financial services; however, I have had numerous engagements with representatives from a consumer financial services background. Details of my ministerial meetings are published quarterly on the OpenDataNI portal.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she has (i) requested any official briefings; and (ii) held any meetings in relation to consumer financial services in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I have held a number of meetings to discuss this matter, with further engagements planned.I met with the Financial Services Union, alongside the then Economy Minister, Conor Murphy MLA, on 17 April 2024 to discuss the impact of bank closures on communities and staff as well as issues around access to services. Subsequently, I held a meeting on 1 May 2024 with senior representatives of the local banks and building societies, LINK and Cash Access UK, where I urged them to protect the local branch network and ensure communities are able to access banking services.It is also my intention to convene a roundtable discussion in the Autumn bringing together a wide range of stakeholder representatives to discuss a variety of financial service matters. My officials have regular discussions with Treasury and industry bodies on these same issues. Recently, they have been engaging with the Treasury and relevant regulatory and industry bodies representing the insurance industry, regarding recent price increases in insurance premiums in the North, to understand what actions are, and can be taken to protect consumers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice (i) whether her Department has any plans to review the rehabilitation period for those with a conviction; and (ii) when these convictions will be considered spent.
answer_text: I commissioned a review of rehabilitation periods in Northern Ireland during the last mandate. A public consultation exercise was launched on 8 January 2021 and responses to the consultation were very supportive of proposals to amend the Rehabilitation of Offenders (Northern Ireland) Order 1978 (“the 1978 Order”) to both reduce existing rehabilitation periods and allow more convictions to become spent.A report on the outcome of the consultation exercise was published on 27 October 2021, which can be viewed here: Consultation report - Proposals for reform of rehabilitation periods in Northern IrelandUnfortunately, it wasn’t possible to complete the legislative process to deliver these changes before the end of the last mandate. I intend to introduce legislative provisions to the Assembly as part of the Modernisation of Justice Bill to effect the changes to the 1978 Order that I wish to deliver.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the timeline for the installation of a pedestrian crossing on Knockbreda Road, Belfast, to facilitate Belfast City Council opening the pedestrian gate into Cherryvale Playing Fields.
answer_text: I can advise that the Department recently wrote to Belfast City Council asking them to carry out further essential consultation with the residents most effected by the alternative crossing proposal, due to the effect on parking directly outside their houses. A response has been received and officials are currently considering this and the best way forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 11309/22-27, whether the decision to not proceed with previously agreed allocations to integrated schools, and to spend the non ring-fenced £150 million elsewhere, was agreed in the Executive.
answer_text: Fresh Start funding for both integrated and shared capital projects, including the Strule Shared Education Campus, was removed by the UK Government.  There was no consultation with my department.All Fresh Start projects not in contract, except the Strule Shared Education Campus, were placed in my Department’s major capital works programme to continue to advance in planning and design.  Like other new build school projects, these former Fresh Start projects will be funded from the education capital budget.Given previous Executive commitments, I requested Executive support and funding to allow the flagship Strule Shared Education Campus to continue.  Given its scale, this important strategic programme is not and would never be affordable solely from my Department’s capital budget.  Without ring-fenced Executive funding, the Strule Programme, which is central to the future development of Shared Education in Northern Ireland, would have ceased.I have, therefore sought to implement my statutory duties to both shared and integrated education.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the proposed policy of securing United States of America immigration pre-clearance services at Belfast International Airport.
answer_text: As civil aviation is a reserved matter, my officials have explored with the Department for Transport (DfT) the possibility of extending pre-clearance services to the North of Ireland. They advised that the decision on whether to operate such facilities at airports overseas ultimately rests with the US authorities.The British government has committed to entering into exploratory talks with the United States Administration on the options for introducing pre-clearance checks on customs and immigration for passengers travelling on direct flights from Belfast International Airport to destinations in the USA.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail her Department's estimates in relation to the unallocated funding to be released at June monitoring, including, but not limited to, (i) funds released from the establishment of the Public Service Transformation Board; (ii) funds released from increased Barnett consequentials from the 2024 Spring Budget; and (iii) additional underspends.
answer_text: The funding available for allocation in the June Monitoring round has yet to be confirmed. The Treasury has not yet confirmed the Barnett consequentials for use by the Executive resultant from additional in-year funding for Whitehall departments, though this is anticipated shortly. i) There is £47 million available from the repurposed funds in the financial package for transformation projects this financial year. This funding is dependent on the establishment of a Public Sector Transformation Board, which is subject to discussion with the British Government. Release of the funding will also be subject to the Board considering proposed projects are transformational. The Executive agreed my proposed approach to taking this work forward on 9 May and officials are working at pace on this. ii) The Spring Budget provided £99 million of additional Barnett consequentials which were allocated in Budget 2024-25. These did not reflect the needs-based factor of 24%. The application of this is subject to negotiation with the Treasury, which I hope will be concluded in time to allow the additional funding to be reflected in June Monitoring. iii) As the additional funding package for 2023-24 was provided through a Reserve claim, there will be no access to carry forward under the Budget Exchange Scheme for Resource DEL. However, since this does not apply to Capital DEL, any capital underspends, within Budget Exchange Limits, will be available in 2024-25. The initial amount, which will be allocated in June Monitoring, will be based on departmental Provisional Outturn returns. The position will be updated when Final Outturn is confirmed later in the year. I have also negotiated with Treasury that Barnett Consequential of £41.5 million Resource DEL and £1.6 million Capital DEL notified late in 2023-24 can be carried forward to 2024-25. This funding will also be available for allocation in June Monitoring.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, further to the Office of National Statistics figures published in April 2024 which state that average rents have increased by 10.1 per cent in the last year, to outline the action he is taking to support private renters.
answer_text: I am acutely aware of the sharp increases in rents in the private rented sector since the pandemic. Research carried out by my Department concluded that the best ways of relieving pressure on affordability for renters was by increasing housing supply, and ensuring the benefit system properly takes account of the cost of housing.Therefore, the increase in Local Housing Allowance rates, which set the level of housing support that households receive through Housing Benefit and Universal Credit, from last month will go some way in helping renters pay their rent and relieve pressure on their household budgets.  Last year, my Department paid out over £325m to cover housing costs in the private rented sector, this will increase this year with the LHA uplift.   Some of these rates have increased by over 40 percent, which is a stark illustration of the scale of the impact that the LHA freeze had on households in the private rented sector. I will be advocating for these rates to keep track of the market rents, so this does not prove to be a very temporary fix for our lower income households.In addition, those households who are still experiencing a shortfall in their housing support against their rental costs after the uplift in LHA, may be able to access Discretionary Housing Payments. As I have already stated in this chamber, one of my key priorities is to increase the supply of safe, warm, and affordable homes. To help achieve this, I will continue to spend most of my Department’s capital budget on building new social homes and exploit new funding sources to support the delivery of affordable housing. A quarter of a million people live in social homes, with highly subsidised rent.  My Department will help about 800 households into home ownership this year, around half of whom we expect to live in the private rented sector. Last week I made an announcement to the Assembly on the development of a new intermediate rent product, another way we will help private renters.  I am also working to finalise a Housing Supply Strategy, aimed at unlocking supply across all tenures, with the intention of bringing it to the Executive for consideration as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department is working on an intervention to financially support hospitality businesses.
answer_text: I understand the importance of the hospitality sector for both our tourism industry and the high street economy, with approximately 57,000 jobs in the accommodation and food service sector. Addressing the challenges of rising costs cuts across several areas of government, including the Department for Communities and local councils.  My Department is taking steps to support businesses through delivery of the Executive’s ‘Path to Net Zero Energy’ Strategy, a key objective being to ensure that all consumers have access to essential and affordable energy. Tourism NI provides a suite of advisory supports to help tourism and hospitality businesses reduce costs, improve productivity and be more sustainable, including Leaner and Greener, and Innovate Tourism.The level of VAT is also a specific issue, and I know that the Department of Finance has repeatedly raised with the Treasury the negative impacts on hospitality of high levels of VAT.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department is progressing plans for a Green New Deal for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I am committed to delivering on our obligation to meet the net zero target by 2050 as set out in the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.To help achieve this I do plan to bring forward a Green Growth Strategy which will set out our long-term strategy to tackle the climate challenge by balancing climate action with the environment and the economy in a way that benefits all our people.The aim of the Green Growth Strategy is to ensure future government policy making here has climate and environmental action at its core, embracing and enabling science and innovation to drive solutions.A follow-on action I would be keen to explore with Executive Ministers following adoption of the Green Growth Strategy would be the potential to make the case for a Green New Deal for Northern Ireland. This of course would be cross cutting and would require an agreed position.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department plans to introduce carbon audits for farms.
answer_text: My Department plans to introduce carbon audits for farms through the Carbon Footprinting Project that will commence in 2024. This Project will enable farm businesses to gain an understanding of their greenhouse gas emissions and use this information to identify where improvements can be made and will be an important management tool to help farm businesses drive better environmental performance and sustainable practices.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they raised human rights concerns during their meeting with Chinese Ambassador Zheng Zeguang in May 2024.
answer_text: We met with Ambassador Zheng on 1st May 2024 to discuss our bilateral relationship with China and how we can continue to develop mutually beneficial relationships in those areas that are the responsibility of the Executive.  The issue of human rights was not raised on this occassion.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether any of his officials are still working in pursuit of the targets or priorities outlined in the Going for Growth strategy or associated action plan.
answer_text: Any DAERA schemes that address the 2014 Going for Growth recommendations were delivered largely as part of the NI Rural Development Programme 2014-2020, which is now drawing to a close, and included a package of capital and non-capital measures in the Farm Business Improvement Scheme and the Environmental Farming Scheme.  My focus in the years ahead is to ensure my Department's policies and programmes deliver sustainable growth for our agri-food and farming sector and do so in a way that will reduce emissions, improve our natural and marine environment and ensure food security and high standards of disease control. Old policies are being replaced by newer, evidence-based approaches including, as I announced in the Assembly on 14 May 2024, a new Farm Support and Development Programme which will transition us to a more sustainable farming sector and, a new environmental improvement plan which I hope to bring shortly to the Assembly following Executive approval.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of resident students from the Republic of Ireland entering Northern Ireland institutions for undergraduate study in the last five academic years.
answer_text: The number of Republic of Ireland domiciled students enrolled in undergraduate courses in North of Ireland institutions for each of the five academic years 2017/18 through to 2021/22 are shown in the table below. Data for Higher Education Institutions is not yet available for 2022/23. However, the number of Republic of Ireland domiciled students enrolled in Further Education Colleges for undergraduate study is available and provided in the table below.Academic Year2017/182018/192019/202020/212021/222022/23Further Education Colleges315285245195195195Universities1,2151,2451,1001,1701,420Not Yet Available
