question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will announce the next steps on the Reshaping Stroke Care consultation, which closed in August 2019.
answer_text: My Department received in excess of 19,000 responses to the ‘Reshaping Stroke Care Consultation’. In the time since the consultation closed, I have been giving careful consideration to the consultation analysis, the evidence base for reform and emerging evidence on stroke treatments. Having done so, I can advise that it is my intention to set out the way forward for the reform of stroke care in the coming weeks and that the Reshaping Stroke Care consultation analysis will be published alongside this.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether she will sign off on the National Joint Council (NJC) pay rise which was agreed on the 28 February 2022, but which has not been implemented by the Housing Executive.
answer_text: Housing Executive 2021/22 Pay Remits have been approved

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 31136/17-22, when the first annual publication of licence data, as per the Licensing and Registration of Clubs (Amendment) Act (NI) 2021, will be released.
answer_text: The first annual publication of licence data, as required under section 24 of the Licensing and Registration of Clubs (Amendment) Act 2021, will be available on 30 June 2022 at 9.30am at https://www.communities-ni.gov.uk/topics/dfc-statistics-and-research.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 31104/17-22, when will the independent review of the licensing system, including the Surrender Principle, commence, as provided in the Licensing and Registration of Clubs (Amendment) Act (Northern Ireland) 2021.
answer_text: The tender opportunity for the independent review of the licensing system including the surrender principle was published on 20 May 2022. The Department is required, under section 23 of the Licensing and Registration of Clubs (Amendment) Act 2021, to appoint an independent person by 26 August 2022.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on phase 2 delivery of the Health and Social Care Staff COVID recognition payment.
answer_text: A letter was issued on 28 January 2022 in relation to Phase 2 covering claims for former employees who had left but worked for the provider during the qualifying period. The letter included full details of eligibility criteria and how the employer should make a claim on behalf of their former staff.Phase 2 had been temporarily paused to allow officials to seek clarity on a number of issues raised relating to the impact of the increase in national insurance contributions which are included as part of the payment provided to the former employer.  A further letter was issued to employers on 14 April 2022 providing clarity on the issues raised and confirming recommencement of the payment process. Claims are being processed by BSO in date order when all relevant information is provided by the claimants. No date has been set for the closure of the scheme and claims will continue to be processed by the Business Services Organisation during 2022/23 therefore I am not in a position to provide a date as to when all payments will be made.I can confirm that as at 11 May 2022, a total of over £16.6m has been paid out under Phase 1 to independent sector care home and domiciliary care providers covering 28,314 individuals. As at the same date £2.1m has been paid out to 5,027 individuals under Phase 2; these payments include staff previously employed in independent sector care home and domiciliary care providers who have since moved employer or left the sector, and staff employed with eligible day opportunity providers and buildings-based day services.In addition we have also opened the Scheme to eligible applicants in the following groups, Personal Assistants employed by Independent Living Fund recipients, Thalidomide Health Grant recipients, and Personal Assistants employed by those in receipt of Direct Payments from HSC Trusts. This element of the scheme was launched on 23 March and is being delivered by ILF Scotland for the payments, Scotland Excel who set up and run the online portal, and SDS Scotland who manage the dedicated helpline. These delivery partners were heavily involved in delivering the equivalent scheme for the Scottish Government.  To date £300k has been paid to 863 individuals. To encourage uptake on this element Trusts have now written to all individuals in receipt of direct payments. My officials are also finalising arrangements to include eligible staff whose employers are no longer trading and I hope to be in a position to include this cohort of individuals in the coming weeks.A FAQ on the Special Recognition Payment has been published on the Department’s website as below:-https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/news/hsc-staff-recognition-payment-faqsThis continues to be updated with relevant information as each phase of the claims process progresses. I remain personally committed to ensuring the recognition payment will be paid to independent sector staff as soon as it is practicable.
